concepts.name: fine-tuning based representation model
concepts.description: 基于预训练模型的表示模型，通过在通用语言数据上预训练后，再针对特定任务或领域进行进一步训练（微调）以适配下游需求。该方法复用既有的表示能力，通常无需为每个任务重新设计专门架构，并在特定基准上表现良好，但其泛化能力可能会受微调数据分布的限制。
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept describes fine-tuning a pretrained model on downstream tasks, while the candidate is a feature-based approach that uses pretrained representations as fixed features without full parameter updating.

concepts.name: document encoder
concepts.description: A model that converts an entire document into a representation suitable for prediction or classification. Document encoders can be pre-trained on unlabeled text so that they transfer better to downstream tasks.
concepts.match_reason: A document encoder represents an entire document, while a sentence encoder represents a sentence; they are related but not the same concept.

concepts.name: Transformer 块
concepts.description: Transformer 块是 Transformer 模型中的可重复处理单元，通过注意力机制与前馈网络对隐藏表示进行变换。将多个这样的块堆叠起来即可形成完整的编码器或解码器网络，从而逐步构建更高层次的上下文表示。
concepts.match_reason: “Transformer 块”是 Transformer 中的单个基础堆叠单元；候选项 0 是由多个块组成的编码器，1 是整个 Transformer 架构，都不是同一概念。

concepts.name: 层数
concepts.description: 神经网络中按顺序堆叠的层的数量，用于衡量模型深度。层数越多，模型通常能通过更多级非线性变换构造更复杂的表示；同时计算成本和参数预算往往随之增加，除非相应调整其他网络维度（如宽度或注意力头数）。

concepts.name: F1 score
concepts.description: An evaluation metric that combines precision and recall into a single harmonic mean. It is commonly used for tasks where both false positives and false negatives matter.

concepts.name: data augmentation
concepts.description: Data augmentation is the practice of expanding training data by adding additional examples or related datasets to improve generalization. In question answering, it can include pretraining or intermediate training on another labeled dataset before the final task.

concepts.name: 自回归语言模型
concepts.description: 一种按照从左到右的顺序预测下一个标记的语言模型。它根据前文上下文估计当前目标标记的条件概率，并以此生成序列。
concepts.match_reason: “自回归语言模型”是按从左到右逐步预测下一个标记的模型；候选项中没有完全对应的同义概念，#1 是训练目标而非模型，#2 更宽泛且不限定自回归，其他也只是泛化概念。

concepts.name: key-value pair
concepts.description: A paired storage unit consisting of a context-derived key and its associated target word as the value. Such pairs allow retrieval systems to map similar contexts to likely next words.

concepts.name: n-gram language model
concepts.description: n-gram 语言模型是一种固定上下文长度的统计语言模型，只根据前面 n 个词（或标记）的历史来预测下一个词（或标记）的概率。它通过建模局部的 n-gram 共现规律来进行语言建模，常见形式包括二元语法模型（n=2）和三元语法模型（n=3）。
concepts.match_reason: An n-gram language model uses a fixed-size local history, while a left-to-right language model is a broader autoregressive setup that can use the full preceding context.

concepts.name: context length
concepts.description: Context length is the maximum number of tokens (or symbols, depending on the model) that can be included in a single input sequence the model can use for generation. Providing a longer context allows more surrounding information to be considered at once, but it may also make it harder to reliably focus on the most relevant parts.
concepts.match_reason: "Context length" is the amount of prior context a model can use, while "left context" is the actual preceding part of a sequence; related but not the same concept.

concepts.name: world knowledge
concepts.description: General factual knowledge about entities, facts, and relationships in the world. In language models, it may be stored implicitly in learned parameters or accessed explicitly through retrieval.

concepts.name: stochastic gradient descent
concepts.description: An iterative optimization method that updates model parameters using noisy gradient estimates computed from training examples or mini-batches. It is widely used to maximize likelihood objectives in neural network training.
concepts.match_reason: Stochastic gradient descent is an optimization algorithm; gradient noise scale is a measure of gradient stochasticity, not the same concept.

concepts.name: MIPS index
concepts.description: An index built for maximum inner product search over document representations. It supports retrieving documents with the largest inner products with a query representation, making it useful for fast top-k document selection in retrieval systems.

concepts.name: Google Suggest API
concepts.description: An API that provides query suggestions related to a given search input. It can be used to expand an initial question into a larger set of related questions.

concepts.name: sparse vector
concepts.description: A sparse vector representation contains mostly zero entries and only a small number of nonzero (active) features. It is commonly used to encode term presence or weighted term statistics for information retrieval, where each dimension corresponds to a vocabulary item. Because only a few features are active, matching between queries and documents can be efficient and the resulting features remain interpretable.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a sparse vector representation, while candidate [1] is a retrieval method that uses sparse vectors; representation vs method are not the same concept.

concepts.name: extractive QA
concepts.description: Extractive QA is a question answering setting in which the answer is restricted to a span taken from one or more passages. The system locates the relevant evidence in the provided or retrieved text and returns the corresponding substring, rather than generating a free-form response.
concepts.match_reason: Extractive QA is the answer format/setting where the response must be a span from the source text, while the candidate is a broader end-to-end QA system architecture.

concepts.name: text passage
concepts.description: A contiguous span of text used as a retrieval unit in question answering and information retrieval. Passages are typically smaller than full documents so that a model can match questions to more precise evidence, and the text is encoded and indexed to retrieve relevant passages for a query.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is the unit of text being indexed, while the candidate is the retrieval process that selects such passages; they are related but not the same.

concepts.name: 词向量
concepts.description: 词向量是一种将词映射到低维连续向量空间的表示方法，通过稠密可学习的向量刻画词语的语义与用法。语义或上下文/句法相近的词通常对应相似的向量，便于神经网络建模词与词之间的相似性与组合关系，并提升对未见词的泛化能力。词向量通常由神经模型在大规模语料上学习，作为后续任务的输入特征。
concepts.match_reason: “词向量”指的是词的嵌入表示（word embedding），而候选项中没有与之完全对应的同一概念；0 只是更泛的稠密向量，2 是更广的语言表示模型。

concepts.name: 预训练加微调范式
concepts.description: 预训练加微调范式是一种两阶段的语言模型训练流程：先在大规模数据上进行通用预训练以学习广泛知识与表示，再在特定任务的数据分布上进行微调以提升任务适配与性能。该范式使同一个预训练语言模型能够迁移到多种下游任务，并在通用能力与具体任务表现之间取得平衡；同时，若微调分布过窄，模型可能过度专门化而降低泛化能力。
concepts.match_reason: “预训练加微调范式”指的是先预训练、再在下游任务上微调的整体流程；候选项中没有完全对应这个整体范式的条目，0和2都更偏向预训练阶段本身。

concepts.name: model parallelism
concepts.description: Model parallelism is a distributed training technique that splits a model across multiple processors or devices. It allows very large models to be trained when a single device cannot hold the full set of parameters.
concepts.match_reason: Model parallelism splits a single model across devices, while distributed data parallel training splits data across devices and keeps one model replicated.

concepts.name: Approximate nearest neighbor Negative Contrastive Learning
concepts.description: Approximate nearest neighbor negative contrastive learning is a contrastive representation learning method for dense retrieval that mines hard negative examples using an approximate nearest neighbor index over a large corpus. Negatives are sampled by retrieving approximate nearest neighbors with the current model, yielding more globally relevant and difficult negatives than random or in-batch sampling. This improves optimization and increases retrieval quality by exposing the model to more informative contrastive signals.
aliases: Approximate nearest neighbor Negative Contrastive Estimation
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a dense retrieval training method for mining hard negatives with an ANN index, not nearest-neighbor retrieval itself or a datastore.
merges: Approximate nearest neighbor Negative Contrastive Estimation

concepts.name: bag-of-words
concepts.description: A text representation that models a document as an unordered multiset of terms, typically ignoring syntax and word order. It captures content through term presence and often term frequency, supporting efficient lexical matching in tasks such as sparse retrieval.
concepts.match_reason: Bag-of-words is a text representation, while sparse bag-of-words matching is a retrieval method that uses such representations.

concepts.name: 密集检索
concepts.description: 密集检索是一种检索范式，将查询与候选文档（或图元素）用向量表示，并依据向量相似度进行相似性搜索与排序，以找到与查询语义相关的结果。它通常借助神经编码器学习查询与文档的语义向量表示，从而在大规模语料中高效检索。
concepts.match_reason: “密集检索”是一个检索范式/类别；候选中没有完全同义项，0 和 1 只是其常见具体实现或框架，不是同一概念。

concepts.name: knowledge retrieval task
concepts.description: A knowledge retrieval task is one in which a system locates relevant information from a stored corpus or knowledge graph in response to a query. The goal is to select knowledge items that can support a correct answer, decision, or subsequent generation, often serving as the grounding step for bringing external information into a workflow.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a retrieval task, while the candidate is the notion of search relevance/degree of match; related but not the same concept.

concepts.name: temperature
concepts.description: Temperature is a sampling parameter that adjusts the sharpness (peakiness) of the probability distribution used for token selection. Lower temperatures make the distribution more concentrated and outputs more deterministic, while higher temperatures flatten it to increase variability.

concepts.name: PaLM
concepts.description: A large language model family developed by Google that is designed to perform a wide range of language and reasoning tasks. Different parameter scales of the model can be evaluated on benchmarks to measure capability.
concepts.match_reason: PaLM is a specific Google model family, while candidate 0 is the generic concept of a large language model, not the same denotation.

concepts.name: toxic text
concepts.description: Toxic text is language that is abusive, offensive, misleading, or otherwise harmful to people or groups. In the context of language models, it refers to undesirable outputs such as profanity, harassment, and slurs that safety mechanisms aim to prevent or reduce.

concepts.name: alignment
concepts.description: Alignment is the problem of making an artificial intelligence system behave in accordance with human values, intentions, or goals. For language models, it typically involves training and other techniques to reduce undesirable behavior and steer outputs toward helpful, instruction-following, and safe responses.

concepts.name: knowledge-intensive tasks
concepts.description: 知识密集型任务是指需要大量外部事实信息或已有记忆才能高质量完成的任务。此类任务通常依赖准确的信息检索与“落地”支撑，以保证生成结果的可靠性与可验证性。常见例子包括问答与事实核查。
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is broader general knowledge-intensive tasks, while the candidate is a narrower NLP-specific subset.

concepts.name: average pooling
concepts.description: Mean (average) pooling is an aggregation operation that averages a set of vectors into a single vector representation. It is used to combine token-, node-, or edge-level embeddings into a fixed-size summary embedding from a variable-length sequence.

concepts.name: accuracy
concepts.description: An evaluation metric that measures how often a model's predicted answer is correct. In question answering, it is commonly computed by checking whether the model's output matches the expected answer.
concepts.match_reason: Accuracy is a general correctness metric, while label accuracy is the classification-specific version; they are related but not the same concept.

concepts.name: 层次化索引
concepts.description: 层次化索引是一种以树形/层级结构组织内容并生成索引或摘要的检索与索引结构，通常通过父子关系连接不同层级。它从细粒度信息逐层汇总，在缩小单次检索范围、加速遍历与查找的同时，有助于降低检索错误并提升结果的稳健性。
concepts.match_reason: “层次化索引”是分层组织并检索的结构/方法，而“全局摘要”是聚合后的摘要结果，不是同一概念。

concepts.name: 基于图的社区发现
concepts.description: 基于图的社区发现是一类图分析方法，通过识别并划分图中的节点，使得同一社区内节点之间的连接更为密集，而不同社区之间连接相对稀疏。该方法通常利用节点、边及其属性刻画社区结构，并将社区作为中间结果用于下游任务，如信息聚合与查询支持。

concepts.name: Text Chunk
concepts.description: A text chunk is a contiguous segment of a longer document produced by splitting source text into smaller units. It is used as an independent processing or retrieval unit—often indexed and searched separately—to make handling large documents practical for downstream tasks.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a general text segment from any document, while the candidate is a specific Wikipedia-based chunking concept.

concepts.name: multi-hop reasoning
concepts.description: Multi-hop (multi-step) reasoning is a reasoning process that reaches an answer by chaining together multiple intermediate inference steps. It is used when relevant evidence is distributed across several linked items or when the relationship between two ideas must be inferred through intermediate connections rather than from a single direct match.
concepts.match_reason: The candidate is a benchmark for testing multi-step retrieval/reasoning, not the reasoning process itself.

concepts.name: contextual awareness
concepts.description: The ability of a system to take surrounding information, relationships, and structural cues into account when selecting or producing an answer. It helps avoid responses that are correct in isolation but incomplete in context, improving retrieval and interpretation to better match the user’s intent and task context.

concepts.name: Generation Component
concepts.description: The part of a retrieval-augmented generation system that generates the final natural-language output from the user query and retrieved (or otherwise provided) information. It uses a language model to condition on the available evidence and synthesize it into a coherent, contextually appropriate, human-like response.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is the answer-generation component within a RAG system, while candidate [0] is the broader generative model itself; they are related but not the same concept.

concepts.name: RQ-RAG
concepts.description: RQ-RAG is a retrieval-augmented generation approach that improves retrieval accuracy by automatically decomposing and reformulating complex queries into multiple, more targeted sub-queries. It uses rewriting and disambiguation to better align structured or multi-part questions with relevant knowledge sources before generating the final answer.
concepts.match_reason: RQ-RAG is a query-rewriting/decomposition retrieval method, while the candidates are graph/vector RAG variants, evaluation criteria, or a sequence model—not the same concept.

concepts.name: 位置表示
concepts.description: 位置表示是一种在序列模型中注入位置信息的表示方式，用以弥补注意力机制缺乏显式顺序感知能力的不足。它通常通过可学习的位置信号（如位置嵌入）使模型区分不同位置上的元素，从而获得对词元顺序及其相对位置的感知能力。
concepts.match_reason: “位置表示”是给序列注入位置信息的表示方法，不是注意力机制或自注意力本身；候选项都不等同。

concepts.name: 隐藏状态
concepts.description: 隐藏状态是序列模型在处理序列时为每个时间步维护的内部表示，用于汇总并承载到当前为止的历史信息。它在相邻时间步之间传递，使模型能够在当前位置生成时结合上下文、形成更具上下文相关性的表示。
concepts.match_reason: “隐藏状态”是循环神经网络内部维护并在时间步间传递的表示；候选项“循环神经网络”是整个模型，不是该内部状态本身。

concepts.name: linear projection
concepts.description: A linear projection is a transformation that maps vectors into a different-dimensional vector space using a linear function. In neural network models, it is used to convert inputs such as queries, keys, and values into representations of a desired size before further computation.

concepts.name: encoder-decoder attention
concepts.description: An attention mechanism in which a decoder attends from its output positions to encoder-produced representations. It conditions sequence generation on external inputs (e.g., documents) by letting each decoder position access information from all encoder positions via the encoder keys and values.

concepts.name: masking
concepts.description: Masking is a constraint in attention mechanisms that limits which positions a token may attend to, thereby controlling information flow in transformer models. In practice, disallowed attention scores are set to negative infinity before the softmax so those positions contribute zero weight to the attention output. This can enforce directional attention patterns (e.g., preventing bidirectional access) when desired.

concepts.name: encoder
concepts.description: The component of a sequence model that transforms the input sequence into internal representations. Its outputs can be attended to by the decoder through encoder-decoder attention.
concepts.match_reason: The encoder is the model component that produces representations of the input sequence; encoder-decoder attention is an attention mechanism that uses those encoder outputs, so they are related but not the same concept.

concepts.name: decoder
concepts.description: The component of a sequence model that generates output representations one position at a time. It uses self-attention over previous positions and can attend to encoder outputs through encoder-decoder attention.
concepts.match_reason: The decoder is the output-generating component of the model, while the candidates are the encoder and one attention mechanism it uses; neither denotes the same concept.

concepts.name: Inner-Layer Dimension
concepts.description: Inner-layer dimension is the size of the intermediate hidden representation within the feed-forward portion of a neural network, such as the position-wise sublayer in a Transformer. It determines the width of the network’s expansion between the input and output projections and directly impacts the total number of parameters.
concepts.match_reason: Inner-layer dimension is the feed-forward expansion width, while model dimension is the general hidden-state/embedding size; they are related but not the same concept.

concepts.name: learning rate schedule
concepts.description: A rule that changes the learning rate over the course of training. Schedules are used to stabilize optimization and improve convergence by varying update size over time.

concepts.name: warmup steps
concepts.description: warmup steps 是一种学习率调度策略：在训练初期先从较小的学习率开始，逐步升高到预设的目标学习率（或学习率上限）。这样可以在模型参数尚未充分适配时抑制不稳定的更新，减少训练开始阶段的震荡，从而使优化过程更稳定。

concepts.name: beam search
concepts.description: A heuristic decoding algorithm that keeps the top-scoring partial hypotheses at each step while generating a sequence. It approximates best-sequence search more efficiently than exhaustive search.

concepts.name: 自然语言处理任务
concepts.description: 一类让计算机理解、分析或生成自然语言的任务集合。典型任务包括句子级推断、问答、命名实体识别和文本改写等。

concepts.name: 语言推断
concepts.description: 一种自然语言推理任务，判断一个文本是否能够推出另一个文本，或在给定句对中判断它们之间的蕴含、矛盾或中立关系。该任务用于评估模型对句子/文本整体语义的理解及其语义推理能力。
concepts.match_reason: “语言推断”指的是自然语言推断/文本蕴含任务；候选中没有这个概念，0是句法分析，1是语言表示模型，均不同。

concepts.name: word embeddings
concepts.description: Word embeddings are learned continuous vector representations of tokens that map discrete vocabulary items into a low-dimensional space. They capture semantic and syntactic information so that similar words have similar vectors. These embeddings are used as inputs or features for neural language models and other downstream natural language processing tasks.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is the general notion of word embeddings, while [0] is specifically pre-trained word embeddings and [1] is a broader learned embedding concept, so neither is the same concept.

concepts.name: 注意力头数
concepts.description: 多头自注意力中并行注意力头的数量，用于将输入表示分割到不同表示子空间以捕捉多样的依赖关系。调整注意力头数会改变模型的注意力容量与表征能力，同时通常可在保持整体参数规模可控的情况下实现能力变化。
concepts.match_reason: “注意力头数”指的是多头注意力中的头的数量，是一个超参数/属性；候选项都在描述注意力机制本身，而不是头数这个概念。

concepts.name: sentence pair classification
concepts.description: Sentence pair classification is a natural language understanding task in which a model takes two sentences and predicts a label describing their relationship. It is used to evaluate how well a system recognizes relations between pieces of text, such as similarity, entailment, or contradiction.
concepts.match_reason: Sentence pair classification is a broader setup; Natural Language Inference is one specific type of sentence-pair task.

concepts.name: QQP
concepts.description: A paraphrase identification task that asks whether two questions are semantically equivalent. It is used to evaluate sentence-pair similarity and duplicate-question detection.

concepts.name: CoLA
concepts.description: A linguistic acceptability task that judges whether a sentence is grammatically acceptable in English. It is used to test syntactic well-formedness judgments.

concepts.name: STS-B
concepts.description: A semantic textual similarity task that measures how similar two sentences are in meaning. Systems are evaluated by how well their predicted similarity scores match human judgments.

concepts.name: MRPC
concepts.description: A paraphrase detection task that determines whether two sentences are paraphrases of one another. It is used to assess sentence-pair semantic equivalence.
concepts.match_reason: MRPC and QQP are both paraphrase detection benchmarks, but they are different datasets/tasks with different sources and scopes, not the same concept.

concepts.name: RTE
concepts.description: A text entailment task that asks whether one sentence entails another. It is a standard benchmark for recognizing inferential relationships between sentence pairs.

concepts.name: 分布式数据并行训练
concepts.description: 分布式数据并行训练是一种并行训练方式，将训练数据的不同批次分配到多个计算单元上，各自计算梯度并对同一个模型参数进行同步更新。它常用于提升大规模训练的吞吐量，但训练效率会受到参数同步与通信开销以及批大小选择的影响。
concepts.match_reason: “分布式数据并行训练”是把数据/梯度分散到多设备上的训练并行方式；“large mini-batch training”只是使用更大批量的训练策略，二者相关但不是同一概念。

concepts.name: pretraining data
concepts.description: The corpus or corpora used to train a model before task-specific fine-tuning or adaptation. This large pretraining dataset exposes the model to a broad distribution of language patterns and associations, shaping what it learns during pretraining; the choice and size of the data strongly influence the model’s behavior.

concepts.name: reversible tokenizer
concepts.description: A reversible tokenizer maps text into a sequence of tokens such that the original text can be deterministically and exactly recovered from the tokens. This enables token-based modeling and training while preserving recoverability of the source text representation.

concepts.name: backward pass
concepts.description: A backward pass is the computation that propagates gradients through a neural network to update parameters. It is the core operation used to perform gradient-based training.
concepts.match_reason: Backward pass and forward pass are opposite neural-network computations: gradients vs input/output propagation.

concepts.name: syntactic information
concepts.description: Syntactic information is information about the grammatical structure of language: how words and phrases are combined into well-formed sentences, including properties like word order and dependency relations. Language models use syntactic information to predict outputs that are grammatically well-formed in context, and it can be learned as a form of linguistic knowledge from text.

concepts.name: latent variable
concepts.description: A latent variable is an unobserved variable introduced in a probabilistic model to explain observed data. In retrieval-augmented generation, it can represent which document was selected to support the output, with probabilities summed over all possible values.

concepts.name: dense inner product model
concepts.description: A dense inner product model is a retrieval model that represents queries and documents as dense vector representations and scores each query–document pair by the dot product (inner product) of their vectors. The resulting scores are often normalized with a softmax to form a retrieval distribution. This simple dense scoring function supports efficient ranking in embedding-based systems.

concepts.name: 答案片段
concepts.description: 文档中与目标答案对应的连续词元序列。问答系统常把答案建模为可从候选文档中抽取的一个跨度，而不是自由生成的任意字符串。
concepts.match_reason: “答案片段”指文档中的答案跨度；候选项是“开放域问答微调”这一训练过程，不是同一概念。

concepts.name: document embedding
concepts.description: A vector representation of a document used for similarity-based retrieval or scoring. Document embeddings are compared with input embeddings to rank documents by relevance.
concepts.match_reason: Document embedding is the vector representation of a document, while none of the candidates denote that exact concept; they are a generic embedding function, an encoder/model, a retriever component, an index, or sentence embeddings.

concepts.name: re-embedding
concepts.description: The process of recomputing vector embeddings for documents in an index using the current model parameters (e.g., an updated retriever) so that stored representations remain consistent with the updated retrieval model. Re-embedding is used when previously stored embeddings may be stale, such as during training or model updates, but it can be computationally expensive for large corpora.
concepts.match_reason: Re-embedding is the process of recomputing document vectors, while the candidates are an embedding artifact, a retriever component, or an unrelated de-embedding operation.

concepts.name: Embedinput
concepts.description: Embedinput 是一种参数化编码器，用于将输入查询编码成向量表示，并将其映射到与文档编码器相同或可比较的检索嵌入空间中。该表示作为查询侧表示供检索函数使用，从而依据当前查询选择相关文档，并可在微调过程中继续更新。

concepts.name: exact string matching
concepts.description: A retrieval behavior in which a model relies on literal overlap between two text strings rather than broader semantic relevance. It can improve surface-form matching but misses paraphrases and other nonidentical expressions of relatedness.

concepts.name: Generation-based Open-QA
concepts.description: Generation-based Open-QA is an open-domain question answering approach that formulates answering as sequence generation. Given a question, a model generates the answer token by token directly from its parameters, without retrieving external documents.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a specific generation-only open-QA formulation, while candidate 2 is the broader open-domain QA task (typically retrieval-based), so they are not the same concept.

concepts.name: Transformer sequence-to-sequence model
concepts.description: A Transformer sequence-to-sequence model is an encoder–decoder architecture based on self-attention that maps an input sequence to an output sequence. It is widely used for conditional generation tasks such as summarization, translation, and question answering.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is specifically an encoder-decoder Transformer for sequence-to-sequence tasks, while the candidates are a general Transformer or its components, not the same concept.

concepts.name: PathRetriever
concepts.description: PathRetriever is a retrieval method that finds supporting passages by exploring paths through a structured representation of a document graph. By following chains of related passages, it aims to surface evidence that may be missed by single-hop retrieval and to support multi-hop reasoning.
concepts.match_reason: PathRetriever is a specific path-based retrieval method, while GraphRetriever is a broader graph-based multi-hop retrieval approach; they are related but not the same concept.

concepts.name: 负样本
concepts.description: 在监督学习或检索任务中，被标记为不相关、错误或不匹配的样本。负样本用于训练模型区分正确对象与干扰对象，并提高排序能力。
concepts.match_reason: 负样本与正样本是相对概念，分别表示不相关/错误样本与相关/正确样本，不是同一概念。

concepts.name: gold passage
concepts.description: 与某问题在原始数据集中标注的金标准上下文相匹配的段落，通常作为检索任务中的正例。它们代表用于回答该问题的目标证据文本，强调这些段落包含完成答案所需的信息。

concepts.name: cross-dataset generalization
concepts.description: Cross-dataset generalization is the ability of a model trained on one dataset to perform well on a different dataset without additional fine-tuning. It evaluates how robust learned representations and decision behavior are under dataset shift, often reflecting transfer of representations across tasks or domains.

concepts.name: hybrid model
concepts.description: 混合模型是同时结合参数化记忆和非参数化记忆的模型架构。它通过把模型内部知识与可检索知识源结合起来，提升可扩展性、可更新性和可解释性。
concepts.match_reason: “Hybrid model” is a broader architecture combining parametric and non-parametric memory, while “显式记忆” is just one retrievable memory mechanism, not the same concept.

concepts.name: Wikipedia dump
concepts.description: A snapshot of Wikipedia used as a large non-parametric knowledge source for retrieval-based systems. The dump is divided into chunks or articles so that documents can be embedded and searched efficiently.
concepts.match_reason: The candidate is specifically the English Wikipedia dump, while the incoming concept is the more general Wikipedia dump; that is a variant, not the exact same concept.

concepts.name: abstractive question answering
concepts.description: Abstractive question answering is a question answering setting in which a system generates an answer as free-form text rather than selecting an exact span from a source document. It synthesizes, paraphrases, or otherwise reformulates information to produce a coherent response, especially when the required answer is not explicitly present as a contiguous span.
concepts.match_reason: “Abstractive question answering” is a specific QA setting where answers are generated in free form, while candidate [0] is the broader general task of question answering.

concepts.name: extractive reader
concepts.description: An extractive reader is a question answering component that predicts answers by selecting a span or excerpt directly from the input passage(s). Because the answer must be explicitly present in the evidence text, it is limited to extractive outputs and is typically evaluated by the accuracy of the extracted span.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a QA reader component that extracts spans from passages, while the candidates are a QA setting, a full architecture, a full system, or a text passage—not the same concept.

concepts.name: 抽取式模型
concepts.description: 一种直接从输入上下文中选择或复制答案片段的问答模型。它通常依赖证据中已经显式出现的答案，因此在答案不出现在任何支持文档中时表现受限。
concepts.match_reason: “抽取式模型”指的是一种抽取答案的问答模型；候选项里没有同义的模型概念，只有相关任务（问答）和答案片段，不是同一概念。

concepts.name: document posterior
concepts.description: 在给定查询与生成/答案条件下，由语言模型诱导出的文档概率分布。它刻画不同文档相对支持目标输出的可能性，可作为训练或优化检索器时的目标分布。
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a posterior distribution over documents, while the candidate is the latent document variable itself; a variable and its posterior are different concepts.

concepts.name: news articles
concepts.description: News articles are journalistic texts that report events, developments, or analysis in a news style. They can be generated by language models and evaluated for realism and human-likeness.

concepts.name: 预训练语言表示
concepts.description: 预训练语言表示是在大规模语料上先学习得到的语言表征，能够作为通用输入被迁移到多种下游任务中，从而降低对任务专用特征的依赖。它们通常由模型自动学习以减少人工特征工程需求，同时提升跨任务的复用性。
concepts.match_reason: Incoming concept is the learned pretrained language representations themselves, while the candidates are a pretraining method, an approach using such features, or language models; none denotes the same concept.

concepts.name: search ranking
concepts.description: Search ranking is the task of ordering retrieved results so that the most relevant items appear first. In text systems, it arranges documents in ranked order based on their scores or estimated relevance, determining what users see or what later processing stages receive. Ranking can also introduce a bias that earlier results are more likely to be useful or correct.
concepts.match_reason: Search ranking is the general task of ordering results, while re-ranking is a specific second-stage ranking variant after initial retrieval.

concepts.name: Similarity function
concepts.description: A similarity function is a mathematical measure of how close two vector representations are. Common examples include cosine similarity and dot product. In dense retrieval, it is used to score and rank candidate items by their semantic similarity to a query.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a general similarity function, while candidate 0 is a specific decomposable variant and candidate 1 is the resulting relevance score, not the function itself.

concepts.name: Learning to rank
concepts.description: Learning to rank is a family of supervised machine-learning methods for training models to assign relevance scores to items for ranking tasks. It is commonly used to order items (e.g., retrieved documents) by predicted usefulness to a query, typically optimizing a scoring function so relevant items rank higher than irrelevant ones.
concepts.match_reason: Learning to rank is a supervised method for training ranking models, while search ranking is the broader task and re-ranking is only a specific second-stage variant.

concepts.name: search relevance
concepts.description: Search relevance is the degree to which retrieved documents or passages satisfy an information need expressed by a query. It reflects not only topical match but also the usefulness and trustworthiness of the retrieved content for subsequent tasks, such as informing downstream generation. Higher relevance of retrieval results typically improves reasoning quality, factuality, and overall effectiveness in retrieval-augmented systems.
concepts.match_reason: Search relevance is the underlying notion of how well a document satisfies a query, while candidate 3 is a numerical score measuring it, not the concept itself.

concepts.name: reinforcement learning
concepts.description: A learning paradigm in which an agent improves its behavior by receiving rewards or penalties for actions taken in an environment. In retrieval and ranking, it can be used to optimize decision-making over candidate selections.

concepts.name: language models as knowledge bases
concepts.description: 将知识与语言规律编码进语言模型参数中的观点，认为大语言模型可把部分事实性信息以参数形式存储。基于这种参数化表示，模型在回答问题时可直接从其内部表征进行预测与生成，从而在无需外部检索的情况下充当类似知识库的功能。该研究关注模型对事实查询的直接回答能力及其从参数表征中检索信息的效果。
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a view of LMs as storing/retrieving knowledge like a knowledge base, not the model itself, factual knowledge, or the language modeling objective.

concepts.name: OpenAI API
concepts.description: 一个提供模型调用接口的应用程序编程接口，用于通过预设提示和参数与语言模型交互。它常被用来获取标准化的模型输出或作为实验默认配置的来源。

concepts.name: reasoning ability
concepts.description: 指完成需要多步推导、分析或计算任务的能力，能够通过逻辑或概率等步骤进行信息整合与推理，从而解决问题并得出结论。它通常体现为将问题分解、连接证据并进行推断的表现。

concepts.name: Codex
concepts.description: Codex is a language model family specialized for code generation and related text-to-code tasks. It is accessed through model variants such as code-davinci-002 in the OpenAI API.

concepts.name: bias
concepts.description: A systematic tendency in model outputs to favor certain groups, viewpoints, or associations over others. In language models, bias is often evaluated by checking for uneven or stereotyped behavior across prompts and contexts.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is the general bias phenomenon, while the candidate is biased text, a specific manifestation of that phenomenon rather than the same concept.

concepts.name: generalization
concepts.description: Generalization is the ability of a model to apply learned behavior to new, unseen inputs or tasks beyond those encountered during training. Higher generalization performance indicates that the learned patterns—such as retrieval and reasoning strategies—transfer effectively to data and queries outside the training or evaluation set.

concepts.name: implicit continuation
concepts.description: A prompting method in which the intended task is conveyed indirectly by providing the beginning of a text and asking a model to continue it. The model infers what to do from the given context rather than relying on an explicit instruction, generating the desired completion or output directly.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is prompting by giving a text prefix and letting the model continue implicitly; neither candidate refers to that continuation-style prompting.

concepts.name: interpretability
concepts.description: Transparency is the extent to which a model’s behavior, data use, and decision process can be understood and inspected by humans. It helps users and developers determine why particular outputs were produced and build trust in AI systems. In retrieval-augmented systems, this also involves examining retrieved documents and how they influence outputs.

concepts.name: quadratic complexity
concepts.description: 一种计算复杂度随输入规模平方增长的性质，表现为计算成本或内存消耗与输入大小的平方成正比。对于较长序列或更大规模数据，处理代价会迅速显著增加。

concepts.name: training set filtering
concepts.description: 训练集过滤是一种数据清理与筛选技术，用于在模型训练前剔除低质量、噪声、错误或不可靠的训练样本。通过减少可能误导模型学习的内容来提升剩余数据的质量，从而降低噪声并改善模型的学习效果与输出可靠性。

concepts.name: RETRO
concepts.description: RETRO is a retrieval-augmented language model that scales retrieval memory to trillions of tokens and changes the model architecture so that retrieved documents are provided as input. It uses large-scale retrieval to supply external information that helps the model make better predictions.
concepts.match_reason: RETRO is a retrieval-augmented language model architecture, while the candidates are components of retrieval systems; they are related but not the same concept.

concepts.name: natural language processing
concepts.description: A field focused on enabling computers to analyze, understand, generate, and interact using human language. It includes tasks such as translation, question answering, text generation, and information extraction.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is the field of natural language processing, while the candidates are a task set, a data-to-SQL task, the language itself, and the generation subtask.

concepts.name: sensemaking task
concepts.description: An analytical information-seeking task that requires integrating evidence across multiple entities and sources to form a coherent global understanding. By reasoning over relationships among people, places, and events (rather than performing local fact lookup), it supports anticipating likely trajectories and acting effectively.
concepts.match_reason: The candidate is a specific query type over an entire dataset, while the incoming concept is a broader analytical task involving sensemaking and reasoning across entities; they are related but not the same concept.

concepts.name: text embeddings
concepts.description: Numerical representations of text that place semantically similar items near one another in a vector space. They are commonly used to retrieve records by comparing the embedding of a query with the embeddings of candidate records.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is the general notion of embeddings for text, while the candidates are narrower forms (document/word/sentence embeddings) or related components (space/function), not the same concept.

concepts.name: semantic similarity
concepts.description: Semantic similarity is a measure of how closely two texts or representations match in meaning rather than exact wording. It is commonly computed by representing each item as an embedding vector and comparing the vectors with a similarity function such as cosine similarity.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is the general idea of semantic similarity, while none of the candidates denotes that exact concept; STS-B is only a specific evaluation task and the others are related but different.

concepts.name: 知识图谱抽取
concepts.description: 从自然语言文本中识别实体、关系及相关属性，并将其组织为结构化知识图谱的过程。该过程将非结构化语料转换为由节点与边构成的图表示，为后续的图构建、检索与推理提供基础。

concepts.name: 事实锚定
concepts.description: 事实锚定是一种让生成输出建立在明确事实或结构化证据基础上的方法，将生成内容与外部证据或可检索的信息源相联系。通过让模型依据这些可验证的依据进行生成，它提高输出的真实性，并增强可追溯性与一致性，降低脱节风险。
concepts.match_reason: “事实锚定”是基于事实/结构证据来约束生成的概念，而候选[1]是解码阶段提升事实性的具体生成策略，二者不完全同义；[0]则是性质而非方法。

concepts.name: 社区
concepts.description: 在图中由若干彼此联系更紧密的元素组成的子图或元素集合。社区检测会把图索引划分为这样的组，以支持并行摘要和后续查询处理。
concepts.match_reason: “社区”指图中内部连接更紧密的子图/元素集合；候选0是社区发现方法，候选1是社区摘要，均不是该概念本身。

concepts.name: Chunk Size
concepts.description: Chunk size is the length or amount of text included in each text chunk. It is a design parameter that affects how many processing calls are needed and how much information may be lost or retained within each chunk.
concepts.match_reason: Chunk size is a parameter describing the length of each chunk, while the candidates are the chunks themselves, not the size setting.

concepts.name: Entity and Relation Extraction
concepts.description: Entity and relation extraction is the process of identifying important entities in text and determining the relationships that connect them. It converts unstructured passages into structured representations, often graph-based, by extracting entity nodes and relationship edges for later analysis or knowledge graph construction.
aliases: extract instances of important entities and the relationships between the entities

concepts.name: 全局查询
concepts.description: 全局查询是面向整个图或数据集的高层次检索与问答过程，旨在获取跨社区、跨主题的整体性答案。它关注更广泛的主题、抽象关系与全局上下文，而非孤立细节；通常依赖跨层级的社区摘要来完成意义理解与推断。
concepts.match_reason: “全局查询”指面向整个图或数据集的查询问题本身；候选项分别是全局回答、全局摘要和社区回答，都是回答/摘要结果，不是查询概念。

concepts.name: semantic search
concepts.description: Semantic search is a retrieval method that ranks text passages by semantic similarity to a query rather than by exact keyword overlap. It typically represents queries and passages with vector embeddings and compares them to find the most meaning-related chunks, often for use as relevant context for downstream tasks such as text generation.
concepts.match_reason: Semantic search is a retrieval method; semantic similarity is a measure used by such methods, so they are related but not the same concept.

concepts.name: corpus
concepts.description: A corpus is a large collection of texts or documents treated as a unified body of source material for analysis, indexing, or model support. In retrieval-based systems, it is the set of documents (and their content) from which relevant passages are searched and retrieved.
concepts.match_reason: Both candidates are specific kinds of corpus used for retrieval, while the incoming concept is the broader general notion of a corpus as any collection of texts.

concepts.name: 可扩展性
concepts.description: 系统在数据量、用户量或工作负载增加时，仍能保持性能与资源消耗在可控范围内的能力。它关注的是随着规模扩大，性能下降或成本是否不会成比例恶化。对于知识密集型系统，这也决定了能否高效处理更大规模的信息源。

concepts.name: document summarization
concepts.description: A task that produces a shorter version of one or more documents while preserving the main information. It often benefits from retrieval when summaries must be grounded in external evidence, and it can be used in graph-based retrieval systems as compact topic-level representations.

concepts.name: FILCO
concepts.description: A context-filtering method for improving the quality of retrieved information supplied to generative models. It identifies useful context with lexical and information-theoretic techniques and trains filtering models to refine retrieved passages at test time.

concepts.name: imitative falsehood
concepts.description: 模仿性虚假是一种错误的陈述，语言模型在生成时会模仿训练数据中常见且看似合理的表达模式。它通常并非基于可靠的事实把握，而是对“听起来像真的”内容进行复现，因此尽管与世界事实不符，却可能更难被识别或避免。

concepts.name: multi-document question answering
concepts.description: 一种问答任务，答案需要根据分布在多个文档中的相关信息进行推断，并将跨文档的证据整合后形成最终回答。它用于检验模型在多源、长上下文条件下能否有效检索并利用多份证据，而不仅依赖单一段落。

concepts.name: factuality hallucination
concepts.description: 事实性幻觉（事实捏造）是指生成内容与客观事实或可核验证据不符的幻觉类型。语言模型可能产出听起来很像“事实”的陈述或细节，但其无法与既有现实知识或可靠信息相对应，因而难以被证实为真。
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a specific type of hallucination; candidate 0 is the quality of factuality, and candidate 1 is a detection process, so neither denotes the same concept.

concepts.name: datastore
concepts.description: A datastore is a collection of key-value pairs built from training examples, where each key is a context representation and each value is the corresponding target information. During inference, the model queries the datastore with a new context representation to retrieve similar entries and use the retrieved information to support its prediction.

concepts.name: GSM8K
concepts.description: GSM8K is a benchmark dataset of grade-school math word problems used to evaluate arithmetic and multi-step reasoning in language models. Models are assessed on how accurately they solve the problems and produce the correct final answers.

concepts.name: Webtext2 training set
concepts.description: 用于训练语言模型的特定文本数据集，常作为大规模语料来进行自回归语言建模等实验。不同来源或版本的数据规模与组成会影响训练结果，从而导致拟合得到的数值常量与经验关系不同。

concepts.name: key-value retrieval
concepts.description: 一种检索任务或基准测试：给定输入中的键值对以及目标查询（或目标键），要求系统根据目标键定位对应的值并输出。该任务用于在简单、可控的合成条件下隔离并评估模型的检索与上下文/条件推理能力，重点考察“从键到值”的精确匹配与信息获取。

concepts.name: dual-level retrieval system
concepts.description: A retrieval strategy that searches knowledge at two different levels of granularity, typically combining fine-grained details with broader contextual structure. Its goal is to improve relevance and completeness by aggregating coarse- and fine-level retrieval signals, yielding better downstream generation quality.

concepts.name: 低秩分解矩阵
concepts.description: 低秩分解矩阵是一种将矩阵参数化为两个或多个秩较低矩阵乘积（或等价的多因子形式）的分解方法，使得得到的近似/更新矩阵具有较低秩。它通过用更少的自由参数来压缩存储与计算开销，并常作为受约束的更新或模型适配手段来保持主要表达能力。

concepts.name: standard prompting
concepts.description: A prompting approach that asks a model to answer directly without providing intermediate reasoning demonstrations. It typically elicits a short final answer rather than a step-by-step solution.
concepts.match_reason: "Standard prompting" is a specific prompting style without intermediate reasoning demonstrations, while candidate [1] is the broader concept of prompting in general.

concepts.name: Generative Pre-trained Transformer
concepts.description: 一种基于 Transformer 架构的自回归预训练语言模型，通过受限自注意力实现从左到右的生成：每个 token 只能关注其左侧上下文，因此预测仅依赖已生成的先前内容。模型先在大规模无标注文本上进行语言建模以学习通用语言表示，再通过微调适配到具体下游任务，从而将预训练获得的语言知识迁移到多种自然语言处理问题。
concepts.match_reason: GPT is a left-to-right generative, unidirectional language model, while BERT is a bidirectional encoder model; they are different concepts.

concepts.name: compute budget
concepts.description: 计算预算是指用于训练或评估模型的可用计算资源总量（上限），通常视为固定资源在模型规模、训练时长与数据规模之间进行分配。它约束在给定条件下可取得的训练进展，并影响这些因素之间的最优权衡。
concepts.match_reason: “compute budget” is a resource limit/allowance, while “training compute” is the amount actually consumed during training; related but not the same concept.

concepts.name: approximate nearest neighbor index
concepts.description: 一种用于在大规模集合中进行相似性搜索的索引数据结构，可针对给定查询快速找到最接近的向量或文档候选结果。通过近似搜索在效率与召回之间进行权衡，从而避免对集合中所有项目进行穷尽比较；因此常用于大规模检索，并可支持在线更新场景。
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is the general data structure type, while FAISS index is a specific implementation of an ANN index, not the same concept.

concepts.name: 适配器
concepts.description: 适配器是一种参数高效微调方法：在预训练模型中插入少量可训练的适配器模块，用以将预训练能力适配到特定任务，而不是对全部原始参数进行大幅更新。通过只训练新增模块来学习任务特定变化，从而显著减少需要更新的参数量；但由于额外模块计算，可能带来一定推理延迟。

concepts.name: adapter layer
concepts.description: An adapter layer is a small trainable neural network module inserted into a pre-trained model to enable parameter-efficient adaptation to new tasks. concepts.name: U-shaped performance curve
concepts.description: A pattern in which performance is higher at the beginning and end of a sequence and lower in the middle. It often indicates that systems access and use edge information more effectively than information located in intermediate positions.

concepts.name: input context
concepts.description: The collection of texts provided to a model at inference time, including the question and the candidate documents. In multi-document question answering, the arrangement and length of the input context can affect whether the model accesses the correct evidence.

concepts.name: knowledge preparation
concepts.description: Knowledge preparation is the set of steps in a retrieval system performed before search to structure and transform external documents or knowledge repositories for efficient retrieval. It typically involves organizing and chunking source material and creating representations or indexes that support fast, accurate, and relevant query-time results.

concepts.name: BLEU
concepts.description: 一种机器翻译评测指标，通过比较机器译文与参考译文的 n-gram 重叠程度来衡量翻译质量。数值越高通常表示译文与参考译文越接近。

concepts.name: large mini-batch training
concepts.description: 一种训练方式，在每个迭代步骤中将许多样本组成较大的 mini-batch 一起计算并在随后进行一次参数更新。通过提高每步的并行处理效率，它可能改变优化行为，并在相近训练轮数与计算成本下影响如困惑度及下游任务性能。若硬件内存受限，可通过梯度累积等方式等效实现较大的 mini-batch，通常还需要配合学习率调整以维持或提升最终表现。

concepts.name: optimal batch size
concepts.description: 在分布式训练中，一个在数据并行下平衡速度与优化效率的批大小阈值。当批大小接近或超过该阈值时，进一步增大批次通常会带来递减的优化收益。因而“最佳批大小”指在给定模型与训练目标下能取得最佳训练效率的批大小，并可在大模型场景中增长到很大。

concepts.name: latent knowledge retriever
concepts.description: 潜在知识检索器是一种学习得到的检索组件，将“文档选择”建模为隐变量，在文本知识源中为给定输入分配检索分布并选择相关信息供语言模型使用。其训练通常不需要显式标注应检索的具体文档，而是通过下游预测目标的反馈间接优化检索器，使检索结果在动态、上下文化的条件下对任务输出更有帮助。可与语言模型联合训练，从而使检索过程受任务反馈影响。

concepts.name: knowledge-augmented encoder
concepts.description: A knowledge-augmented encoder is a generation component that conditions on both the original input and retrieved documents. It uses retrieved evidence to improve the prediction of the output sequence.

concepts.name: one-shot learning
concepts.description: A setting in which a model is given a single demonstration at inference time before performing a task. concepts.name: gradient norm
concepts.description: The magnitude of a gradient vector during optimization. It reflects how strong an update signal is for the parameters and is often used to analyze training dynamics.
concepts.match_reason: Gradient norm is the magnitude of a gradient vector; gradient noise scale measures stochasticity/variance in gradients, so they are different concepts.

concepts.name: BERT-Siamese

concepts.name: 预训练模型权重
concepts.description: 预训练模型权重是指在通用大规模数据或源任务上预先训练得到的模型参数（权重矩阵/参数集合），用于在进行下游任务适配前提供初始知识与表示能力。下游适配时，它们通常作为微调起点，并可能被继续更新或在大部分保持冻结，仅训练少量新增参数，以在降低计算与数据成本的同时实现任务迁移。

concepts.name: GPT-Neo

concepts.name: adversarial procedure
concepts.description: An adversarial procedure is a method for constructing or curating evaluation items by deliberately targeting a model’s weaknesses. Examples are iteratively selected or filtered—often using model predictions—so the resulting dataset is more challenging and less likely to be solved via superficial cues, thereby exposing the behavior under study.

concepts.name: MAWPS benchmark
concepts.description: A benchmark dataset of math word problems expressed in natural language, used to evaluate systems on arithmetic and symbolic reasoning. It includes subsets of varying difficulty, ranging from easier single-step problems to more complex multi-step reasoning.

concepts.name: human-labeled comparisons
concepts.description: 人类对不同模型输出进行成对或成组的比较，并为候选输出标注偏好或优劣关系，形成包含“人类更喜欢哪种结果”的数据集。该数据集常用于训练奖励模型，将比较偏好信息转化为可计算的奖励信号，并进一步用于优化模型目标，使生成内容更符合人类期望。

concepts.name: statistical language model
concepts.description: 统计语言模型是一种语言模型，利用统计学习估计词序列（或标记序列）的概率，从而实现词预测。它通常依赖马尔可夫假设等简化条件，用观测到的频率与上下文关系来刻画条件概率。该模型常用于自然语言处理与信息检索等任务。

concepts.name: ChatGPT
concepts.description: An AI chatbot built on large language models for interactive dialogue. It is designed to generate helpful conversational responses across a wide range of topics.

concepts.name: primacy bias
concepts.description: A tendency to give disproportionately strong weight to information that appears near the beginning of a sequence or context. In language model behavior, it can make early context easier to use than middle context.

concepts.name: recency bias
concepts.description: A tendency to give disproportionately strong weight to information that appears near the end of a sequence or context. In language model behavior, it can make late context easier to use than middle context.
concepts.match_reason: Primacy bias is the opposite concept: it emphasizes early information, while recency bias emphasizes late information.

concepts.name: Taxonomy of hallucination
concepts.description: A taxonomy of hallucination is a structured classification scheme that groups hallucination phenomena into distinct categories and subcategories. It helps organize analysis of underlying causes and supports the development of detection methods and mitigation strategies.

concepts.name: faithfulness hallucination
concepts.description: Faithfulness hallucination is a type of hallucination in which generated content diverges from the user input or fails to remain self-consistent. It covers cases where the output does not faithfully reflect the instruction, context, or its own internal logic.

concepts.name: hierarchical community

concepts.name: global sensemaking
concepts.description: 对整个语料库进行宏观理解、归纳与解释的过程，目标是识别跨文本的主题、模式和关联。它强调从整体层面生成洞见，而不仅是回答局部事实问题。

concepts.name: textual subgraph retrieval
concepts.description: The process of selecting a connected subset of nodes and text from a larger graph for downstream language-model use. It aims to find the most relevant subgraph structure rather than retrieving isolated documents, so that multi-hop context is preserved.

concepts.name: divide-and-conquer strategy
concepts.description: An algorithmic approach that solves a complex problem by splitting it into smaller parts, solving those parts, and combining the results. In graph retrieval, it can be used to search for an optimal subgraph structure more efficiently than exhaustive methods.

concepts.name: textual subgraph
concepts.description: A subgraph whose nodes, edges, or both are associated with text, combining the subgraph’s structural relationships with the textual content it contains. concepts.name: hierarchical text descriptions
concepts.description: Hierarchical text descriptions are textual representations of graph data organized across multiple levels of abstraction, typically in a tree-like progression from coarse to fine detail. concepts.name: WebQSP

concepts.name: ExplaGraphs
concepts.description: 一个面向常识推理的数据集，重点在于根据图中的关系判断论证或辩论中的立场。它用于评测模型对图结构中因果、支持与反对等常识关系的理解能力。

concepts.name: low-level knowledge discovery
concepts.description: 一种面向具体实体及其细粒度信息的知识检索与发现方法，强调精确获取局部、具体的事实细节及其关联。它通常聚焦特定事实、实体间关系或邻接线索，用于需要定位精确证据并给出严格引用支持的问答与查询场景。
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is specifically fine-grained knowledge retrieval, while the candidate is a dual-level system that includes both fine-grained and broader retrieval, so they are not the same concept.

concepts.name: HyDE
concepts.description: HyDE is a retrieval method that first uses a language model to generate a hypothetical, answer-bearing document or passage from a query. That generated text is then embedded and used to retrieve relevant text chunks, improving the quality of what the final system can support or answer.

concepts.name: RAG pipeline
concepts.description: RAG（检索增强生成）管线是一种检索增强生成的端到端流程：先对外部知识源进行切分、向量化并组织索引（常以向量数据库为核心），再根据用户查询进行语义检索，取回相关文本片段，最后将检索结果与用户输入一同提供给生成模型以生成回答。通过在生成时引入可检索的外部证据，RAG可提升答案的准确性与可论据性，并缓解纯参数化模型在知识覆盖与时效性方面的不足。其效果受检索粒度与知识组织/索引方式影响。

concepts.name: 英德机器翻译任务

concepts.name: 英法机器翻译任务

concepts.name: scaled dot-product attention
concepts.description: An attention mechanism that computes compatibility scores by taking the dot product between queries and keys, then scaling the result before applying a softmax. The scaling helps stabilize gradients and makes the attention weights numerically well behaved in high-dimensional settings.

concepts.name: parameter-free position representation
concepts.description: Parameter-free position representation encodes token order in a sequence without learning additional position-specific parameters. It adds position information to token embeddings—using relative or absolute position cues—so a model without recurrence or convolution can distinguish different token positions and their order via attention.

concepts.name: convolutional layer
concepts.description: A layer that applies convolutional filters over local neighborhoods of a sequence. Its computation is parallel across positions, but information can propagate only gradually through stacked layers, giving a logarithmic maximum path length in the kernel size.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a general convolutional layer, while candidate 0 is the specific 1x1 convolution variant, not the same concept.

concepts.name: sinusoidal positional encoding
concepts.description: A fixed positional encoding that represents each position with sine and cosine functions at different frequencies. The frequencies form a geometric progression, which can help the model learn relative offsets and may support extrapolation to longer sequences.
aliases: sin and cosine functions of different frequencies

concepts.name: dynamic masking
concepts.description: A masking strategy in which the masked token positions are regenerated every time a sequence is fed to the model. This produces different corruption patterns across epochs and increases the variety of training examples.
concepts.match_reason: Dynamic masking is the regeneration of masked positions each time; none of the candidates denotes that concept, and static masking is the contrasting fixed-pattern variant.

concepts.name: segment-pair
concepts.description: A segment-pair is a pretraining input construction that forms each example from two text segments presented together as a single input. The segments are typically separated by a special marker or delimiter so the model can distinguish the pair, and they may be sampled from within the same document or from different documents. It is commonly used to support objectives such as next sentence prediction by modeling relationships between the two segments.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is the general two-segment input format, while candidate 0 is a specific BERT variant that also includes the NSP objective; they are not the exact same concept.

concepts.name: full sentences
concepts.description: An input construction that packs sequences of text from multiple documents into a contiguous sequence up to a fixed token limit. It may cross document boundaries and can insert an extra separator token between documents. It is used to create training sequences of roughly fixed length.

concepts.name: k近邻模型
concepts.description: k近邻模型是一种基于表示相似度或距离度量的检索型非参数方法。给定查询表示后，在存储样本中找到与其最接近的k个邻居，并根据这些邻居携带的标签或输出生成预测。它可用于多种任务；在语言建模中，可借助邻近样本的前缀嵌入信息来辅助下一词预测。
concepts.match_reason: kNN-LM 是结合预训练语言模型的特定检索式语言建模方法，不等同于泛指的 k 近邻模型。

concepts.name: 线性插值
concepts.description: 线性插值是一种将两个或多个概率分布（或表示）按权重进行加权混合的组合方式。它常用于在不同来源的输出之间进行折中与融合，例如将检索得到的分布与原模型的分布结合，以同时利用记忆检索与参数化预测能力。

concepts.name: prefix embedding
concepts.description: A prefix embedding is a fixed-size vector representation of a text prefix (context) that encodes preceding information to predict the next token. It is produced by a pretrained language model and captures the context in a latent space so that internal states can be stored and compared. Similar contexts yield similar embeddings under a distance or similarity measure.
concepts.match_reason: A prefix embedding represents a prefix/context for next-word prediction, not a general word embedding or the specific token-level contextual embeddings described here.

concepts.name: Wikitext-103
concepts.description: A large benchmark corpus of Wikipedia text used for evaluating language models. It is commonly used to compare model perplexity and other language modeling performance measures.

concepts.name: squared L2 distance
concepts.description: A distance metric that measures the squared Euclidean distance between two vectors. In retrieval systems, it is used to identify the closest stored representations to a query representation.

concepts.name: WIKI-100M
concepts.description: WIKI-100M is a random subset of English Wikipedia containing about 100 million tokens. It is composed of complete articles and is used as a smaller-scale corpus derived from a larger Wikipedia collection.
concepts.match_reason: WIKI-100M is a specific 100M-token Wikipedia subset, while Wikitext-103 is a different Wikipedia benchmark corpus for language modeling.

concepts.name: WIKI-3B
concepts.description: WIKI-3B is an English Wikipedia corpus containing about 2.87 billion tokens. It consists of whole articles, with some articles held out for validation and test.
concepts.match_reason: WIKI-3B and WIKI-100M are different Wikipedia corpora with different sizes and sampling; they are related but not the same concept.

concepts.name: interpolation parameter
concepts.description: A tunable coefficient used to combine two probability distributions or scores. In retrieval-augmented language models, it controls the balance between the base model and the retrieved-neighbor distribution.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is the tunable coefficient for interpolation, while the candidate is the interpolation method itself, so they are not the same concept.

concepts.name: question encoder
concepts.description: A model component that converts a question into a vector representation. The resulting embedding is used to compare the question with candidate passages during retrieval or matching.
concepts.match_reason: A question encoder is a specific encoder for questions; the candidates are broader architectures/functions or output embeddings, not the same concept.

concepts.name: 少样本学习
concepts.description: 一种只依赖少量示例或简单指令就完成新任务的能力或设置。它强调模型在几乎不进行额外参数更新的情况下，利用上下文中的少量演示来适配任务。
concepts.match_reason: “少样本学习”是用少量示例适配新任务的学习设置；“长尾模式”指数据中罕见但分布长尾的模式，二者不是同一概念。

concepts.name: Test set contamination
concepts.description: Test set contamination is the unintended overlap between a model’s training data and the evaluation or test data, such as identical examples or highly similar ones appearing in training. This overlap can inflate measured performance because the model may have effectively “seen” parts of the held-out benchmarks, making generalization appear better than it truly is.
aliases: C Details of Test Set Contamination Studies

concepts.name: meta-learning
concepts.description: A learning approach in which a model acquires general skills or inductive biases during training and then uses them at inference time to adapt quickly to a new task. It is often described as “learning to learn,” because prior experience is meant to improve rapid adaptation to unfamiliar problems.

concepts.name: stochastic gradient variance
concepts.description: The variability of gradient estimates produced by stochastic optimization across different samples or stochastic draws (e.g., mini-batches). High gradient variance can make training unstable and slow convergence, while reducing it can improve optimization stability and is commonly studied when analyzing sampling and weighting strategies.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is the variance of stochastic gradients across mini-batches; the candidate is a different but related measure called gradient noise scale, not the same concept.

concepts.name: negative sampling
concepts.description: A training strategy that supplies examples known or assumed to be incorrect so a model can learn to distinguish them from correct ones. In retrieval, the choice of negatives strongly affects training difficulty and final performance.
concepts.match_reason: “Negative sampling” is the training strategy of using incorrect examples, while [0] is only a specific subtype (in-batch negatives) and [1] is the examples themselves, not the strategy.

concepts.name: Binary cross entropy
concepts.description: Binary cross entropy is a loss function that measures the discrepancy between predicted probabilities (or scores converted to probabilities) and binary target labels. In ranking and retrieval settings, it can be used to compare positive and negative pairs by penalizing miscalibrated predictions. It can become uninformative when examples are already classified with high confidence, since the gradients diminish.
concepts.match_reason: Binary cross entropy is a specific binary-label variant of cross entropy loss, not the same concept as the general cross entropy loss.

concepts.name: TREC 2019 Deep Learning Track
concepts.description: TREC 2019 深度学习赛道是一个面向文档检索与排序的公开评测基准，提供测试集合与官方评价设置，用于在标准化条件下比较不同检索系统的效果。该基准常用于衡量稠密检索与传统稀疏检索（例如基于关键词的检索）在排序性能方面的差异，并配套相应的相关性评估以支撑系统对比。
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a specific TREC 2019 Deep Learning retrieval track, while candidate [0] is the broader TREC benchmark suite and [2] is a QA track; none denotes the same specific track.

concepts.name: 支持文档
concepts.description: 为回答问题而从外部知识源检索到的相关文档。它们作为证据与上下文，用于支撑后续答案的抽取或生成，尤其在开放域问答中可直接提供答案所依据的文本。
concepts.match_reason: “支持文档”是检索得到的证据/上下文文档本身；候选项是使用这些文档进行生成的整体方法，不是同一概念。

concepts.name: 多段落证据聚合
concepts.description: 多段落证据聚合是一种将来自多个证据来源的片段进行合并与综合的过程，用于在单个信息不充分或不同段落分别提供部分线索时，支撑更完整的推理与问答。它常用于开放域问答等场景，通过融合跨文档或跨段落的支持信息来提升答案或候选结果的排序与选择准确性。
aliases: aggregating and combining evidence from multiple passages
concepts.match_reason: “多段落证据聚合”是对多个段落证据进行合并推理的机制；“支持性段落”只是被检索到的证据段落本身，不是同一概念。

concepts.name: hard expectation-maximization
concepts.description: Hard expectation-maximization is a variant of the expectation-maximization algorithm that learns using hard assignments: for each instance, it selects the most likely latent explanation rather than averaging over all latent possibilities. It alternates between choosing a single latent value (the “hard E-step”) and updating model parameters based on that selection (the “hard M-step”). It is especially useful under weak or noisy supervision when latent variables are unobserved and exact marginalization is impractical due to a large latent space.

concepts.name: Multi-Passage BERT
concepts.description: A question answering model that uses BERT over multiple retrieved passages. It aggregates evidence across passages to improve answer prediction in open-domain question answering.

concepts.name: 前缀调优
concepts.description: 前缀调优是一种参数高效的适配方法，通过学习一段连续的可训练前缀表示，并将其加入模型的输入或注意力上下文以影响生成。原模型权重通常保持冻结，训练只更新前缀参数，从而使模型更好地适配下游任务。它也常与其他微调方法协同使用。
concepts.match_reason: 前缀调优是通过在输入侧加入可训练前缀向量的参数高效微调方法；候选中的适配器、LoRA 和预训练加微调范式都不是这一具体概念。

concepts.name: AdptD
concepts.description: AdptD is a parameter-efficient adapter-based method that adapts a pretrained model by training only a small set of added or modified, task-specific parameters while keeping most of the pretrained model fixed. It is designed to reduce the number of trainable parameters compared with full fine-tuning.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a specific adapter-based method for efficient tuning, while the candidates are either a broader adaptation strategy or a model component, not the same concept.

concepts.name: language model truthfulness
concepts.description: Truthfulness is the degree to which a language model’s responses are factually correct and non-deceptive rather than false or misleading. It reflects how often answers match reality, not merely how plausible they sound, and it is influenced by training data and alignment choices. Higher truthfulness makes models more reliable across diverse contexts and helps reduce accidental errors, which is especially important for safer use in high-stakes settings.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is the general property of a model being factually truthful, while TruthfulQA is a specific benchmark used to measure it, not the same concept.

concepts.name: reference answers
concepts.description: Reference answers are authoritative candidate responses used as evaluation targets for benchmark questions, often in multiple-choice settings where correct and incorrect options may be included. They may be labeled true/false and compared against model outputs by likelihood or other scoring criteria.

concepts.name: multiple-choice task
concepts.description: An evaluation format in which a model selects one option from a fixed set of candidate answers rather than generating free-form text. The model is scored by comparing its chosen answer (or relative preference over candidates) against the correct target. It is commonly used to assess whether the model can identify the best answer among distractors.

concepts.name: math word problems
concepts.description: 以自然语言叙述并包含数值关系的数学题，要求根据题意给出某个数值答案。解题者需要从文字中提取数量关系，建立相应的算术表达并进行推算或计算。

concepts.name: arithmetic reasoning
concepts.description: Reasoning that involves numerical computation and stepwise manipulation of quantities. It includes addition, subtraction, multiplication, division, and multi-step word-problem solving.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a reasoning ability involving arithmetic computation and multi-step manipulation, not the math branch itself or just word problems.

concepts.name: AQuA dataset
concepts.description: AQuA is a multiple-choice benchmark dataset of arithmetic and algebraic word problems. It evaluates mathematical reasoning by requiring models to interpret a textual problem, translate it into algebraic relationships, and select the correct answer from a set of options.
concepts.match_reason: AQuA and ASDiv are different math word-problem datasets with different scopes and benchmarks.

concepts.name: biased text
concepts.description: Biased text is model- or human-generated language that systematically reflects, amplifies, or unbalances associations in ways that are unfair, prejudicial, or skewed toward particular groups or attributes. In language-model outputs, bias can reinforce stereotypes and social inequities learned from training data or model behavior, leading to discriminatory wording or uneven treatment of different entities and distorting information presented to users.

concepts.name: human-written demonstrations
concepts.description: Examples of desired inputs and outputs written by humans to show how a model should respond to prompts. They are used as supervised training data to teach target behavior, providing direct demonstrations the model can imitate during training or adaptation.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is human-created supervised training examples, while the candidate is in-prompt few-shot examples for in-context learning; related but not the same concept.

concepts.name: toxicity
concepts.description: A property of language outputs referring to abusive, offensive, or harmful content. It is commonly measured to assess safety and the likelihood that a model produces socially harmful text.
concepts.match_reason: ‘Toxicity’ is the property/measure of harmful language, while ‘toxic text’ is the harmful text itself; they are related but not the same concept.

concepts.name: Atlas
concepts.description: Atlas is a retrieval-augmented language model designed to learn knowledge-intensive tasks from very few training examples. It combines parametric language modeling with external document retrieval so it can incorporate and update factual knowledge more efficiently than models that store all knowledge in parameters.

concepts.name: KILT
concepts.description: KILT is a benchmark suite for knowledge-intensive language tasks that evaluates systems in knowledge-grounded settings such as retrieval-augmented question answering and fact checking. It emphasizes using external knowledge sources to support responses and measure how well models leverage retrieved facts and evidence to produce correct, well-grounded outputs. The suite also includes tasks like entity linking alongside other related problem types.

concepts.name: cross-attention score
concepts.description: A weight produced by a decoder's cross-attention mechanism that measures how strongly an output token attends to an input token or document. Aggregated across heads, layers, and tokens, these scores can serve as a proxy for the importance of input documents during generation.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is the weight/score produced by cross-attention, while the candidate is the cross-attention mechanism itself.

concepts.name: neural language model
concepts.description: A language model that uses neural networks to learn representations of language and predict text. It generalizes statistical language modeling by learning distributed features from data.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is specifically a neural-network-based language model, while the candidates are either broader language models or different specific model types; none is the same concept.

concepts.name: query-aware contextualization
concepts.description: A prompting or input arrangement strategy that places the query both before and after the supporting documents or key-value pairs. It is used to help a model attend to the query and retrieve relevant information more reliably.

concepts.name: Hallucination benchmarks
concepts.description: Hallucination benchmarks are standardized evaluation tasks designed to measure how often language models produce hallucinated content. They assess generation quality in terms of truthfulness and groundedness, using common metrics so methods for detection and mitigation can be compared reliably.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is an evaluation benchmark/task, while the candidates are a detection method and a quantitative rate, which are different concepts.

concepts.name: Hallucination mitigation
concepts.description: Hallucination mitigation refers to a set of methods used to reduce the frequency or impact of hallucinations in large language model outputs. These methods typically target multiple stages—data, training, and inference—to improve factuality and reliability of the generated text.
concepts.match_reason: Mitigation is about reducing hallucinations, while the candidates are a benchmark, a detection task, and a metric for measuring hallucinations, not the mitigation concept itself.

concepts.name: Knowledge boundaries
concepts.description: 知识边界是模型能够可靠覆盖的知识与可从上下文推断的能力范围，超出该范围时模型更可能出现不确定或错误输出。理解知识边界有助于判断何时需要检索、拒答或进行额外验证。

concepts.name: sensemaking query
concepts.description: A sensemaking query is one that goes beyond narrow fact lookup and instead prompts broad synthesis across a corpus or system output. It is designed to reveal patterns, trends, or relationships that emerge only when information is considered together, supporting understanding and interpretation of the material in retrieval-augmented or similar settings.

concepts.name: map-reduce processing
concepts.description: A two-stage processing pattern in which partial results are first produced independently in parallel and then combined into a final result. It is useful for aggregating information from multiple partitions into a single answer, including scaling summarization by summarizing smaller text units and then combining the intermediate summaries.

concepts.name: LLM-as-a-judge technique
concepts.description: 一种评估方法，让大语言模型充当评判者，依据指定标准对系统的多个生成结果进行判断与比较，并输出优选结果或平局。该过程可重复多次以减弱生成随机性带来的波动，从而提升评估的稳定性。
concepts.match_reason: LLM-as-a-judge is an evaluation method using an LLM to score or compare outputs, while vanilla LM is a basic standard language model baseline; they denote different concepts.

concepts.name: Leiden algorithm
concepts.description: Leiden算法是一种用于图社区检测的划分方法，旨在将图划分为若干内部联系更密集的社区。它在划分过程中通过迭代方式改进社区质量，通常相较于基于模块度的流程能得到更可靠、更稳定的结果。该算法也可进行层次化应用，以在不同尺度上发现子社区。
concepts.match_reason: Leiden is a distinct community detection algorithm that improves on Louvain, but it is not the same concept.

concepts.name: 社区回答
concepts.description: 社区回答是针对用户查询生成的面向查询回答方法，先基于社区摘要的各个片段在“映射”阶段产生中间答案，再通过有用性/相关性对中间答案进行排序与筛选，最后将其组合整合为更广泛的最终全局答案。该做法强调先在社区范围内组织信息并形成中间结果，再参与后续全局生成与提升。
concepts.match_reason: “社区回答”是基于社区摘要生成的面向查询的答案；候选0是社区摘要本身，候选1是全局摘要，都不是同一概念。

concepts.name: 文本图
concepts.description: 文本图是由文本节点及其之间的关系（边）构成的图结构，节点和边都携带自然语言相关的属性或语义信息。它将结构关系与文本内容结合，用于表示文档间的链接、交互或关联，并使图数据能够以语言感知的方式进行处理，从而支持更结构化的信息检索与生成。
concepts.match_reason: “文本图”是由文本节点及其关系构成的图结构；“图索引”是用图结构组织信息的索引方式，属于相关但不同的概念。

concepts.name: graph soft pruning mechanism
concepts.description: A graph pruning method that gradually reduces the influence of less useful nodes and edges, typically by downweighting them rather than deleting them outright. It restricts graph encoding to the most relevant substructure while preserving differentiability, suppressing redundancy and removing only non-critical components. The remaining information stays best aligned with a chosen retrieval objective without exhaustive search.

concepts.name: Topological information
concepts.description: Topological information is the structural information that describes how nodes and edges are connected in a graph. It is essential for reasoning about graph-structured data because it captures relationships that are not present in text alone.

concepts.name: K-hop ego-graph
concepts.description: A K-hop ego-graph is the subgraph induced by the nodes within K hops of a chosen center node. It captures the local neighborhood structure around that node for indexing, retrieval, or downstream analysis.
concepts.match_reason: A K-hop ego-graph is a specific kind of ego-graph, while candidate [1] is the generic notion and [0] is only the set of nodes within K hops, not the subgraph itself.

concepts.name: Hit@1
concepts.description: 一种排序或检索评价指标，表示正确答案是否排在第一位。它常用于衡量模型在候选答案排序中的最高位命中情况。

concepts.name: MiniLM-L12-v2
concepts.description: 一种预训练的轻量级语言模型表示模块，常用于生成句子或段落的向量表示。它在检索与语义匹配任务中经常被用作编码器或嵌入模型。
concepts.match_reason: MiniLM-L12-v2 是一个具体的预训练句向量/嵌入模型实例，而候选 [0] 只是泛指语言表示模型类别，不是同一概念。

concepts.name: high-level knowledge discovery
concepts.description: 从知识源中检索更广泛、更抽象层次的信息的一种检索/发现方式，强调以高阶语义来组织与表示内容。它用于捕捉主题脉络、概念聚合以及更宏观的上下文与层次关系，从而揭示整体结构与更高阶的关联。
concepts.match_reason: This is the opposite level of abstraction from low-level knowledge discovery, not the same concept.

concepts.name: incremental update algorithm
concepts.description: An algorithm that incorporates new data into an existing system without rebuilding everything from scratch. It helps keep retrieval systems current and efficient in changing data environments.

concepts.name: 检索机制
concepts.description: 一种从外部信息源（如语料库、数据库或知识源）中查找并筛选相关文档或片段的检索技术过程。它可在生成之前或生成过程中提供证据，帮助后续系统提升回答的事实性、相关性与文本落地性，尤其在检索增强生成场景中用于增强生成内容的可靠性。
concepts.match_reason: “检索机制”指检索信息的过程/机制本身；候选中只有更具体的检索模块或检索增强生成方法，没有完全等同的概念。

concepts.name: integration
concepts.description: Integration is the stage in a retrieval-augmented generation system where retrieved evidence is combined with the user query or the model’s internal knowledge to form a basis for generation. Its purpose is to synthesize grounded, coherent, and more accurate output by leveraging multiple knowledge sources during downstream reasoning and the final response.

concepts.name: complex query understanding
concepts.description: 复杂查询理解是指对包含多个条件、多个子问题或隐含推理链的查询进行准确解析，并据此给出恰当回答的能力。它不仅依赖表面语义匹配，还需要系统整合多步证据来满足各项约束与推理要求。该能力有助于选择合适的检索与证据组织策略，从而获得相关且可支撑的答案。

concepts.name: knowledge-based GraphRAG
concepts.description: 知识驱动的GraphRAG方法：将非结构化文本转化为显式的知识图（或结构化知识），用节点与边分别表示实体及其语义关系。通过利用实体、关系与语义结构来进行检索与生成，从而实现更精确的知识获取与知识中心化推理。
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a specific knowledge-graph-based variant of GraphRAG, while the candidates are either the broader GraphRAG family or the knowledge graph itself, not the exact same concept.

concepts.name: hybrid GraphRAG
concepts.description: Hybrid GraphRAG是一类结合知识型方法与索引型方法的GraphRAG框架，同时利用显式知识组织与图索引机制来支持检索与推理。它旨在在语义表达能力、检索效率与系统灵活性之间取得平衡，以应对更复杂的推理任务。
concepts.match_reason: “Hybrid GraphRAG” denotes a specific combined approach mixing knowledge-based and index-based methods, which is narrower than the generic GraphRAG variants listed and not the same as any one of them.

concepts.name: recursive splits
concepts.description: A chunking strategy that repeatedly divides text into smaller segments using predefined, often structure-aware rules or limits, continuing until each segment fits a desired size. It is used in retrieval pipelines to create retrieval units that are semantically coherent and easier to index and search.
concepts.match_reason: “Recursive splits” is a specific chunking method, while the listed concepts are either the general chunking notion or unrelated units/strategies; none denotes exactly this technique.

concepts.name: sliding windows
concepts.description: A chunking or segmentation strategy that moves a fixed-size window across a text to create overlapping segments.
