The overlap preserves some local context between neighboring chunks while ensuring each segment remains bounded in size. This can improve downstream tasks such as retrieval by keeping adjacent information accessible across boundaries.

concepts.name: Small-to-Big
concepts.description: A chunking strategy that uses smaller retrieval units while preserving links to larger surrounding context. It is intended to balance fine-grained searchability with broader semantic completeness.
concepts.match_reason: Small-to-Big is a specific retrieval/chunking strategy that uses small units plus larger surrounding context; none of the candidates denote that exact idea, only broader or different chunking methods.

concepts.name: ByteNet
concepts.description: 一种用于序列建模的卷积型架构，能够并行计算所有位置的表示。它通过分层结构在较少的步骤中连接远距离位置，从而减少顺序计算。

concepts.name: ConvS2S
concepts.description: 一种基于卷积神经网络的序列到序列模型，能够并行处理输入和输出位置。它通过卷积堆叠逐步扩大感受野，以建模序列中的依赖关系。
concepts.match_reason: ConvS2S 指的是特定的卷积式序列到序列模型，而候选项中没有该具体模型；[0] 只是另一种并行卷积序列架构，非同一概念。

concepts.name: 解码器
concepts.description: 解码器是编码器-解码器结构中的后半部分，负责根据编码得到的表示逐步生成输出序列。它通常在每一步生成一个符号，并利用先前已经生成的符号作为额外输入。
concepts.match_reason: “解码器”是编码器-解码器结构中的一个组成部分，而候选项只有“编码器”和整个“编码器-解码器结构”，都不是同一概念。

concepts.name: key
concepts.description: A key is a vector representation in an attention mechanism that is compared with a query to assess their similarity. That similarity determines the attention weights used to weight the corresponding values, effectively selecting which information is most relevant. Keys function as address-like representations for retrieving relevant content.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a key vector in attention, while the candidate is a query vector; they are distinct roles that are compared with each other, not the same concept.

concepts.name: value
concepts.description: A value is a vector representation in the attention mechanism that is combined using attention weights to produce the output representation. After computing attention weights from queries and keys, the values are aggregated according to those weights to carry the information used for the result.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is the attention 'value' vector, while the candidate is a 'query' vector; they are different components with different roles in attention.

concepts.name: recurrent layer
concepts.description: A sequence-processing layer that updates its state step by step across the input order. Its computation is inherently sequential, so both the number of sequential operations and the maximum path length grow linearly with sequence length.

concepts.name: NVIDIA P100 GPU
concepts.description: NVIDIA P100 is a graphics processing unit model designed to accelerate parallel computation. It is commonly used as a high-performance accelerator in computing systems, providing high-throughput parallel execution for workloads such as matrix and tensor operations in machine learning training.

concepts.name: base model
concepts.description: A smaller model configuration used as a standard baseline or default variant. Base models typically have fewer parameters and require less computation than larger versions.

concepts.name: big model
concepts.description: A larger model configuration with greater parameter count or capacity than a base version, often implemented in Transformer architectures by increasing model dimensionality, feed-forward width, and the number of attention heads. This increased capacity is designed to improve performance, such as translation quality, typically at higher computational cost and longer training time.
concepts.match_reason: "Big model" refers to a larger, higher-capacity model, while "base model" is the smaller baseline version, so they are contrasting variants, not the same concept.

concepts.name: Label Smoothing
concepts.description: 一种训练时的目标分布平滑技术，通过把原本过于尖锐的硬标签分布变得更平缓，降低模型对单一正确类别的过度自信。它通常会略微恶化困惑度，但常常能提高准确率和 BLEU 分数。

concepts.name: 检查点平均
concepts.description: 检查点平均是一种模型组合技术，通过将多个训练过程中保存的模型检查点参数（通常是各层权重）进行平均，形成单一的参数集合。它用于在推断与评估时稳定预测，往往能提升最终性能与泛化能力，并常在训练后期应用以降低参数更新噪声带来的波动。

concepts.name: attention key dimension
concepts.description: The dimensionality of the key vectors used in an attention head to compute attention scores. It determines the size of the representation used for matching queries to keys, influencing how well the model can align related tokens and affecting computational cost.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is the dimensionality of the key vectors, while candidate 0 is the key vector itself; these are related but not the same concept.

concepts.name: unidirectional language model
concepts.description: 一种只沿单一方向建模词序列条件概率的语言模型，通常只能利用左侧或右侧上下文进行预测。由于上下文信息受限，这类模型在需要同时利用双向语境的任务中往往不如双向模型有效。
concepts.match_reason: 候选中只有通用的“语言模型”，没有明确表示“单向/单向上下文建模”的同一概念。

concepts.name: pre-trained word embeddings
concepts.description: Vector representations of words learned from large amounts of unlabeled text before being used in downstream NLP systems. Pre-training on broad corpora captures distributional semantic information and provides useful lexical knowledge that can be adapted to specific tasks, often improving performance over embeddings learned only from task-specific data.

concepts.name: 双向自注意力
concepts.description: 一种自注意力机制，允许序列中每个标记同时与所有其他标记建立注意力关联，从而同时利用左侧与右侧上下文信息。它用于构建融合了全局输入信息的表示，适合需要整体语义理解的表示学习任务。
concepts.match_reason: “双向自注意力”是注意力机制本身；候选0“**双向表示**”是利用双向上下文得到表示的方法，概念更宽且不等同。

concepts.name: perturbed autoregressive language modeling
concepts.description: A training objective for language models that predicts tokens autoregressively while using perturbations to the input or prediction process. It is a proposed alternative to masked-language-model pretraining for learning strong language representations.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a perturbed variant of autoregressive language modeling, not the standard left-to-right objective described by either candidate.

concepts.name: static masking
concepts.description: A masking strategy in masked language modeling where masked token positions are chosen once during preprocessing and then reused across training epochs. This can cause the model to see the same corruption pattern repeatedly for a given sequence.
concepts.match_reason: Static masking is a masking strategy/variant for MLM, not the MLM objective itself and not the [MASK] placeholder token.

concepts.name: document sentences
concepts.description: An input construction that builds training sequences from blocks of text taken from a single document only, avoiding any crossing of document boundaries. It preserves contiguous sentence structure within each document while assembling model inputs up to a fixed target length. Because examples near the end of a document may be shorter, it can require variable batch sizes or padding behavior to handle these cases.
concepts.match_reason: The candidate is a broader sentence-packing scheme that may cross document boundaries, while the incoming concept specifically samples contiguous sentences within documents.

concepts.name: gradient accumulation
concepts.description: A technique that simulates a larger batch size by accumulating gradients over multiple smaller forward and backward passes before performing an optimization step. It is used when memory limits prevent processing the full batch at once.
concepts.match_reason: Gradient accumulation is a training technique for delaying optimizer updates, while a training batch is the set of examples processed together; they are related but not the same concept.

concepts.name: byte-level BPE
concepts.description: A byte-based variant of byte pair encoding that uses bytes rather than Unicode characters as the base subword units. It can represent any input text without producing unknown tokens while still learning a compact subword vocabulary.
concepts.match_reason: Byte-level BPE is a specific byte-based variant of byte-pair encoding, not the same concept as generic BPE.

concepts.name: 事实知识
concepts.description: 语言模型中与世界事实、实体属性和关系相关的可预测内容。它常以稀有但可记忆的模式出现，并对检索式方法特别有帮助。
concepts.match_reason: “事实知识”指模型中关于世界事实、实体属性和关系的可预测内容；“语言表示模型”是把文本映射为向量的模型本身，二者不是同一概念。

concepts.name: nearest neighbors
concepts.description: Nearest neighbors are stored items in a vector space that are closest to a query representation under a chosen distance metric. They are commonly used to estimate probabilities over candidate outputs by aggregating information from retrieved neighbors and weighting contributions more heavily for items that are closer (more similar) to the query.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is the retrieved closest items themselves, while [0] is the datastore that stores examples and [2] is the kNN method; neither denotes exactly the same concept.

concepts.name: compute-efficient training
concepts.description: 计算高效训练是一种在固定计算预算下最大化模型性能的训练策略。它强调在模型规模、数据量和训练时长之间进行最优分配，以获得更高的最终效果。
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a training strategy for maximizing performance under a fixed compute budget, while the candidates refer to the compute cost itself or the budget constraint, not the strategy.

concepts.name: performance scaling
concepts.description: Performance scaling is the study of how model performance metrics (e.g., loss or accuracy) change as resources increase, such as data size, model size, compute, or training time. It is often characterized empirically by fitting functional relationships between performance and a resource variable, such as power-law trends with respect to parameter count. The goal is to understand whether gains continue smoothly, follow predictable regimes, or eventually saturate.

concepts.name: non-embedding training compute
concepts.description: Non-embedding training compute is the portion of a model’s training computation that excludes embedding-layer operations, such as vocabulary and positional embedding work. It is commonly estimated from factors like model size, batch size, and the number of training steps, and is used to compare training cost across runs or model scales in transformer training.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a specific compute measure excluding embedding-related computation, while candidate 1 is total training compute and candidate 0 is about parameters, not compute.

concepts.name: Universal Transformer
concepts.description: A Universal Transformer is a Transformer variant that reuses the same layer parameters across multiple processing steps, creating an iterative refinement structure rather than a fixed depth stack. By sharing weights across steps (or positions), it reduces the number of distinct parameters and can improve performance for a given parameter budget, sometimes at the cost of increased compute per parameter. It preserves the Transformer’s self-attention while adding recurrence-like processing.
concepts.match_reason: Universal Transformer is a specific Transformer variant with shared parameters across steps, not the general Transformer architecture.

concepts.name: Transformer shape
concepts.description: The configuration of a Transformer model’s structural hyperparameters, such as its depth (number of layers), width (hidden size), number of attention heads, and feed-forward network dimension. These settings determine how model capacity and parameters are distributed across layers and submodules, often rearranging capacity even when the total parameter count remains fixed.
concepts.match_reason: Transformer shape is the model’s hyperparameter configuration, not the Transformer architecture itself.

concepts.name: aspect ratio
concepts.description: Aspect ratio is the ratio between a model’s width (e.g., model dimension/hidden size) and its depth (e.g., number of layers) in architectures such as Transformers. It summarizes the width–depth tradeoff when parameter count is held approximately fixed. Very large or very small values correspond to unusually wide or unusually deep models, which may cause performance to deviate from the expected scaling behavior.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a specific width-to-depth ratio, while the candidates are a feed-forward-to-model ratio and a broader Transformer configuration, so none denote the same concept.

concepts.name: 检索式方法
concepts.description: 检索式方法是一类先从知识源中检索可能相关的文档（或证据），再利用这些检索结果进行条件生成，从而产生答案或预测。它将知识访问与生成过程分成两个阶段，以便在大规模语料或知识库中定位并利用有用信息。适用于需要借助外部知识来增强输出的任务。
concepts.match_reason: “检索式方法”是先检索再生成的整体两阶段系统设计；“检索器”只是其中负责找相关文档的组件，不是同一概念。

concepts.name: marginal probability
concepts.description: The marginal probability (or marginal likelihood) of observed data is obtained by summing or integrating a joint probability over latent or hidden variables. It removes the hidden variables by aggregating over all of their possible values. In models with latent retrieval or selection components, it is commonly used as an objective during training.
concepts.match_reason: Marginal probability is the probability after summing/integrating over latent variables; a latent variable is the hidden variable itself, not that probability.

concepts.name: end-to-end question answering system
concepts.description: An end-to-end question answering system that combines retrieval with answer extraction or generation into a single pipeline. In open-domain QA, a retriever first selects a small set of relevant passages, and a reader then inspects them to produce the answer. This decomposition reduces the search space and improves practicality while its effectiveness depends on both retrieval quality and answer identification.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a full question answering pipeline, while the candidate is only the retrieval component of that pipeline.

concepts.name: 段落编码器
concepts.description: 一种将段落或文档映射到固定维度稠密向量表示的编码网络，常用于检索系统中以快速表示候选文本。它可与问题编码器共同训练，使查询与相关段落在向量空间中具有更高的相似度，从而便于高效的相关性匹配与检索。
concepts.match_reason: “段落编码器”特指把段落编码成向量用于检索的编码网络；候选项分别是问题编码器、Transformer 编码器和泛指的编码器，概念都不完全相同。

concepts.name: Freebase
concepts.description: A structured knowledge base of entities and relations used in question answering and semantic search. It serves as an answer source for datasets that map questions to entities.

concepts.name: decomposable similarity function
concepts.description: 一种可以按维度或组成部分分解计算的相似度函数，便于高效地对表示向量进行比较。它常用于神经检索和匹配模型中，把整体匹配分数建立在局部数值运算之上。
concepts.match_reason: decomposable similarity function is a general class of similarity functions, while 余弦相似度 is only one specific similarity measure.

concepts.name: Fast Decoding
concepts.description: An approximate decoding procedure for retrieval-augmented sequence generation that skips additional forward passes after candidate hypotheses are generated. It treats hypotheses not produced during beam search from a given document as having negligible probability, reducing computation.
concepts.match_reason: Fast Decoding is an approximate retrieval-augmented decoding shortcut, while Thorough Decoding is the more exhaustive alternative; beam search is only a component, not the same concept.

concepts.name: MSMARCO NLG task v2.1
concepts.description: MSMARCO NLG task v2.1 is a knowledge-intensive benchmark for open-domain abstractive question answering and natural language generation. It uses the MS MARCO collection to provide questions along with retrieved passages and annotated full-sentence answers, enabling evaluation of generated responses—typically emphasizing factual correctness and text quality.
concepts.match_reason: MSMARCO NLG task v2.1 is a different benchmark for abstractive answer generation, not NaturalQuestions-Open.

concepts.name: Jeopardy 问题生成
concepts.description: 一种问答生成任务，给定答案线索或相关事实，要求生成符合 Jeopardy 风格的具体、精准问题。由于问题需要与所给实体及证据高度对应，往往要将多条信息整合为单一、可答的题面。
concepts.match_reason: Jeopardy 问题生成是“给定答案生成问题”的特定任务；开放域生成式问答是“根据证据生成答案”的另一类任务，方向不同，不是同一概念。

concepts.name: Q-BLEU-1
concepts.description: 一种用于问句生成的自动评价指标，是 BLEU 的变体，对实体匹配赋予更高权重。它被设计为比标准 BLEU 更能反映人类对问题生成质量的判断。
concepts.match_reason: Q-BLEU-1 is a specialized BLEU variant for question generation with different weighting, not the same metric as standard BLEU.

concepts.name: retrieval supervision
concepts.description: Retrieval supervision is training guidance that explicitly tells a model which retrieved documents or passages are relevant. It is used to improve retrieval components, but some systems are designed to work without it.
concepts.match_reason: Retrieval supervision is training guidance/labels for relevance, while a retriever is the model component that performs retrieval; they are related but not the same concept.

concepts.name: 三位数算术
concepts.description: 三位数算术是指涉及三位数进行加、减、乘等基本运算的算术题类别。它常用于检验或评估模型在进行精确计算与步骤性数值推理（包括符号计算）方面的能力。

concepts.name: on-the-fly reasoning
concepts.description: On-the-fly reasoning is the ability to solve a problem by composing intermediate steps at inference time rather than relying on memorized answers. It is often tested with tasks that require immediate, context-sensitive inference.

concepts.name: unscrambling words
concepts.description: Unscrambling words is a task in which a jumbled sequence of letters must be rearranged into a valid word. It probes a model’s ability to manipulate word structure and infer lexical forms from partial cues.

concepts.name: human evaluators
concepts.description: Human evaluators are people who judge the quality, authenticity, or other properties of model outputs. They are used to assess how well generated text or responses match human-produced counterparts and to evaluate whether outputs are distinguishable from human-written text.

concepts.name: Synthetic news articles
concepts.description: News-style articles generated artificially by a language model or other automated system rather than written by a human reporter. They are used to evaluate how closely machine-generated language can resemble authentic journalism, including whether it can affect human judgments of authenticity. They also support studies of text generation quality and realism.
aliases: Human Quality Assessment of Synthetic News Articles
concepts.match_reason: The candidate is the broader category of news articles, while the incoming concept is specifically synthetic/generated news articles.

concepts.name: 监督训练数据集
concepts.description: 监督训练数据集是包含输入与目标输出配对标注的训练数据集合，用于监督学习以训练模型学习输入到期望结果的映射关系。通过从标注示例中学习，模型能为特定任务建立从输入到输出的对应关系。
concepts.match_reason: “监督训练数据集”指带输入-正确输出标注的通用监督学习训练集；候选“任务特定数据集”只是面向某一具体任务的数据集，范围更窄，不是同一概念。

concepts.name: 虚假相关性
concepts.description: 训练数据中与目标标签呈现表面相关、但并非任务本质原因的偶然特征或模式。模型若依赖这些虚假线索，往往在训练集上获得较好性能，但在数据分布变化或迁移到新场景时容易失效、泛化变差。

concepts.name: out-of-distribution generalization
concepts.description: 模型在训练数据分布之外的样本、环境或任务条件下仍能保持有效表现的能力。它反映模型是否学到了可迁移的规律，而非仅仅记住训练分布中的偶然关联。
concepts.match_reason: Out-of-distribution generalization is the ability to work on data outside the training distribution, while the candidate is the training distribution itself, so they are different concepts.

concepts.name: 单样本学习
concepts.description: 一种上下文学习形式，在提示中只提供一个示例来引导模型执行任务。它考察模型在极少演示条件下的快速适应能力。
concepts.match_reason: “单样本学习”是只用一个示例进行上下文适应的设置；候选[0]是“少样本学习”，包含多个少量示例，不是同一概念。

concepts.name: BERT-based cascade IR pipeline
concepts.description: A multi-stage information retrieval pipeline in which an initial retriever generates candidate documents, followed by a BERT-based reranker or reader that processes the candidates in later stages. The cascade design balances efficiency and accuracy by using fast retrieval to limit the search space and more expensive contextual modeling to refine rankings.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a full multi-stage cascade IR pipeline, while the candidate is only a BERT-based cross-encoder reranking model used within such a pipeline.

concepts.name: continuous representation space
concepts.description: A continuous representation space is a vector space in which texts (or other items such as queries and documents) are embedded as dense numerical representations. In this space, similarity between vectors captures semantic relatedness, so that relevant items are placed near one another while irrelevant items are separated, enabling similarity-based retrieval and other downstream prediction tasks.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is the continuous vector space itself, while candidate 0 is a dense representation in that space and candidate 1 is a mapping function, so none are the same concept.

concepts.name: negative contrastive estimation
concepts.description: Negative contrastive estimation 是一种对比学习目标，通过区分真实样本与噪声（负样本）来学习概率模型或表示。它通常在检索训练中使用负样本进行对比学习，使与目标匹配的正样本与不匹配的负样本在表示空间中拉开差异。
concepts.match_reason: Negative contrastive estimation is a specific objective, not the same as the general contrastive learning paradigm or the ANN negative mining variant.

concepts.name: Hinge loss
concepts.description: A margin-based loss that penalizes cases where a negative example is scored too close to or above a positive example. It is often used to train ranking models to maintain a separation margin.

concepts.name: importance sampling
concepts.description: A sampling technique that corrects for nonuniform selection probabilities by reweighting sampled examples. In optimization, it is used to produce an unbiased estimate of the full gradient under biased sampling.

concepts.name: unbiased estimator
concepts.description: An estimator whose expected value equals the quantity being estimated. In gradient-based optimization, it ensures that reweighted sampled gradients match the full gradient in expectation.

concepts.name: variance reduction
concepts.description: A family of techniques that reduce the variability of stochastic gradient estimates. Lower variance can improve the stability and convergence behavior of optimization algorithms.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a set of techniques for reducing gradient variance, while candidate [0] is a framework for analyzing it and [1] is the variance itself.

concepts.name: Per-instance gradient norm
concepts.description: 单个训练样本（或实例）对应的梯度向量的长度，表示该样本对目标函数与参数更新的影响强弱。梯度范数越大，通常意味着该样本对当前优化步骤的作用越显著，因而常用于分析或刻画样本级学习动态。
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is the gradient norm of a single sample/instance, while candidate 0 is the general gradient norm in optimization; it is broader and not the same concept.

concepts.name: Jensen's inequality
concepts.description: Jensen's inequality 是一种关于凸（或凹）函数与期望的基本不等式，指出对随机变量先作用函数再取期望，与先取期望再作用函数之间存在确定的大小关系：对凸函数有“函数的期望不小于函数在期望处的值”（凹函数则相反）。它常用于在概率与优化中推导上、下界并证明最优性等结果。

concepts.name: Trainer
concepts.description: The component that learns representations by optimizing the retrieval model with negative examples drawn from an approximate nearest neighbor index. It consumes the current index contents to form training signals and updates the model parameters during asynchronous training.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a training component/module, while the candidate is a training scheme/procedure; they are related but not the same concept.

concepts.name: Inferencer
concepts.description: The component that encodes documents in the corpus using a recent checkpoint of the model. After finishing encoding, it refreshes the approximate nearest neighbor index with the newest document representations.

concepts.name: checkpoint
concepts.description: A saved snapshot of model parameters at a particular training step. Checkpoints are used to encode documents with a recent version of the model while training continues on newer parameters.

concepts.name: 难负样本
concepts.description: 难负样本是指对于当前模型而言最容易被误判为相关、因此最具区分性的负样本。它们通常带来更高的训练损失和更有价值的梯度信号，能促进模型收敛并提升区分能力。常见做法是从批内挑选最难负例，作为对比对象进行噪声对比式训练。
concepts.match_reason: “难负样本”指的是更难区分、信息量更高的负样本类型；候选[0]只是泛指负样本，[1]是特定采样策略产生的批内负例，都不等同于该概念。

concepts.name: Rand Neg
concepts.description: 一种负样本构造策略，在训练过程中从语料或候选集合中随机采样与查询不相关的样本作为对比对象，用于对比学习或检索/排序模型训练。通过提供易于区分的非相关样本，促使模型学习区分相关与不相关文档。
concepts.match_reason: “Rand Neg”指随机负采样；候选中没有对应的随机负例策略，0是批内负例，2是近似最近邻负例，3是难负例，1和4都过于泛化。

concepts.name: BM25 Warm Up
concepts.description: 一种训练初始化或预热设置，先使用BM25相关的训练信号或负样本来启动模型训练。它通常用于帮助稠密检索模型在早期获得更稳定、更有效的表示学习。
concepts.match_reason: BM25 Warm Up is a training warm-up/initialization scheme, while BM25 + Rand Neg is a specific mixed negative-sampling strategy; related but not the same concept.

concepts.name: FirstP
concepts.description: A long-document handling strategy that represents a document using only its first 512 tokens. It is a simple truncation-based approach used when a transformer encoder cannot process the full document length.

concepts.name: MaxP
concepts.description: A long-document handling strategy that splits a document into 512-token passages, encodes each passage separately, and combines their scores by taking the maximum. It allows retrieval models to handle long documents while preserving the strongest matching passage signal.
concepts.match_reason: MaxP keeps the maximum score over multiple 512-token passages, while FirstP uses only the first 512 tokens; they are different long-document strategies.

concepts.name: DeepCT
concepts.description: 一种基于深度学习的词项权重分配方法，用神经模型为文档中的词项预测更适合检索的权重。它旨在增强稀疏检索模型，使文档表示更接近与查询相关的词项分布。

concepts.name: paragraph reranking
concepts.description: Paragraph reranking is a retrieval-stage method that reorders candidate paragraphs after an initial retrieval step, using their estimated usefulness for answering a question so the most relevant evidence is ranked higher. It is typically applied to improve downstream question answering performance by increasing recall through placing likely supporting passages near the top of the candidate list.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a specific reranking step for candidate paragraphs, while candidate [0] is the broader general reranking of retrieved documents, not exactly the same concept.

concepts.name: NarrativeQA
concepts.description: A question answering dataset based on narrative sources that often requires answers to be generated rather than copied from an evidence span. It is commonly used to evaluate abstractive question answering models.
concepts.match_reason: NarrativeQA is a narrative-based abstractive QA dataset; CoQA is conversational reading comprehension, and TriviaQA is trivia/open-domain evidence retrieval, so neither denotes the same dataset.

concepts.name: Inverse document frequency
concepts.description: A weighting scheme that gives higher importance to terms that occur in fewer documents. It is used to downweight common words in retrieval and ranking.
concepts.match_reason: Inverse document frequency is only one component of TF-IDF, not the same concept.

concepts.name: text-based explicit memories
concepts.description: 一种以文本形式存储信息并支持直接检索的外部记忆机制，系统在生成回答时可显式查找并使用其中的文本证据。它用于进行外部知识检索，区别于仅依赖参数化模型权重的记忆方式，便于更新、检索与追溯信息来源。
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a text-based variant of explicit memory, while the candidates describe explicit memory in general rather than specifically text-stored memory.

concepts.name: AllReduce
concepts.description: AllReduce is a collective communication operation in distributed computing that aggregates values across devices and then distributes the result back to every participant. It is commonly used in parallel neural network training and inference to synchronize computations across GPUs.

concepts.name: Broadcast
concepts.description: Broadcast is a collective communication operation that sends the same data from one device to many other devices. In distributed neural network systems, it is used to replicate parameters or activations across multiple GPUs.

concepts.name: RoBERTa-large
concepts.description: RoBERTa-large is a large-sized pretrained transformer language model in the RoBERTa family. With substantially more parameters than the base variant, it is commonly used as a strong backbone for downstream tasks. It functions as a pretrained encoder that can be adapted via standard fine-tuning or other parameter-efficient update methods.
concepts.match_reason: RoBERTa-large is the large-sized variant, while the candidates are the base variant or the general RoBERTa family, not the same concept.

concepts.name: BitFit
concepts.description: BitFit is a parameter-efficient fine-tuning method that updates only the bias vectors of a pretrained model while freezing all other parameters. It adapts large models with a very small number of trainable weights, making it a lightweight option for transfer learning.
concepts.match_reason: BitFit is a parameter-efficient method that updates only bias terms, while the candidates describe standard/full fine-tuning that updates all or most parameters.

concepts.name: AdapterL
concepts.description: An efficient adapter design in which the adapter layer is applied only after the MLP module and a LayerNorm. This selective placement reduces overhead while enabling parameter-efficient tuning. It is a later variant within adapter-based parameter-efficient fine-tuning approaches.
concepts.match_reason: AdapterL is a specific selective adapter placement variant, while candidate [1] is the broader Houlsby-style adapter and [0] is only the generic adapter layer concept.

concepts.name: control trivia questions
concepts.description: Control trivia questions are comparison items designed to preserve the surface form of benchmark questions while avoiding the targeted misconceptions they aim to test. They help distinguish whether a model’s errors stem from the question’s phrasing or formatting versus the underlying content and belief susceptibility. Typically, they are created by minimally editing existing questions so they become straightforward trivia or common-sense prompts.

concepts.name: UnifiedQA
concepts.description: A question-answering model family trained to handle many QA formats. It is one of the systems evaluated for average truthfulness in the comparison shown.
concepts.match_reason: UnifiedQA is a QA model family, while the candidates are QA benchmarks/datasets; none denotes the same concept.

concepts.name: 真实性
concepts.description: 一种回答性质，要求回答不去断言错误陈述。它允许拒答、表达不确定性，或给出真实但与问题无关的答案。

concepts.name: 信息性
concepts.description: 一种回答性质，表示回答提供了能够减少问题所引起不确定性的有用信息。它强调答案不仅要真实，还要对提问者有实质帮助。
concepts.match_reason: “信息性”强调回答能减少不确定性、对提问者有帮助；“真实性”只要求不说假话，甚至可与问题无关，因此不是同一概念。

concepts.name: filtered questions
concepts.description: Questions that were tested against a target model and removed if the model consistently answered them correctly under multiple sampled generations. They are intended to preserve only items that remain difficult for the target model.

concepts.name: unfiltered questions
concepts.description: Questions created using the same adversarial experience but not tested against the target model before inclusion. They are kept as part of the benchmark without model-based filtering.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is the opposite of the candidate: unfiltered questions are kept without model-based filtering, while filtered questions are removed based on target-model success.

concepts.name: Truthfulness score
concepts.description: 一种用于衡量答案真实性的评分指标。它等于真实答案的总归一化似然，并在所有真实和错误参考答案之间进行归一化，以反映模型对正确回答的偏好程度。
concepts.match_reason: Incoming concept is a specific evaluation metric based on normalized likelihood over reference answers, while candidate [1] is a general scalar truth-value score and [0]/[2] are a benchmark and an answer property, not the same metric.

concepts.name: non-imitative weakness
concepts.description: A model property that causes falsehoods for reasons not driven by imitation, such as sensitivity to the syntax or style of a question. It leads the model to produce non-imitative falsehoods.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a model weakness/property that causes non-imitative falsehoods, while candidate 1 is the falsehood itself, not the underlying weakness.

concepts.name: commonsense reasoning
concepts.description: Reasoning that relies on everyday knowledge about how the world works. It involves inferring implicit facts, likely outcomes, or plausible relationships that are not stated directly.

concepts.name: symbolic reasoning
concepts.description: Reasoning over symbols, rules, or formal structures rather than direct surface patterns. It often requires applying explicit transformations or logical operations to derive an answer.

concepts.name: SVAMP dataset
concepts.description: SVAMP is a benchmark dataset of math word problems designed to test reasoning under lexical and structural variations. It evaluates how well models generalize across different surface forms and problem organizations while still solving the underlying arithmetic task.

concepts.name: ASDiv dataset
concepts.description: ASDiv is a benchmark dataset of diverse arithmetic word problems. It is used to evaluate mathematical reasoning and generalization across different problem types and linguistic formulations typical of grade-school style questions.
concepts.match_reason: ASDiv and SVAMP are different math word-problem datasets; they are related in purpose but not the same concept.

concepts.name: equation-only prompting
concepts.description: A prompting variant that asks a model to output only a mathematical equation before giving the answer. It is useful when the problem can be directly translated into an equation, but it lacks the natural-language reasoning steps of chain-of-thought prompting.
concepts.match_reason: Equation-only prompting is a distinct prompting style that asks for just an equation before the answer, not standard direct answering and not chain-of-thought reasoning.

concepts.name: LaMDA 137B
concepts.description: A 137-billion-parameter language model variant used for evaluating prompting methods. It is a large pretrained conversational model that can be assessed on reasoning benchmarks.
concepts.match_reason: LaMDA 137B is a specific 137B-parameter variant, while candidate 0 is the broader LaMDA family rather than that exact model.

concepts.name: 语言模型对齐
concepts.description: 使语言模型的行为与人类意图、偏好以及安全约束相一致的过程，核心是确保其输出既有帮助又诚实，并避免误导与有害内容。它通常通过减少有害行为、提升有用性，并防止目标设定不当导致的“奖励黑客”等失败模式来实现。
concepts.match_reason: “语言模型对齐”是更广泛的目标/过程；[2] 只是实现对齐的一种具体方法（基于人类反馈的微调），不完全等同。

concepts.name: supervised learning baseline
concepts.description: 通过监督学习在示范数据上训练得到的策略模型，学习在给定输入下生成期望输出。它以人类期望行为的示范为训练依据，常作为后续优化（如强化学习微调）的初始策略与性能基准。

concepts.name: personally identifiable information
concepts.description: Information that can be used to identify a specific individual, either by itself or when combined with other data. It is often filtered or protected to reduce privacy and security risks.

concepts.name: few-shot prompt
concepts.description: A prompt that includes a small number of example input-output pairs to condition a language model’s behavior. It is used to improve performance on a task without updating the model’s parameters.
concepts.match_reason: Incoming is a specific prompt format with a few examples; none of the candidates denotes exactly that concept (0 is the broader learning paradigm, 3 is prompt in general).

concepts.name: hallucination rate
concepts.description: A measure of how often a model produces information that is not supported by the input or available context. It is used to quantify factual overgeneration in tasks where answers should remain grounded in the source material.
concepts.match_reason: “Hallucination rate” is a metric measuring how often hallucinations occur, while “hallucination” is the underlying phenomenon itself.

concepts.name: labelers
concepts.description: Labelers are people who annotate examples, judgments, or model outputs according to predefined criteria for training and/or evaluation. They may be screened or selected to ensure reliability on the specific annotation tasks they perform, and their judgments can provide supervision for preference learning and alignment methods.

concepts.name: FLAN
concepts.description: 一种将多种自然语言处理任务与对应的自然语言指令组合起来的训练集合，用于训练模型更好地理解并执行指令式提示，提升其跨任务的指令跟随能力。
concepts.match_reason: FLAN 是把多种 NLP 任务与自然语言指令结合起来的指令调优任务集合，候选中没有同一概念；[0] 只是指令，[1] 是泛指 NLP 任务，[2] 是评测基准 SuperGLUE。

concepts.name: trigger phrase
concepts.description: A trigger phrase is a specially chosen phrase used to elicit or probe particular model behavior. In data filtering, trigger phrases can be used to identify documents that a language model is especially likely to reproduce.

concepts.name: MMLU
concepts.description: MMLU is a benchmark of multitask multiple-choice question answering covering a broad range of subjects and difficulty levels. It is used to evaluate a model's general knowledge and reasoning across many domains.

concepts.name: Perplexity Distillation
concepts.description: Perplexity Distillation 是一种用于训练检索器的损失目标，通过考察在删除单个已检索文档后语言模型输出对数概率（或困惑度）如何变化来构造监督信号。它将移除每个文档后输出对数概率的下降幅度作为文档相关性分数，经 Softmax 形成文档后验分布，并最小化该分布与检索器预测文档分布之间的 KL 散度，从而促使检索器学会选择能显著降低模型困惑度的文档。
concepts.match_reason: Perplexity Distillation is a specific training loss/KL objective for retrievers, while latent knowledge retriever is a broader retriever model trained with latent-document feedback.

concepts.name: Query-side fine-tuning
concepts.description: A retrieval training strategy that fine-tunes only the query encoder (or query representation side) while keeping the document encoder fixed after indexing. Since document embeddings remain unchanged, the existing document index need not be rebuilt immediately, avoiding extra indexing or refresh computation overhead.
concepts.match_reason: Query-side fine-tuning updates only the query encoder and keeps document embeddings fixed, while the candidates are either broad dense retrieval training or the opposite operation of re-embedding documents.

concepts.name: utilization
concepts.description: The use of a trained large language model to carry out downstream applications and user-facing tasks. It covers how the model is applied after development, including practical deployment and task execution.

concepts.name: capacity evaluation
concepts.description: Capability evaluation is the assessment of what a language model can do, including its abilities, limitations, and performance across tasks. It is conducted using benchmarks, experiments, and other empirical tests to measure competence and compare different systems, clarifying what the model can and cannot achieve.
concepts.match_reason: Capacity evaluation is about assessing a model’s capabilities and performance, while utilization is about using the model in applications after development.

concepts.name: 神经语言模型
concepts.description: 神经语言模型是一种用神经网络估计词序列概率的语言模型，通常通过学习分布式表示与非线性映射来进行词预测。它将语言建模与表示学习结合，使模型能够从数据中自动提取特征并支持端到端训练，从而为上下文表示提升及下游任务迁移提供基础。
concepts.match_reason: “神经语言模型”特指基于神经网络的语言模型；候选中没有完全对应的同义项，且“语言模型”/“language models”都更宽泛，包含统计模型。

concepts.name: Word2vec
concepts.description: Word2vec is a method for learning dense word representations from text. It produces static word embeddings that capture semantic regularities from distributional context.
concepts.match_reason: Word2vec is a specific method for learning embeddings, while word embeddings are the resulting representations, so they are related but not the same concept.

concepts.name: GPT-4
concepts.description: GPT-4 is a large language model designed for high-capacity text generation and reasoning-style language tasks. It builds on scaling language models to support a broad set of real-world applications.
concepts.match_reason: GPT-4 is a distinct model from GPT-1/2/3 and not the same as the GPT-3 architecture.

concepts.name: task-agnostic feature learner
concepts.description: 任务无关的特征学习器是一种不针对单一下游任务专门设计的通用表示学习模型，能够学习可复用的特征表示。其目的在于减少为每个任务分别进行特征工程的需求，并将学到的表示迁移到多种应用中使用。

concepts.name: downstream task
concepts.description: 下游任务是指在预训练或通用表示学习之后，模型被用于解决的具体应用任务，通常依赖已有表示，并通过微调或提示等方式进行适配。此类任务的表现常被用来评估模型的实际可用性与泛化能力。
concepts.match_reason: “Downstream task” is the specific application problem after pretraining, while candidate 4 describes the utilization/application of a trained model on such tasks, not the task itself.

concepts.name: artificial general intelligence
concepts.description: 一种具备广泛通用认知能力的人工智能形态，能够在多种任务和环境中灵活表现。它通常被视为超越单一任务专用系统的长期目标。

concepts.name: irreducible loss
concepts.description: The portion of prediction loss that cannot be removed even by an ideal model because it reflects the intrinsic uncertainty of the true data-generating process. It is identified with the entropy of the true data distribution.

concepts.name: Chinchilla scaling law
concepts.description: An empirical scaling law for language model training that relates loss to model size and data size, with coefficients fitted from experiments. It is used to derive compute-optimal allocations of training resources between parameters and tokens.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is the specific Chinchilla scaling law, while candidate [2] is a broader class of scaling laws for language models and candidate [0] is the Chinchilla model itself, not the law.

concepts.name: compute-optimal scaling law
concepts.description: 用于分析在固定计算预算下如何在模型规模与数据规模之间分配资源的缩放规律。它通常给出当可用计算变化时，最优模型规模与数据规模之间的幂律关系，从而最大化训练效率或最终性能。一般而言，预算更高时会更偏向将计算分配给模型参数规模而非数据规模。
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a general compute-optimal scaling law, while candidate 2 is a specific instance (the Chinchilla scaling law), not the same concept.

concepts.name: data-constrained regime
concepts.description: 一种训练环境，其中可用于训练的数据量成为主要限制因素。该情形下，模型规模继续扩大时，数据不足会显著影响缩放效果。
concepts.match_reason: “Data-constrained regime” is a training setting where limited data is the bottleneck, while “dataset size” is just the quantity of data itself, not the regime.

concepts.name: data repetition
concepts.description: 在训练中重复使用已有数据样本的做法。它常用于缓解数据不足，但也可能改变模型对数据分布的学习方式。

concepts.name: phase transition
concepts.description: A phase transition is a sudden change in a system’s behavior when a control variable crosses a critical point. In machine learning, it is used by analogy to describe abrupt appearances of new capabilities as model scale increases.

concepts.name: lost in the middle effect
concepts.description: 长上下文性能现象：当与任务相关的信息出现在输入上下文的中间位置时，模型的表现更容易下降。模型往往更能利用开头或结尾附近的信息，但对既不靠近开端也不靠近末端的关键信息获取与利用不足，因而中部信息处理效果更差。

concepts.name: answer-containing document
concepts.description: A document in a multi-document question answering input that contains the information needed to answer the question. The model must identify and use this document’s contents, and its position within the input context may vary while evaluating performance.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is the specific document that contains the answer, not the overall multi-document QA task or method.

concepts.name: distractor document
concepts.description: A distractor document is a document or passage included in a multi-document retrieval or question-answering input that does not contain the target answer. It is used to make retrieval and answer selection more difficult by adding irrelevant or misleading competing context.
concepts.match_reason: A distractor document is the irrelevant/non-answer document, which is the opposite of an answer-containing document.

concepts.name: GPT-3.5-Turbo
concepts.description: A closed language model accessed through the OpenAI API. It is used as a chat-oriented model for generating responses to prompts.
concepts.match_reason: GPT-3.5-Turbo is a specific OpenAI chat model; none of the candidates denote that exact model, and ChatGPT is the product/interface rather than the model itself.

concepts.name: oracle setting
concepts.description: An evaluation setting in which the model is supplied with the relevant evidence needed to answer the question. It is used to estimate performance when the necessary information is available without retrieval or search difficulty.
concepts.match_reason: Oracle setting gives the model the needed evidence, while closed-book setting forbids external evidence/retrieval; they are opposite evaluation settings.

concepts.name: Hallucination detection
concepts.description: Hallucination detection is the process of identifying whether generated text contains unsupported or nonfactual content. It aims to improve the trustworthiness of model outputs by flagging statements that are inconsistent with evidence or known facts.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a detection process, while candidate [0] is a metric and [1] is the hallucination phenomenon itself, not the act of detecting it.

concepts.name: Large vision-language models
concepts.description: Large vision-language models are multimodal large models that jointly process visual and textual information. They combine image and text to support both understanding and generation conditioned on visual inputs, but may exhibit cross-modal alignment issues such as hallucinations.
concepts.match_reason: The candidate is the general concept of a vision-language model, while the incoming concept specifically refers to large vision-language models, which is a narrower variant.

concepts.name: Factuality
concepts.description: Factuality is the degree to which generated content matches verifiable facts. In language generation, it is a key quality dimension because fluent text can still be wrong if it is not factually grounded.

concepts.name: Faithfulness
concepts.description: Faithfulness is the degree to which generated content is supported by the provided source information or evidence. It is commonly used to assess whether model outputs stay aligned with the input context or retrieved documents.

concepts.name: intrinsic hallucination
concepts.description: Intrinsic hallucination is a type of hallucination in which generated output contradicts the source content. It occurs when the model produces content that is incompatible with the information it was given.

concepts.name: extrinsic hallucination
concepts.description: Extrinsic hallucination is a type of hallucination in which generated output cannot be verified from the source content. It occurs when the model introduces information that is not supported by the provided evidence.
concepts.match_reason: Extrinsic hallucination is unsupported by the source, while intrinsic hallucination contradicts the source; they are different subtypes, not the same concept.

concepts.name: instruction inconsistency
concepts.description: Instruction inconsistency is a form of faithfulness hallucination in which the generated content deviates from the user’s original instruction. It occurs when the output fails to follow requested constraints, goals, or directions.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a specific subtype of faithfulness hallucination, while the candidate is the broader category, so they are not the same concept.

concepts.name: context inconsistency
concepts.description: Context inconsistency is a form of faithfulness hallucination in which the generated content conflicts with the provided context. It occurs when the output does not align with information that should be grounded in the preceding input or surrounding materials.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is specifically about conflicts with provided context, while the candidates are either a different subtype (instruction inconsistency) or a broader umbrella concept (faithfulness hallucination).

concepts.name: logical inconsistency
concepts.description: Logical inconsistency is a form of faithfulness hallucination in which the generated content contains internal contradictions. It occurs when different parts of the output cannot all be true at the same time or do not fit together coherently.
concepts.match_reason: Logical inconsistency is a specific internal-contradiction type, while the listed candidates are broader or different faithfulness error types; none is the same concept.

concepts.name: Hallucinations in LLMs
concepts.description: Hallucinations in large language models are generated texts that are factually incorrect, unfaithful, or otherwise not grounded in the provided context or user intent. The outputs often appear plausible, yet they are unsupported or inconsistent with reliable information. Hallucinations can stem from multiple underlying causes and are studied via distinct categories.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is about hallucinated/unsupported outputs from LLMs, while the candidates are model families, not that phenomenon.

concepts.name: Hallucination Detection Benchmarks
concepts.description: 幻觉检测基准（Hallucination detection benchmarks）是一类用于评估大语言模型是否产生幻觉的标准化测试集合。它们通常包含带标注的输入输出样本，用以衡量模型回答的事实一致性、真实性，以及其可检测的幻觉倾向，从而便于对不同检测方法进行对比评估。
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is about benchmark datasets for evaluating detection systems, while the candidates are either the detection task itself or broader hallucination benchmarks, not the same concept.

concepts.name: 解码阶段事实性增强
concepts.description: 一种在推理解码/生成过程中提升输出事实性与对输入内容忠实程度的方法。通过对候选词或候选序列进行约束、重排序或调整生成策略，使生成结果更贴合源信息，从而降低与输入不一致或无依据内容的出现。

concepts.name: 自回归预测
concepts.description: 自回归预测是一种序列建模方式，模型基于前文的词元来逐步预测下一个词元，并将生成结果不断作为后续预测的上下文。它把序列生成建模为从历史到当前的条件概率估计，常用于大语言模型的预训练学习目标。
concepts.match_reason: “自回归预测”指的是一种训练/生成目标；候选项中没有完全对应的同名目标，且 [0]/[1]/[2]/[3] 都是模型或更宽泛的自回归建模方式，不是同一概念。

concepts.name: 事实矛盾
concepts.description: 事实矛盾是指语言模型输出的内容与已知事实存在直接冲突，表现为可被现实信息识别的事实错误。它可同时体现为表面上与外界信息相“可支持”，但内容内部彼此矛盾或整体与真实情况不一致，从而导致回答在客观上错误。
concepts.match_reason: “事实矛盾”指回答与真实事实直接冲突；候选项中 TruthfulQA 是基准而非该现象，imitative falsehood 也只是相关的虚假生成方式，不是同一概念。

concepts.name: Relation-error hallucination
concepts.description: 一种事实性幻觉，表现为模型在实体之间给出了错误的关系。它不是把对象本身说错，而是把对象之间的联系、归属或因果关系说错。
concepts.match_reason: Incoming concept is a specific subtype (wrong relations between entities), while candidate 1 is the broader factuality hallucination category.

concepts.name: overclaim hallucination
concepts.description: A subtype of factual fabrication in which a model states a claim more strongly or broadly than the available evidence supports. It involves overstating certainty, scope, or entitlement beyond what can be justified.
concepts.match_reason: “Overclaim hallucination” is a narrower subtype about overstating certainty/scope, while “factuality hallucination” is the broader mismatch with facts or evidence, so they are not the same concept.

concepts.name: adaptive benchmarking
concepts.description: A benchmarking approach that dynamically generates evaluation tasks tailored to a specific domain, use case, or target system. It aims to produce more relevant and diverse evaluations than fixed benchmark sets.
concepts.match_reason: Adaptive benchmarking is a specific dynamic benchmark-generation approach, while the candidate is the general concept of a benchmark.

concepts.name: LLM evaluator
concepts.description: An LLM evaluator is a language model used as an automated judge to compare, score, or rank candidate text or system outputs. It assesses responses against defined evaluation criteria and can approximate human judgments, especially when human evaluation or gold labels are limited.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a language model used as an evaluator, while candidate [0] names the evaluation technique itself rather than the model; the others are unrelated.

concepts.name: 全局回答
concepts.description: 对用户查询给出的最终综合回答，通常由多个社区层级的摘要与中间答案逐步汇总而成。其目的是覆盖全局范围内与问题相关的信息，并在结构化表达中形成统一、可解释的结论。
concepts.match_reason: “全局回答”是针对整个查询生成的最终答案，而候选项分别是社区层面的回答/摘要或全局摘要，不是同一概念。

concepts.name: root-level community summaries
concepts.description: Summaries of the highest-level (topmost) communities in a graph-based hierarchy. They condense the broadest clusters into a small number of general descriptions, typically retaining the least detailed but most wide-ranging information for use in downstream retrieval or question answering.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is about summaries of the top/root communities, while candidate 0 is a general community summary, not specifically root-level; 1 is the communities themselves, and 2 is the lowest-level leaf communities.

concepts.name: text view
concepts.description: A representation of graph data that emphasizes the textual content attached to nodes, edges, or substructures. It allows a language model to process the graph as text while preserving the retrieved semantic information.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a graph text representation/view, while the candidate is a retrieval process for selecting a subgraph and its text; related but not the same concept.

concepts.name: hard prompts
concepts.description: Hard prompts are prompt inputs expressed as explicit, discrete text tokens rather than learned continuous vectors. They convey information to a language model directly in natural-language form and can be used to encode structured information, such as graph structure, through generated descriptions.
concepts.match_reason: Neither candidate denotes hard prompts specifically; [0] is the general prompt concept and [1] is a data-collection prompt type, not ordinary-text hard prompts.

concepts.name: multi-hop graph reasoning
concepts.description: 一种需要沿着图中多步连接路径逐步组合证据的推理任务。它要求跨越多个关系或节点传播并整合信息以得到最终结论，而不仅依赖单一局部信息。
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is specifically reasoning over graph links/edges, while candidate [0] is the broader general notion of multi-hop reasoning, not necessarily graph-based.

concepts.name: optimal subgraph
concepts.description: An optimal subgraph is a selected portion of a larger graph that retains the structural information most relevant to a given task or query. It is used as a compact intermediate representation that can be encoded and then provided to another model for downstream prediction or generation.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is the chosen best subgraph itself, while the candidates describe retrieval/search processes rather than that selected subgraph.

concepts.name: 图嵌入
concepts.description: 图嵌入是将图结构数据（节点、边及整体拓扑）映射为固定维度的向量表示，以保留其结构与可用信息。通过这种表示，图的信息被转化为可直接输入模型计算的形式（如神经网络），从而便于建模与比较。
concepts.match_reason: 图嵌入是“图的向量表示”，而候选0是图视图/图结构表示，候选1是一般的嵌入函数，概念都不完全相同。

concepts.name: Graph Neural Network
concepts.description: A neural network architecture that computes representations for graph-structured data by passing messages along edges. It encodes topology through iterative aggregation of information from neighboring nodes and edges.
concepts.match_reason: A graph neural network is a specific message-passing neural architecture; graph encoders is a broader category that can include GNNs but is not the same concept.

concepts.name: Recall
concepts.description: 一种评价指标，衡量系统找回的相关结果占全部相关结果的比例。它用于评估模型覆盖真实答案或目标实例的能力。
concepts.match_reason: “Recall” is a general evaluation metric, while candidate [0] is a specific top-100 retrieval variant of recall; the others are different metrics.

concepts.name: LaBSE
concepts.description: 一种跨语言句子嵌入模型，用于把不同语言的句子映射到共享向量空间。它常用于语义检索和跨语言匹配任务，以便检索语义相近的文本。
concepts.match_reason: LaBSE 是特定的跨语言句子嵌入模型；候选项是更一般的语言表示模型/嵌入空间，不是同一概念。

concepts.name: mContriever
concepts.description: mContriever 是一种用于密集检索的对比学习编码器，旨在将查询和文档表示到共享的嵌入空间中，从而学习二者的语义相似性以支持检索。它能够在跨语言场景下发现语义相关文本，尤其在缺乏显式词项匹配时也能找到相关内容。
concepts.match_reason: mContriever通常指Contriever的多语言/变体版本，不是与候选0完全同一概念；其余候选更泛或不同。

concepts.name: flat data representation
concepts.description: A data organization that stores information in a non-hierarchical, non-relational form. Such representations can make it difficult to preserve structure and interdependencies among pieces of information.

concepts.name: text indexing
concepts.description: The process of organizing textual content so it can be searched and retrieved efficiently. Indexing often prepares documents or chunks for downstream retrieval systems.
concepts.match_reason: Text indexing is the process of preparing text for retrieval, while a search index is the resulting retrieval data structure; related but not the same concept.

concepts.name: 双层检索框架
concepts.description: 双层检索框架是一种将检索过程分为两个层次的框架，用于同时处理面向细节的查询与面向主题的查询。它在不同抽象层级组织检索：一层用于返回具体实体的精确信息，另一层用于汇总跨多个相关实体的高层语义。通过兼顾细粒度与高层次检索，提升回答的全面性和相关性。
concepts.match_reason: “双层检索框架”是一个同时做局部与全局检索的两阶段/双层检索思路；候选项中没有同一框架，0更像层次化索引结构，1是图增强RAG系统，2只是全局查询。

concepts.name: deduplication
concepts.description: Deduplication is the process of detecting and removing duplicate or near-duplicate representations of the same entity or relation across data segments, then merging or consolidating them. It reduces redundancy in the underlying index or graph, improving consistency and the efficiency of subsequent operations such as retrieval and graph processing.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is general deduplication, while the candidate is a specific variant for fuzzy/near-duplicate removal, so they are not the same concept.

concepts.name: entity
concepts.description: A distinct, nameable object or concept represented in a knowledge graph or retrieval system. Entities are used as retrieval targets when systems aim to fetch structured knowledge rather than raw text.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is the general notion of an entity, while the candidates are a specific graph type, a paired component set, a subset of entities, or the extraction process.

concepts.name: Overall
concepts.description: 一种综合评价维度，用于汇总前面多个维度的结果，以确定最终更优的答案。它反映的是跨维度的总体表现，而不是单一方面的优势。
concepts.match_reason: “Overall”指的是用于汇总各维度表现的综合评价维度；“全局回答”是对用户问题的最终综合答案，二者是评估标准与答案结果，不是同一概念。

concepts.name: diversity metric
concepts.description: An evaluation measure that reflects how varied or wide-ranging system responses are. Higher diversity indicates that the system produces a broader spread of outputs rather than repetitive answers.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a metric/measure of diversity, while the candidate is the diversity property itself, not the same concept.

concepts.name: 生成模块
concepts.description: 生成模块是检索增强系统中的组件，负责基于输入上下文与检索到的证据生成自然语言输出。它将选取到的信息转化为回应，力求使生成内容连贯且更有依据。但由于生成过程并非总是严格受证据约束，可能存在对检索事实使用不准确或不一致的情况。
concepts.match_reason: “生成模块”指的是系统中的一个生成组件；候选项要么是整体方法/模型，要么是任务类型，并不等同于该组件。

concepts.name: Query expansion
concepts.description: A retrieval technique that augments an initial query with additional terms or reformulations to improve document matching. It is used to increase the chance of retrieving relevant information, especially for ambiguous or underspecified queries.
concepts.match_reason: RQ-RAG is a specific retrieval-augmented generation method that rewrites/decomposes queries into sub-queries, not the general concept of query expansion.

concepts.name: Contextual disambiguation
concepts.description: A technique for using surrounding context to resolve ambiguity in a query or term. It helps retrieval or understanding systems distinguish between multiple possible meanings and select more relevant evidence.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is specifically using surrounding context to disambiguate, while candidate 0 is the broader disambiguation process without the contextual emphasis; the others are different concepts.

concepts.name: METRAG
concepts.description: METRAG is a multi-layer retrieval-augmented generation framework that improves final generation by inserting intermediate thought-like representations between retrieval and output. It combines document similarity with utility signals, using summarization to condense the intermediate reasoning content. The approach aims to organize evidence, strengthen reasoning, and produce more informed, utility-oriented responses.
concepts.match_reason: METRAG is a specific multi-layered/utility-guided RAG framework, not the same as the generic RAG variants or benchmark listed here.

concepts.name: Meta Knowledge Summary
concepts.description: A summary representation produced for clusters of related documents to capture shared higher-level information. It is used to improve retrieval-augmented generation by giving the system compact metadata-style knowledge about a document group.

concepts.name: CommunityKG-RAG
concepts.description: A zero-shot retrieval-augmented generation framework that uses community knowledge graphs to support generation. It is presented as a recent method for improving retrieval and grounding without task-specific training.
concepts.match_reason: CommunityKG-RAG is a specific zero-shot RAG method using community knowledge graphs; none of the candidates denotes that exact framework, and GraphRAG/LightRAG are different graph-based RAG variants.

concepts.name: LA-RAG
concepts.description: A retrieval-augmented generation paradigm developed to improve automatic speech recognition within large language models. It incorporates external retrieved information to support more accurate transcription and recognition outcomes.
concepts.match_reason: LA-RAG is an ASR-focused retrieval-augmented generation paradigm, while all candidates are general text-oriented RAG variants with different mechanisms.

concepts.name: automatic speech recognition
concepts.description: A technology that converts spoken language into written text. It is used to transcribe audio by modeling acoustic and linguistic patterns from speech input.

concepts.name: domain-specific knowledge
concepts.description: Domain-specific knowledge is specialized information about a particular field or subject area, including relevant facts, relationships, and conventions. It is often too specialized or scattered to be captured reliably by general-purpose methods alone, so it may be added or supplied to improve performance on tasks in that domain.

concepts.name: semantic units
concepts.description: Semantic units are coherent pieces of meaning in text—such as a phrase, sentence, or paragraph—that function as a whole. Preserving them helps maintain interpretability and continuity, supporting logical flow and accurate understanding when text is processed or summarized.
concepts.match_reason: “Semantic units” are coherent language chunks, while “semantic information” is meaning-related content in language; related, but not the same concept.

concepts.name: parent-child relationships
concepts.description: A hierarchical organization pattern that arranges information into levels of abstraction, where broader concepts contain or group more specific ones as child nodes. It provides a structured alternative to flat lists by supporting clearer navigation and improving retrieval by linking higher-level summaries to underlying details.

concepts.name: post-retrieval process
concepts.description: The set of operations performed after relevant content has been retrieved but before final output. It typically filters, reranks, or restructures the retrieved information, often incorporating metadata, to make it more useful for downstream generation by a language model.
concepts.match_reason: Post-retrieval process is a broader after-retrieval pipeline (e.g., filtering, reranking, metadata use), while re-ranking is only one specific step, not the whole concept.

concepts.name: 多样上下文生成
concepts.description: 一种在检索前通过生成多种多样相关上下文来扩展查询语义的方法。它将原始查询在不同方面进行补充，从而提升后续检索的召回与准确性，并可用于增强基于如BM25的检索效果。
concepts.match_reason: 该概念是“检索前通过生成多个相关上下文来扩展查询”的查询扩展方法，候选中没有同一概念；RAG/检索机制都只是相关但不同。

concepts.name: 混合检索方法
concepts.description: 混合检索方法是一种在同一检索系统中结合多种检索技术的策略，常见做法是把不同类型的检索方法（如图结构与文本/语义检索等）相互补充以提升效果。它通常通过先快速缩小候选集、再进行更精确的重排序来兼顾检索效率与结果质量，从而提升召回率、精确度与鲁棒性。
concepts.match_reason: “混合检索方法”指结合多种检索技术的整体策略，而候选中只有重排序或近似索引等单一环节，没有完全对应的同一概念。

concepts.name: Retrieval Strategy
concepts.description: The overall scheme used to organize and conduct retrieval in a system. It determines how queries are processed, how candidate evidence is selected, and how retrieved information is assembled for downstream use.
concepts.match_reason: “Retrieval Strategy” is a higher-level overall scheme, while the candidates are specific system types or techniques; none denotes the same concept exactly.

concepts.name: 卷积神经网络
concepts.description: 一种利用局部卷积运算提取特征的神经网络架构。它擅长利用局部结构信息，但在序列建模中通常需要额外机制来处理长距离依赖。

concepts.name: 缩放点积注意力
concepts.description: 一种注意力计算方式，先对查询和键做点积，再用缩放因子调节数值大小以稳定训练。它是许多现代注意力模型中的基础计算单元。

concepts.name: 英语成分句法分析
concepts.description: 一种句法分析任务，目标是把英语句子解析成短语结构树，标出句子的成分层次关系。它常用于测试模型对结构化语言信息的建模能力。

concepts.name: 模型集成
concepts.description: 一种将多个模型的预测结果组合起来以提升整体性能的方法。它通常比单个模型更稳健，但计算成本也更高。

concepts.name: Tensor2Tensor
concepts.description: An open-source library for deep learning research that provides reusable implementations of models, data pipelines, and training utilities. It was widely used to build, test, and compare sequence-modeling systems and related neural network variants.

concepts.name: 门控循环神经网络
concepts.description: 一种循环神经网络结构，利用门控机制控制信息在时间上的保留与更新。它通过更灵活地调节状态传播，增强了对长距离依赖的建模能力。
concepts.match_reason: “门控循环神经网络”通常指 GRU，而候选0是长短期记忆网络（LSTM）；二者都是门控RNN但不是同一概念。

concepts.name: 序列建模
concepts.description: 一种机器学习任务，目标是对按顺序排列的符号、事件或观测进行建模，并捕捉其中的依赖关系。它广泛用于语言、翻译和其他需要处理序列输入输出的场景。

concepts.name: 转导问题
concepts.description: 一类将输入序列映射为输出序列的任务，输出长度可以与输入长度不同。其核心在于学习输入与输出之间的对齐和依赖关系。

concepts.name: 并行化
concepts.description: 一种将计算分配到多个位置、样本或设备上同时执行的方法，以提高训练或推理效率。对于序列模型而言，并行化通常受限于按时间步递归计算的结构。

concepts.name: Extended Neural GPU
concepts.description: 一种通过卷积式计算并行处理序列位置的神经网络模型，旨在减少序列计算中的顺序依赖。它属于试图降低长序列处理开销的一类架构。
concepts.match_reason: Extended Neural GPU 是一种特定的序列并行神经架构，不等同于普通卷积神经网络或循环神经网络；候选中没有同一概念。

concepts.name: 端到端记忆网络
concepts.description: 一种基于递归注意力机制的神经网络结构。它通过多轮注意和记忆交互来处理输入，曾在简单语言问答和语言建模任务中表现良好。
concepts.match_reason: “端到端记忆网络”是基于多轮注意和记忆交互的特定架构，不是LSTM，也不是Transformer。

concepts.name: 自回归
concepts.description: 一种逐步生成的建模方式，其中当前输出会把先前已经生成的输出作为额外输入。它适用于需要按顺序预测后续元素的序列生成任务。

concepts.name: 多头自注意力机制
concepts.description: 一种注意力机制，它把输入表示映射到多个并行的注意力头，再将各头结果合并。它能够让模型从不同表示子空间中同时捕捉序列内部的依赖关系。
concepts.match_reason: “多头自注意力机制”是“多头注意力”的一种具体变体（自注意力而非一般注意力），候选中没有完全同一概念。

concepts.name: 逐位置前馈网络
concepts.description: 一种对序列中每个位置独立应用的全连接前馈网络。它不在位置之间共享计算路径，主要用于对每个位置的表示进行非线性变换。

concepts.name: 残差连接
concepts.description: 一种将层输入直接与层输出相加的结构，用于改善深层网络的梯度传播与训练稳定性。它允许子层在保留原始信息的同时学习增量式变换。

concepts.name: 层归一化
concepts.description: 一种对单个样本的特征维度进行归一化的技术，用于稳定神经网络训练。它通常与残差连接配合使用，以控制激活尺度并加速优化。
concepts.match_reason: 层归一化是对特征进行归一化的技术；候选项“残差连接”是把输入与输出相加的网络结构，概念不同。

concepts.name: 查询
concepts.description: 注意力机制中的向量，用于表示当前需要检索的信息。它与键进行匹配，以决定应从哪些值中聚合信息。
concepts.match_reason: “查询”是注意力机制中的一个向量/组成部分，不是注意力机制本身；候选项都不是这个具体概念。

concepts.name: Dot-Product Attention
concepts.description: 一种以查询和键之间的点积作为兼容性函数的注意力机制，用于计算每个值的权重。它的计算通常可以通过高效的矩阵乘法实现，因此在实践中具有较好的速度和空间效率。
concepts.match_reason: Incoming concept is generic dot-product attention, while candidates 0 and 2 are the scaled variant; candidate 1 is broader and 3 is different.

concepts.name: Additive Attention
concepts.description: 一种注意力机制，通过带单个隐藏层的前馈网络来计算查询与键之间的兼容性函数，从而为各个值分配权重。它在小维度情况下通常与点积注意力表现相近，但在较大维度下往往更稳定。
concepts.match_reason: Additive attention is a specific attention scoring function (feedforward-based/Bahdanau), and none of the candidates names that same concept.

concepts.name: attention function
concepts.description: The attention function computes weighted combinations of values based on the compatibility between queries and keys. It allows a model to focus on the most relevant parts of its input when producing an output.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is the generic attention function, while the candidates are specific components or variants of attention (queries/values, additive, dot-product, scaled dot-product), not the same concept.

concepts.name: variance
concepts.description: Variance is a measure of how widely a random variable’s values are spread around its mean. In probability and statistics, it quantifies the expected squared deviation from the average value.

concepts.name: independent random variable
concepts.description: An independent random variable is a random variable whose outcomes are not influenced by the outcomes of another random variable. Independence allows the joint behavior of variables to be analyzed as the product of their separate behaviors.

concepts.name: auto-regressive property
concepts.description: A property of sequence generation in which each position may depend only on earlier positions. It is preserved by preventing a decoder position from attending to future positions.

concepts.name: Linear Transformation
concepts.description: A linear transformation maps an input vector through a learned weight matrix, often with an added bias term. In neural networks, it is used to change the representation of features without introducing nonlinearity by itself.
concepts.match_reason: A linear transformation is broader, while a linear projection is a specific kind of linear map used for dimensional change; they are not the same concept.

concepts.name: ReLU Activation
concepts.description: ReLU, or rectified linear unit, is a nonlinear activation function that outputs the input when it is positive and zero otherwise. It is commonly used to introduce nonlinearity between linear layers in neural networks.

concepts.name: Convolution with Kernel Size 1
concepts.description: A convolution with kernel size 1 operates independently at each position using the same set of learned parameters. It is often equivalent to a position-wise linear transformation applied across sequence positions.

concepts.name: Weight Sharing
concepts.description: Weight sharing is a parameter-sharing scheme in which the same weight matrix is used in multiple parts of a model. It reduces the number of learned parameters and can tie the geometry of related representations together.

concepts.name: Pre-Softmax Linear Transformation
concepts.description: A pre-softmax linear transformation is a learned linear layer applied to model outputs before the softmax function. It converts hidden representations into scores over the target vocabulary or class set.
concepts.match_reason: The candidate is a general linear transformation, while the incoming concept is the specific linear layer used before softmax; they are related but not the same concept.

concepts.name: computational complexity per layer
concepts.description: The amount of computation required to execute one layer of a neural network. It is used to compare how expensive different layer types are as sequence length changes.

concepts.name: path length
concepts.description: The number of computational steps a signal must traverse between two positions in a network. Shorter path lengths make it easier for information and gradients to move between distant parts of a sequence.

concepts.name: maximum path length
concepts.description: The longest path length between any two positions in a network. It summarizes the worst-case distance that information or gradients must traverse across input and output positions.
concepts.match_reason: The candidate is the general notion of path length between two positions, while the incoming concept is the maximum/longest path length over all position pairs, so they are not the same concept.

concepts.name: sequential operations
concepts.description: Operations that must be executed one after another rather than in parallel. The number of sequential operations determines how much of a layer can be parallelized on a given computation device.

concepts.name: parallelization
concepts.description: The extent to which computations can be carried out simultaneously instead of in a strict sequence. Higher parallelization generally reduces the time needed to process a layer or sequence.
concepts.match_reason: Parallelization is the degree of simultaneous computation, while sequential operations are those executed one after another; they are opposites, not the same concept.

concepts.name: dilated convolution
concepts.description: A convolutional operation that inserts gaps between sampled input positions within a kernel. By enlarging the receptive field without proportionally increasing the number of parameters, it can connect distant positions more efficiently than standard contiguous convolution.

concepts.name: separable convolution
concepts.description: A convolutional design that factorizes a standard convolution into simpler operations, typically a depthwise step followed by a pointwise step. This lowers computational cost compared with a full convolution of the same kernel width.

concepts.name: WMT 2014 English-German dataset
concepts.description: A benchmark machine translation dataset of English-German sentence pairs from the 2014 Workshop on Machine Translation. It is commonly used to train and evaluate translation models on a standard parallel corpus.

concepts.name: WMT 2014 English-French dataset
concepts.description: A large machine translation dataset of English-French sentence pairs from the 2014 Workshop on Machine Translation. It provides a high-volume parallel corpus for training and evaluating translation systems.
concepts.match_reason: The candidate is English-German, while the incoming concept is English-French; they are different parallel corpora, not the same dataset.

concepts.name: sentence-pair batching
concepts.description: A batching strategy that groups paired input and target sentences together for training. Pairs are assembled so that examples of similar length can be processed efficiently in the same batch.

concepts.name: approximate sequence length
concepts.description: A rough measure of how long a token sequence is, used to group examples with similar lengths. It improves computational efficiency by reducing padding and balancing batch sizes.

concepts.name: source token
concepts.description: A token belonging to the input side of a sequence-to-sequence training example. Source tokens are the tokens the model conditions on when producing the target output.

concepts.name: target token
concepts.description: A token belonging to the output side of a sequence-to-sequence training example. Target tokens are the tokens the model is trained to predict given the source input.
concepts.match_reason: The candidate is the opposite side of the seq2seq example: source tokens are input tokens, while target tokens are output tokens to be predicted.

concepts.name: inverse square root decay
concepts.description: A learning-rate decay pattern in which the rate decreases proportionally to the inverse square root of the training step. It produces a slow decline that can remain effective during long training runs.

concepts.name: regularization
concepts.description: A set of techniques used during training to reduce overfitting and improve generalization. Regularization methods typically constrain model behavior or add noise so the model does not memorize the training data too closely.

concepts.name: newstest2014
concepts.description: 一个常用的机器翻译测试集，包含用于基准评测的新闻领域句子。它经常被用来比较不同翻译系统在英语到德语或英语到法语任务上的表现。
concepts.match_reason: newstest2014 是一个新闻领域的测试集，而候选项 0/1 是翻译任务，2 只是 WMT 2014 英德平行数据集，不是同一个概念。

concepts.name: Residual Dropout
concepts.description: 一种在残差连接中使用的正则化方法，先对每个子层的输出施加 dropout，再将其与子层输入相加并归一化。它还可用于嵌入与位置编码之和，以减少过拟合并提升模型泛化能力。
concepts.match_reason: Residual Dropout 是在残差结构中对子层输出施加 dropout 的正则化做法，不等同于残差连接本身，也不是层归一化。

concepts.name: 基准模型
concepts.description: Transformer 架构的较小配置版本，作为性能和成本比较的参考实现。它通常使用较少的参数和更低的训练开销，便于评估模型设计变化带来的影响。

concepts.name: 束搜索
concepts.description: 一种序列解码算法，在生成过程中保留若干个最有希望的候选序列，而不是只选择单一路径。它常用于机器翻译等任务，以在搜索效率和生成质量之间取得平衡。
concepts.match_reason: 束搜索是序列解码/搜索算法；编码器-解码器结构是序列到序列建模框架，二者不是同一概念。

concepts.name: 长度惩罚
concepts.description: 一种在序列解码中调整候选输出长度的评分机制，用来避免模型偏好过短或过长的结果。它通常与束搜索配合使用，以改善生成序列的整体质量。
concepts.match_reason: 长度惩罚是束搜索中用于调整候选序列长度的评分机制，而候选项是束搜索算法本身，二者是相关但不同的概念。

concepts.name: K80
concepts.description: K80 is a graphics processing unit model used for parallel computation. It is commonly referenced as a high-performance accelerator in computing systems.

concepts.name: K40
concepts.description: K40 is a graphics processing unit model used for parallel computation. It is commonly referenced as a high-performance accelerator in computing systems.
concepts.match_reason: K40 and K80 are different GPU models, so they do not denote the same concept.

concepts.name: M40
concepts.description: M40 is a graphics processing unit model used for parallel computation. It is commonly referenced as a high-performance accelerator in computing systems.
concepts.match_reason: M40 and K40 are different GPU models, not the same concept.

concepts.name: TFLOPS
concepts.description: TFLOPS is a unit of computing performance equal to one trillion floating-point operations per second. It is used to express the throughput of processors and accelerators.

concepts.name: attention value dimension
concepts.description: The dimensionality of the value vectors used in an attention head. It determines the size of the information returned by attention and influences both model capacity and computation.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is the dimensionality of value vectors, while candidate [0] is the dimensionality of key vectors; these are distinct attention head components.

concepts.name: compatibility function
concepts.description: A compatibility function computes how well a query matches a key in attention-based models. It produces the score used to weight values, and different forms can change the expressiveness of the attention mechanism.
concepts.match_reason: A compatibility function is the scoring component inside attention, while an attention function is the broader weighting mechanism that uses it.

concepts.name: English constituency parsing
concepts.description: English constituency parsing is the task of predicting the phrase-structure tree of an English sentence. It groups words into nested constituents and must satisfy strong structural constraints on the output.

concepts.name: Wall Street Journal portion of the Penn Treebank
concepts.description: The Wall Street Journal portion of the Penn Treebank is a standard annotated English text corpus used for parsing and other natural language processing experiments. It provides sentence-level data with phrase-structure annotations for training and evaluation.
aliases: WSJ portion of the Penn Treebank

concepts.name: semi-supervised setting
concepts.description: A semi-supervised setting is a training regime that combines a smaller labeled dataset with additional unlabeled or weakly labeled data. It is used to improve model performance when fully annotated data are limited.

concepts.name: BerkeleyParser corpora
concepts.description: BerkeleyParser corpora are large parsed text corpora associated with the BerkeleyParser pipeline. They are used as additional training data, often with high-confidence parses, to improve supervised models.

concepts.name: beam size
concepts.description: Beam size is the number of partial hypotheses retained during beam search decoding. Larger beams can improve search quality at the cost of greater computation.
concepts.match_reason: Beam size is a parameter of beam search, not the same concept as the beam search algorithm itself.

concepts.name: 双向表示
concepts.description: 一种同时利用目标位置左侧和右侧上下文来构建文本表示的方法。与只依赖单向上下文的表示相比，它通常能更全面地编码词语或句子的语义信息。

concepts.name: 语言表示模型
concepts.description: 一种把文本映射到向量表示的模型，用于捕获词语、句子或篇章中的语义和上下文信息。它们常被预训练后用于分类、问答和推断等下游自然语言处理任务。
concepts.match_reason: “语言表示模型”指将文本映射为向量表示的模型，而“语言模型”是估计符号序列概率分布的模型，概念不同，只是相关。

concepts.name: MultiNLI
concepts.description: 一个多领域自然语言推断数据集，用于评估模型在不同文本域中的句子关系判断能力。它包含来自多个体裁的句子对，并考察模型的泛化能力。

concepts.name: feature-based approach
concepts.description: 一种将预训练语言表示作为附加特征接入下游任务模型的方法。任务特定架构保持独立，预训练参数通常不直接在下游任务中整体更新，而是由外部模型利用这些表示进行学习。
concepts.match_reason: Incoming concept is the feature-based use of pretrained representations in downstream models, while the candidate is the pretraining process itself; they are related but not the same concept.

concepts.name: bidirectional pre-training
concepts.description: A pre-training approach that learns language representations from both left and right context. It is designed to produce contextual embeddings that can use information from the entire surrounding sentence rather than only earlier words.

concepts.name: shallow concatenation
