concepts.description: A representation strategy that combines separately trained left-to-right and right-to-left language model outputs with a simple concatenation. It merges directional information without fully integrating the two contexts throughout the model.

concepts.name: discriminative context objective
concepts.description: A training objective that learns word representations by distinguishing correct words from incorrect words using both left and right context. It encourages embeddings to encode contextual compatibility rather than only local prediction from one side.
aliases: objectives to discriminate correct from incorrect words in left and right context
concepts.match_reason: The incoming term is a specific discriminative training objective, while the candidate is a broader bidirectional pre-training approach; they are related but not the same concept.

concepts.name: sentence embeddings
concepts.description: Vector representations of entire sentences that summarize their meaning in a fixed-dimensional form. They are used to represent sentences for comparison, retrieval, or as input to later models.
concepts.match_reason: Sentence embeddings represent whole sentences, while the candidates are word-level embeddings; they are related but not the same concept.

concepts.name: paragraph embeddings
concepts.description: Vector representations of paragraphs that encode the meaning of a longer span of text in a single fixed-size representation. They are designed to support tasks that require information aggregated over multiple sentences.
concepts.match_reason: Paragraph embeddings are for longer text spans than sentence embeddings, so they are related but not the same concept.

concepts.name: candidate next sentence ranking
concepts.description: A training objective that learns sentence representations by scoring and ordering possible next sentences. It teaches a model to prefer the sentence that is most coherent or likely to follow a given context.
concepts.match_reason: Next-sentence ranking is a sentence-level coherence/ranking objective, while left-to-right language modeling predicts the next word in sequence; they are different training objectives.

concepts.name: next-sentence word generation
concepts.description: A training objective in which a model generates the words of a following sentence from a representation of the previous sentence. It trains sentence embeddings to support sequence generation conditioned on prior context.
aliases: left-to-right generation of next sentence words given a representation of the previous sentence
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a sentence-level generation objective from a previous sentence representation, while the candidates are next-sentence ranking and left-to-right word prediction; neither denotes the same training objective.

concepts.name: right-to-left language model
concepts.description: A language model that predicts words using the context that follows them in a sequence. It complements left-to-right modeling by encoding information from later context.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is specifically a right-to-left language model, while the candidate is a broader unidirectional language model that could also be left-to-right; they are not the same concept.

concepts.name: sentence encoder
concepts.description: A model that maps a sentence into a fixed or contextualized vector representation. Sentence encoders are often pre-trained on unlabeled text and then adapted to supervised tasks.
concepts.match_reason: A sentence encoder is a model that produces representations, while sentence embeddings are the vector outputs themselves, so they are related but not the same concept.

concepts.name: supervised downstream task
concepts.description: A target task learned with labeled data after a model has been pre-trained on unlabeled data. Fine-tuning on downstream tasks adapts general representations to a specific application.

concepts.name: left-to-right language model
concepts.description: A language model that predicts each token using only the tokens that come before it in the sequence. This autoregressive factorization is commonly used for text generation and next-token prediction.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a specific left-to-right autoregressive language model, while candidate 1 is a training objective and candidate 2 is a broader category that also includes right-to-left models.

concepts.name: 双向 Transformer 编码器
concepts.description: 一种允许每个位置同时利用左侧和右侧上下文的 Transformer 编码器。与只使用单向上下文的编码方式相比，它能够形成更完整的上下文表示。
concepts.match_reason: “双向 Transformer 编码器”是 Transformer 编码器的一种具体变体，而候选[0]只是通用 Transformer 编码器，候选[1]则是更泛的双向表示概念，不是同一概念。

concepts.name: left context
concepts.description: The portion of a sequence that appears before a given token. In autoregressive models, it is the available context that a token may attend to when future positions are masked out.

concepts.name: segment embeddings
concepts.description: Learned embeddings that identify which segment of an input sequence each token belongs to. They are used to distinguish tokens from sentence A and sentence B in paired inputs.
concepts.match_reason: Segment embeddings encode sentence/segment identity in paired inputs, which is different from positional, sentence-level, or generic learned embeddings.

concepts.name: deep bidirectional representation
concepts.description: A contextual representation learned from both left and right contexts at multiple layers. It is designed to capture richer dependencies than models that only use one direction or a shallow combination of directions.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a learned deep bidirectional representation, while the candidate is a bidirectional pre-training method; they are related but not the same thing.

concepts.name: monolingual corpus
concepts.description: A collection of text written in a single language. It can be used to generate self-supervised training examples, such as sentence-pair prediction data, without manual labeling.

concepts.name: IsNext
concepts.description: A label used in next sentence prediction indicating that the second sentence is the actual sentence that follows the first one in the original corpus. It marks positive examples in the binary classification task.

concepts.name: NotNext
concepts.description: A label used in next sentence prediction indicating that the second sentence is a random sentence rather than the true continuation of the first. It marks negative examples in the binary classification task.
concepts.match_reason: NotNext is the negative label, while IsNext is the positive label indicating the true continuation; they are opposite concepts.

concepts.name: BERT input representation
concepts.description: BERT的输入表示是将词元嵌入、句段嵌入和位置嵌入相加得到的表示方式。它用来同时编码词元身份、句子对所属片段以及顺序信息，以供模型后续处理。
concepts.match_reason: BERT input representation is a specific input embedding scheme for BERT, while the candidate is the BERT model itself, not the same concept.

concepts.name: 词元嵌入
concepts.description: 词元嵌入是将每个输入词元映射为可学习向量的表示方法。它负责编码词元本身的语义身份，是神经语言模型输入表示的基础组成部分。
concepts.match_reason: 词元嵌入只是输入表示的一部分；候选项是把词元、句段和位置嵌入相加得到的整体输入表示，不是同一概念。

concepts.name: 句段嵌入
concepts.description: 句段嵌入是用于区分输入序列中不同句段或文本片段的可学习向量。它常用于文本对任务中，帮助模型识别每个词元属于哪一段输入。
concepts.match_reason: 句段嵌入是区分不同句段/片段的向量，而词元嵌入是表示单个词元本身的向量，概念不同。

concepts.name: 英语维基百科
concepts.description: 英语维基百科是一个大规模的英文百科文本来源，常被用作预训练语料。它提供丰富的通用知识文本，并可抽取为连续的文段进行建模。

concepts.name: 文档级语料库
concepts.description: 文档级语料库是以完整文档而不是打乱后的独立句子为单位组织的语料。它保留长距离上下文和相邻句子之间的连续性，适合抽取长连续序列进行预训练。

concepts.name: 双向交叉注意力
concepts.description: 双向交叉注意力是一种让两个文本序列中的表示彼此互相关注的机制。它可在两个方向上建模跨序列对应关系，常用于文本对编码。
concepts.match_reason: “双向交叉注意力”是两个序列之间的跨序列注意机制；候选项分别是自注意力或双向上下文表示，概念不同。

concepts.name: BERT fine-tuning
concepts.description: The process of adapting a pre-trained BERT model to a specific downstream task by adding task-specific inputs and outputs and updating all parameters end-to-end. It reuses the pre-trained language representations while requiring relatively little additional training.
concepts.match_reason: The candidate is a broad fine-tuning approach for pre-trained language models, while the incoming concept is specifically fine-tuning BERT for a downstream task.

concepts.name: sequence tagging
concepts.description: A token-level prediction task in which a label is assigned to each token in an input sequence. It is used for tasks such as named entity recognition and other forms of structured text labeling.

concepts.name: sentiment analysis
concepts.description: A text classification task that predicts the emotional or opinion polarity expressed in a piece of text. It typically assigns labels such as positive or negative to an input sentence or document.

concepts.name: final hidden state
concepts.description: The final hidden state is the output vector produced by the last layer of a neural network for a given input position or token. In Transformer models, it carries contextual information from the entire input sequence and can be used as a learned representation for downstream tasks.

concepts.name: classification layer
concepts.description: A classification layer is the output layer of a model that maps an input representation to scores for a fixed set of labels. It typically contains a weight matrix, and its outputs are used to compute class probabilities and prediction losses.

concepts.name: classification loss
concepts.description: Classification loss is an objective function used to train a model to assign the correct label among a fixed set of classes. It compares predicted class probabilities with the target label and provides the gradient signal for learning.
concepts.match_reason: Classification loss is a broad objective for classification, while cross entropy loss is one specific common example; they are not identical concepts.

concepts.name: BiLSTM+ELMo+Attn
concepts.description: A neural language understanding model that combines bidirectional LSTM layers, ELMo contextual embeddings, and an attention mechanism. It is used as a baseline architecture for benchmark tasks.

concepts.name: WNLI
concepts.description: A Winograd-style natural language inference task that requires resolving ambiguous pronoun references by reasoning over sentence context. It is used as a challenging entailment benchmark.
concepts.match_reason: WNLI is a specific Winograd-style NLI benchmark, while the candidates are general NLI or other NLI variants, not the same concept.

concepts.name: Spearman correlation
concepts.description: A rank-based correlation coefficient that measures the strength of a monotonic relationship between two variables. It is often used to compare predicted and gold ordinal scores.

concepts.name: random restart
concepts.description: A repeated fine-tuning procedure in which training is started multiple times from the same pretrained checkpoint but with different data shuffling or parameter initialization. It is used to reduce sensitivity to unstable optimization and to select the best-performing run.

concepts.name: single packed sequence
concepts.description: A single packed sequence is an input representation that concatenates multiple text segments into one model input while marking segment boundaries with different embeddings or segment identifiers. It allows a model to jointly encode related texts, such as a question and a passage, in one forward pass.

concepts.name: start vector
concepts.description: A start vector is a learned parameter used to score each token for being the beginning of an answer span. It is combined with token representations, often by a dot product, to produce a distribution over possible start positions.

concepts.name: end vector
concepts.description: An end vector is a learned parameter used to score each token for being the end of an answer span. It is combined with token representations, often by a dot product, to produce a distribution over possible end positions.
concepts.match_reason: An end vector scores answer span end positions, while a start vector scores span start positions; they are distinct, opposite components.

concepts.name: answer span
concepts.description: An answer span is a contiguous sequence of tokens in a passage that constitutes the answer to a question. Span-based question answering systems identify the start and end positions of this segment rather than generating free-form text.

concepts.name: candidate span scoring
concepts.description: Candidate span scoring is the process of assigning a score to each possible text span so that the best answer can be selected. In span-based extraction systems, the score is typically computed from the start and end position representations.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a scoring procedure for spans, while the candidate is the span itself; they are related but not the same concept.

concepts.name: 自训练方法
concepts.description: 一种通过利用模型自身产生的监督信号或伪标签来继续训练的学习方法。它常用于在缺少人工标注时扩大训练信号，并提升模型在下游任务上的效果。
concepts.match_reason: 自训练方法是利用模型自身预测/伪标签继续训练；候选项是预训练，指先在大规模无标注数据上学习通用表示。两者不是同一概念。

concepts.name: hyperparameter tuning
concepts.description: The process of adjusting training settings that are not learned directly from data, such as learning rate, batch size, or number of training steps. It is used to improve model performance and stability before or during training.
concepts.match_reason: Hyperparameter tuning adjusts training settings like learning rate or batch size; fine-tuning adapts a pretrained model on task-specific data.

concepts.name: Gaussian error linear unit
concepts.description: A smooth activation function that weights inputs by their probability under a Gaussian distribution before passing them through. It is used in neural networks as an alternative to rectified linear units.

concepts.name: FAIRSEQ
concepts.description: An open-source sequence modeling toolkit developed for training and evaluating neural network models on tasks such as machine translation and language modeling. It provides implementations and infrastructure for efficient experimentation with sequence-to-sequence and related architectures.

concepts.name: Replication study
concepts.description: A study that attempts to reproduce the methods and results of an original experiment or system using the same or closely related setup. Replication studies are used to verify findings, compare implementations, and identify sources of discrepancy between reported results and reproduced outcomes.

concepts.name: Adam epsilon term
concepts.description: Adam 优化器中的一个数值稳定性参数。它加入到分母中以避免除零或过小值导致的不稳定更新，并且有时需要针对特定训练设置单独调节。
concepts.match_reason: The epsilon term is only a numerical stability parameter within Adam, not the Adam optimizer itself.

concepts.name: β2 parameter
concepts.description: Adam 优化器中控制二阶矩指数滑动平均衰减速度的参数。它决定了历史平方梯度被记忆的程度，较大的取值常用于改善训练稳定性。
concepts.match_reason: β2 是 Adam 中控制二阶矩衰减的参数；epsilon term 是数值稳定性项，作用不同，不是同一概念。

concepts.name: mixed precision floating point arithmetic
concepts.description: 一种训练计算方式，同时使用不同精度的浮点表示来平衡速度、显存占用和数值稳定性。它通常在高性能 GPU 上用于加速大规模模型训练。

concepts.name: full-length sequences
concepts.description: 在语言模型预训练中始终使用达到最大长度的输入序列，而不是混合加入较短序列的训练方式。它用于保持训练目标和输入分布的一致性，并简化训练流程。

concepts.name: OpenWebText
concepts.description: An open-source recreation of the WebText corpus built from web content extracted from URLs shared on Reddit with at least three upvotes. It is used as a large-scale text corpus for language model pretraining.

concepts.name: Stories dataset
concepts.description: A dataset formed by filtering a subset of CommonCrawl data to match the story-like style of Winograd schemas. It is designed to provide narrative text with characteristics useful for language understanding and generation tasks.

concepts.name: single-sentence classification
concepts.description: A classification task format in which a model assigns a label to one input sentence. It is used to evaluate sentence-level understanding such as sentiment, entailment-related judgments, or grammaticality.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is classification on one sentence, while the candidate is classification on a pair of sentences; they are different task formats.

concepts.name: answerability classifier
concepts.description: A binary classifier used in question answering systems to predict whether a question has an answer in the given context. It is typically combined with span prediction so the model can abstain when no answer is supported by the passage.

concepts.name: SEGMENT-PAIR+NSP
concepts.description: SEGMENT-PAIR+NSP is a BERT input training format in which each example consists of two text segments and includes the next sentence prediction objective. The segments may each contain multiple natural sentences, subject to a maximum total length of 512 tokens.

concepts.name: SENTENCE-PAIR format
concepts.description: An input construction that uses individual sentences as paired examples for pretraining. It retains the next sentence prediction loss while replacing longer text segments with single sentences.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a sentence-level pair format for NSP, while candidate [2] allows multi-sentence segments and [1] is the broader segment-pair idea; they are related but not the same concept.

concepts.name: 批量大小
concepts.description: 每次参数更新时一起处理的训练样本数量。它会影响梯度估计的稳定性、训练效率以及模型最终性能。
concepts.match_reason: “批量大小”是每次更新时处理的样本数；候选项是使用较大 mini-batch 的训练方式，属于训练策略而非该参数本身。

concepts.name: 字节对编码
concepts.description: 一种基于统计合并规则构造子词词表的分词方法，介于字符级和词级表示之间。它通过反复合并频繁出现的符号对来形成子词单元，从而缓解稀有词和大词表问题。

concepts.name: 子词单元
concepts.description: 介于字符和完整词语之间的文本表示单位。它们通常由统计方法从语料中学习得到，用于在控制词表规模的同时表达开放词汇中的词语。
concepts.match_reason: “子词单元”是泛指介于字符和词语之间的表示单位；“字节对编码”是生成这类单位的一种具体方法，不是同一概念。

concepts.name: 字节级字节对编码
concepts.description: 一种以字节而不是 Unicode 字符作为基本单位的字节对编码变体。它能够用较小的子词词表表示任意输入文本，并避免产生未知词元。
concepts.match_reason: “字节级字节对编码”是以字节为基本单位的 BPE 变体；候选“字节对编码”是更一般的分词方法，不是同一概念。

concepts.name: 未知词元
concepts.description: 在词表中没有对应表示时使用的占位符词元。避免未知词元有助于模型处理开放词汇输入，并减少信息丢失。

concepts.name: subword vocabulary
concepts.description: A vocabulary made up of subword units rather than whole words. It allows text to be encoded with a limited set of units that can combine to represent many different strings.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a subword vocabulary in general, while candidate 0 is a specific WordPiece subword token/technique, not the same concept.

concepts.name: unknown token
concepts.description: A special token used when an input symbol or sequence cannot be represented by the available vocabulary. Tokenization schemes that avoid unknown tokens can encode arbitrary input text without fallback symbols.
concepts.match_reason: [MASK] is a placeholder for masked language modeling, not a fallback symbol for out-of-vocabulary or unrepresentable input tokens.

concepts.name: heuristic tokenization rules
concepts.description: Manually designed rules used to preprocess text into tokens before subword learning or model training. They aim to standardize input text but may introduce additional preprocessing requirements.

concepts.name: XLNet
concepts.description: A pretrained language model architecture that uses a large amount of data and many training sequences to learn contextual representations. It is designed to improve language modeling by leveraging extensive pretraining at scale.

concepts.name: training passes
concepts.description: The number of times a model iterates over the training data during learning. More passes can increase exposure to the data and affect how thoroughly the model learns from it.

concepts.name: 预训练LM嵌入空间
concepts.description: 语言模型内部表示所构成的向量空间，其中语义或上下文相似的文本前缀会彼此靠近。近邻检索可以在这个空间中按距离找到与当前上下文相似的历史表示。
concepts.match_reason: “预训练LM嵌入空间”指的是语言模型内部表示的向量空间；候选项中没有这个空间本身，1是模型，2是利用该空间的kNN-LM方法。

concepts.name: 近邻数据存储
concepts.description: 一种保存可检索表示及其对应文本片段的外部存储结构。模型在预测时从中查找与当前上下文最相近的条目，并用这些条目辅助生成输出。
concepts.match_reason: 该概念指用于近邻检索的外部存储结构，而候选0是k近邻模型方法、候选1是嵌入空间本身，都不是同一个存储概念。

concepts.name: 长尾分布
concepts.description: 一种少数高频事件占据大部分概率质量、而大量低频事件各自出现很少的分布形态。它在语言中对应罕见词、稀有模式和长尾事实知识等难预测现象。

concepts.name: pre-trained embedding space
concepts.description: The vector space formed by embeddings learned by a pre-trained model. Distances in this space reflect contextual similarity and can be used to retrieve nearest neighbors for prediction.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is the embedding space itself, while candidate 0 refers to the word embeddings learned by pre-training, which is a related but different concept.

concepts.name: nearest neighbor datastore
concepts.description: A stored collection of text examples or embeddings used for nearest-neighbor retrieval at inference time. It can be swapped or enlarged to adapt a model to new data or domains without retraining the base model.

concepts.name: 上下文-目标对
concepts.description: 一种由输入上下文及其对应预测目标组成的成对表示。它常被写入存储库中，用于在相似上下文出现时检索并复用目标信息。

concepts.name: 左侧上下文
concepts.description: 位于当前目标标记之前的前缀上下文。它包含用于预测下一个标记的历史信息，并可被编码为检索键。

concepts.name: 目标分布
concepts.description: 在给定检索到的邻居后，对候选目标标记形成的概率分布。该分布通常根据邻居与查询上下文的距离加权得到，并可与基础模型输出结合。

concepts.name: 长尾模式
concepts.description: 一种只在少量样本中频繁出现、总体上却很稀有的模式。它通常包含难以通过参数记忆充分覆盖的知识，因此适合借助外部记忆或检索来获取。
concepts.match_reason: “长尾模式”指稀有但在少量样本中高频出现的模式/知识形态；“长尾分布”是描述频率分布的统计形态，二者相关但不是同一概念。

concepts.name: 显式记忆
concepts.description: 一种将信息以可直接检索的形式存储的记忆机制。与仅依赖模型参数不同，它允许在推理时根据相似输入快速取回相关内容。

concepts.name: interpolation
concepts.description: A method for combining two probability distributions into one by taking a weighted mixture. In language modeling, it is used to blend retrieval-based predictions with the base model’s predictions.

concepts.name: RBF kernel
concepts.description: A radial basis function similarity measure that turns distances between vectors into smoothly decaying similarity weights. It is often equivalent to using an exponential of negative squared distance in nearest-neighbor retrieval.

concepts.name: BERT subword vocabulary
concepts.description: The BERT subword vocabulary is a 29,000-entry vocabulary of subword tokens associated with BERT. It provides the token inventory used for byte-pair encoded text in the described setup.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is the specific BERT vocabulary (a 29,000-entry token inventory), while the candidate is only the general idea of any subword vocabulary.

concepts.name: adaptive inputs
concepts.description: Adaptive inputs are a parameter-sharing technique for language models that allocates different embedding capacities to frequent and infrequent words. They reduce model cost while preserving representational power.

concepts.name: adaptive softmax
concepts.description: Adaptive softmax is a hierarchical output layer designed to speed up training and inference for large vocabularies. It assigns more computation to frequent classes and less to rare ones.

concepts.name: tied weights
concepts.description: Tied weights is a parameter-sharing scheme in which the input embedding matrix and output softmax matrix use the same learned parameters. This reduces the number of parameters and can improve generalization.
concepts.match_reason: Tied weights is a specific parameter-sharing scheme for input embeddings and output softmax, while weight sharing is the broader general concept.

concepts.name: FAISS index
concepts.description: An efficient similarity-search index used for approximate nearest-neighbor retrieval over high-dimensional vectors. It partitions vectors into clusters and supports fast lookup of nearest items during inference.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a FAISS index/data structure, while the candidate is the FAISS library itself; related but not the same concept.

concepts.name: cluster centroid
concepts.description: A representative vector for a cluster of embedded items, typically used to accelerate approximate nearest-neighbor search. Cluster centroids help limit the search space by routing queries to a small subset of candidate vectors.

concepts.name: quantization
concepts.description: A compression technique that represents vectors with fewer bytes or lower precision to reduce storage and speed up computation. In similarity search, it trades some accuracy for faster retrieval and lower memory use.

concepts.name: 键和值
concepts.description: 键和值是键值检索结构中的两类存储内容，其中键用于与查询进行相似度匹配，值用于提供对应的输出信息。它们共同支持基于邻近性的快速查找和条件生成。
concepts.match_reason: “键和值”指的是键值检索结构中的两类内容/组成部分，而“键值存储库”是整个存储结构本身，二者是部分与整体，不是同一概念。

concepts.name: 插值参数 λ
concepts.description: 插值参数是一种控制两种预测来源相对权重的超参数。它通常用于将语言模型分数与检索分数进行组合，并可在验证集上调节以获得更好的效果。
concepts.match_reason: “插值参数 λ”是用于控制加权融合的超参数，而“线性插值”指的是这种加权融合方法本身，不是同一个概念。

concepts.name: 验证集
concepts.description: 验证集是从训练数据之外划分出的开发数据，用于调节超参数和比较模型配置。它不直接参与参数学习，但常用于选择最优设置。

concepts.name: In-domain datastore
concepts.description: An in-domain datastore is a datastore built from training data drawn from the target domain of interest. Because its contents match the evaluation domain more closely, it can provide more relevant neighbors and improve adaptation.
concepts.match_reason: The candidate is the general concept of a datastore, while the incoming concept is a specific in-domain datastore built from target-domain data.

concepts.name: intermediate state
concepts.description: An internal representation produced at some point inside a neural network, before the final output layer. Different intermediate states can capture different kinds of information and may be more or less useful for retrieval.

concepts.name: BOOKS domain
concepts.description: A text domain consisting of book content, used as an out-of-domain target for evaluating language models. Performance in this domain can improve when a corresponding datastore is added to a retrieval-augmented model.

concepts.name: quantized key
concepts.description: A compressed vector representation used to reduce memory and speed up similarity search. Quantization approximates the original key vector so that nearest-neighbor lookup can be performed more efficiently.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a quantized vector/key, while the candidate is the quantization technique itself; related but not the same concept.

concepts.name: full precision key
concepts.description: An uncompressed vector representation stored at its original numeric precision. Using full precision keys can improve the accuracy of similarity computations compared with quantized representations.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is an uncompressed full-precision vector, while the candidate is a compressed quantized vector; they are contrasting forms, not the same concept.

concepts.name: learned representation function
concepts.description: A function that maps text contexts or instances into vector representations used to measure similarity between examples. Such representations allow models to compare varied contexts in a continuous space rather than relying only on exact symbol matches.

concepts.name: training speed
concepts.description: 训练速度是指模型性能随训练过程提升的快慢，或达到给定损失所需的优化进展速率。它受模型规模、优化设置和计算资源等因素影响。

concepts.name: training time
concepts.description: The amount of compute time used to optimize a model on training data. It can affect model performance by determining how much learning has been carried out and how far optimization has progressed.
concepts.match_reason: “Training time” is elapsed compute time spent optimizing, while “training compute” refers to total compute resources used; related but not the same concept.

concepts.name: unsupervised learning
concepts.description: A learning setting in which a model discovers patterns from unlabeled data rather than from explicit input-output targets. It is often used to learn general representations or generative models from large text corpora.
concepts.match_reason: Unsupervised learning uses only unlabeled data, while semi-supervised setting combines labeled and unlabeled data; they are different training regimes.

concepts.name: generative modeling
concepts.description: A modeling approach that learns the distribution of data so new samples can be generated. In language tasks, it aims to produce text that resembles natural language examples from the training data.
concepts.match_reason: Generative modeling is the broader approach of learning a data distribution to generate samples; a text generation model is a specific model for producing text, so they are not the same concept.

concepts.name: neural models
concepts.description: Models built from interconnected artificial neurons and trained with gradient-based methods. They are used to learn complex mappings from input data to predictions or generated outputs.

concepts.name: universality of overfitting
concepts.description: An empirical regularity stating that the penalty from overfitting depends predictably on the ratio between model size and dataset size. It captures the idea that scaling one factor without the other eventually yields diminishing returns, while scaling them together avoids a performance penalty.
concepts.match_reason: "Universality of overfitting" is a scaling-law regularity about how overfitting penalty depends on model/data ratio, not the overfitting phenomenon itself.

concepts.name: universality of training
concepts.description: An empirical regularity that training curves follow predictable power laws whose form is largely independent of model size. It allows early training behavior to be extrapolated to estimate the loss that would be reached with much longer training.
concepts.match_reason: The candidate is about overfitting scaling with model/data size, while the incoming concept is about training-loss power-law extrapolation over training time; they are different regularities.

concepts.name: transfer performance
concepts.description: Performance measured when a model is evaluated on data from a different distribution than the one used for training. It often tracks in line with in-distribution validation performance but may incur an approximately constant loss offset under distribution shift.

concepts.name: convergence inefficiency
concepts.description: The phenomenon in which the best performance under a fixed compute budget is obtained by training very large models and stopping before full convergence. It implies that fully converged training can be a poor use of compute compared with earlier stopping.
concepts.match_reason: Convergence inefficiency is about the compute-optimal benefit of stopping training before full convergence, while convergence just means the training process stabilizes.

concepts.name: gradient noise scale
concepts.description: A measure of the stochasticity in gradients caused by using finite minibatches. It can be used to predict an effective or optimal batch size for training.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a measure of gradient stochasticity, while the candidates are a batch-size target and a training technique; none denote the same concept.

concepts.name: test loss
concepts.description: Test loss is the value of a loss function measured on held-out data to estimate a model's predictive performance. Lower test loss indicates better generalization to unseen examples.
concepts.match_reason: Test loss is an evaluation metric on held-out data, while classification loss is a training objective for class prediction; related but not the same concept.

concepts.name: early stopping
concepts.description: Early stopping is a training strategy in which optimization is halted before convergence to limit overfitting or to match a resource constraint. In compute-limited settings, it can determine the final performance reached before training is fully complete.
concepts.match_reason: Early stopping is a training strategy; convergence inefficiency is a related compute-budget phenomenon, not the same concept.

concepts.name: loss
concepts.description: A numerical measure of model error used during training and evaluation. Lower loss indicates better fit to the data or better predictive performance under the chosen objective.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is the general notion of loss, while all candidates are specific kinds of loss (test loss, classification loss, negative log-likelihood).

concepts.name: early-stopped test loss
concepts.description: 在训练早期停止时评估得到的测试损失，用来衡量模型在有限训练预算下的泛化性能。它可以随着模型规模、数据规模和训练过程的推进而系统变化，并常被用作缩放规律分析中的目标量。
concepts.match_reason: “early-stopped test loss” is a specific test-loss value measured after stopping training early, while candidate [1] is the generic metric and [0] is the stopping strategy itself.

concepts.name: learning curve
concepts.description: A learning curve is a function that describes how model performance changes as training progresses or as resources such as data or optimization steps increase. It is used to summarize the rate of improvement and to fit empirical training behavior.

concepts.name: infinite data limit
concepts.description: The infinite data limit is an idealized regime in which the amount of training data is treated as unbounded. It is useful for analyzing how performance depends on optimization and model capacity without data scarcity effects.

concepts.name: model parameters
concepts.description: The learned numerical values that define a neural network’s computations. They determine how the model transforms inputs into outputs and are adjusted during training to reduce loss.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is the model’s learned weights in general, while the candidates are either parameter count/capacity or a subset of parameters, not the same concept.

concepts.name: minimum non-embedding compute
concepts.description: An estimate of the least amount of non-embedding training compute needed to reach a target loss. It corresponds to the compute that would be used when training with a batch size much smaller than the critical batch size.
concepts.match_reason: The candidate is the general notion of non-embedding training compute, while the incoming concept is the minimum amount needed to reach a target loss; they are related but not the same concept.

concepts.name: minimum number of training steps
concepts.description: An estimate of the smallest number of training steps needed to reach a target loss. It corresponds to the step count that would be used when training with a batch size much larger than the critical batch size.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is about the minimum number of training steps, while the candidate is about minimum non-embedding compute; they are different quantities and even refer to opposite batch-size regimes.

concepts.name: power-law exponent
concepts.description: A parameter that controls how a quantity scales according to a power law. In scaling analyses, it determines how loss changes as compute, data, model size, or other quantities increase.

concepts.name: LSTM
concepts.description: An LSTM is a recurrent neural network architecture with gated memory cells designed to preserve information over long sequences. Its gating mechanism helps mitigate vanishing gradients and supports sequence modeling over extended contexts.

concepts.name: residual stream
concepts.description: The residual stream is the main hidden-state pathway that carries information through successive layers of a Transformer. Layer outputs are added back into this stream so that later computations can build on earlier representations.

concepts.name: matrix multiplication
concepts.description: A binary operation that combines two matrices to produce a third matrix whose entries are computed from row-by-column products and sums. It is a core linear algebra operation used in many numerical and computational systems.

concepts.name: de-embedding
concepts.description: A projection from hidden representations back into vocabulary space to produce output scores over symbols. In language models, it is used to convert internal states into token logits for prediction.

concepts.name: Adafactor
concepts.description: An adaptive optimizer designed to reduce memory usage by factorizing second-moment estimates. It is often used for very large models when standard Adam is too memory-intensive.
concepts.match_reason: Adafactor is a different optimizer from Adam; it is a memory-efficient factorized variant, not the same concept.

concepts.name: linear warmup
concepts.description: A learning-rate strategy in which the rate starts small and increases linearly for a fixed number of steps. It is commonly used at the beginning of training to improve stability.
concepts.match_reason: The candidate is the general warmup phase, while the incoming concept specifically means a linear increase schedule; that’s a narrower variant, not the same concept.

concepts.name: cosine decay
concepts.description: A learning-rate schedule that gradually decreases the learning rate following a cosine-shaped curve. It is used to taper optimization smoothly toward zero near the end of training.
concepts.match_reason: Cosine decay and inverse square root decay are different learning-rate schedules with different decay shapes.

concepts.name: Reddit outbound links
concepts.description: Links posted on Reddit that point to external web pages. Such links can be harvested to build a large web text corpus for language model pretraining.

concepts.name: Reddit karma
concepts.description: A voting-based scoring mechanism used on Reddit to indicate community approval of posts or links. A minimum karma threshold can be used as a heuristic for filtering content that appears interesting or useful.

concepts.name: Newspaper3k
concepts.description: A Python library used to extract the text content of web pages and articles. It automates article parsing and text extraction from online sources.

concepts.name: feed-forward ratio
concepts.description: The ratio between the feed-forward dimension and the model dimension in a Transformer. It controls how much capacity is allocated to the position-wise feed-forward sublayers relative to the hidden representation size.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a specific ratio hyperparameter, while the candidates are a feed-forward sublayer itself or a broader Transformer configuration, not the same concept.

concepts.name: attention head dimension
concepts.description: The dimensionality assigned to each attention head in a multi-head attention layer. It determines the size of the subspace each head operates on and is derived from the model dimension and the number of heads.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is the per-head hidden size in multi-head attention, while the candidates are the key/value dimensions or the head itself; none denotes the same concept.

concepts.name: embedding parameters
concepts.description: Parameters used in token and position embeddings rather than in the core Transformer layers. They can affect measured performance trends when included in the total parameter count.
concepts.match_reason: The candidate is the opposite category: it excludes embedding tables, while the incoming concept includes embedding parameters.

concepts.name: ResNet
concepts.description: A residual neural network architecture that uses skip connections to ease optimization of deep models. It has been suggested as an analogy for interpreting deeper networks as collections of effectively shallower components.

concepts.name: in-distribution validation loss
concepts.description: In-distribution validation loss is the loss measured on validation data drawn from the same distribution as the training data. It is used as an indicator of how well a model has learned the target distribution and often tracks generalization performance.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a validation loss on same-distribution data, while the candidate is performance on a different distribution under transfer; they are related but not the same concept.

concepts.name: 文档检索
concepts.description: 从大规模语料库中选取若干候选文档的过程。它为语言模型提供外部上下文，使模型能够利用与当前预测相关的文本证据。
concepts.match_reason: “文档检索”是选取候选文档的过程；候选0“检索器”是执行该过程的模块/组件，不是同一概念。

concepts.name: 大规模语料库
concepts.description: 包含大量文本文档的集合，供检索系统搜索相关证据。它为检索式语言模型提供可查询的外部知识来源。
concepts.match_reason: “大规模语料库”是可检索的文档集合；“文档检索”是从该语料库中选取候选文档的过程，概念不同。

concepts.name: 维基百科
concepts.description: 一个大规模、众包编辑的百科全书式文本资源，常被用作检索语料库。它提供覆盖广泛主题的文档，适合支持基于检索的语言建模。
concepts.match_reason: “维基百科”是泛指维基百科，而候选[0]是特定的“英语维基百科”，范围更窄，不是同一概念。

concepts.name: 潜变量语言模型
concepts.description: 一种把离散或未观测变量纳入概率建模的语言模型。通过对潜变量进行边缘化，可以在预测时把检索结果等隐藏选择纳入生成过程。
concepts.match_reason: 候选项是泛指的语言模型；“潜变量语言模型”是把潜变量纳入建模的具体变体，不是同一概念。

concepts.name: 边缘似然
concepts.description: 将潜变量的所有可能取值积分或求和后得到的整体似然。它用于训练包含隐变量的模型，使模型不必显式选择唯一的潜变量取值。
concepts.match_reason: “边缘似然”是对潜变量积分/求和后的似然值；“潜变量语言模型”是一个使用潜变量边缘化的模型本身，不是该概念。

concepts.name: 离散检索步骤
concepts.description: 在神经模型中显式选择一个或多个离散候选项作为中间步骤的机制。它使模型可以先从外部记忆或语料中选取内容，再基于所选内容进行预测。

concepts.name: 反向传播
concepts.description: 一种通过计算图传播梯度以更新模型参数的优化方法。它使模型能够根据最终损失调整前面各组件的行为，包括检索决策。

concepts.name: 缓存
concepts.description: 将已经计算过的中间结果保存起来以便后续重用的技术。它可以减少重复计算，特别适合大规模检索系统中对文档表示的复用。

concepts.name: 异步更新
concepts.description: 在不同时间或不同线程中独立更新参数或状态的机制。它可以降低大规模系统中的计算阻塞，并提高检索表示的更新效率。

concepts.name: semantic information
concepts.description: Semantic information is information about meaning in language, including how words and phrases relate to each other in context. Models use it to choose tokens that fit the intended meaning of an input sequence.
concepts.match_reason: Semantic information is about meaning, while the candidate is about grammatical structure; they are related but different concepts.

concepts.name: wordpiece tokenization
concepts.description: Wordpiece tokenization is a subword tokenization method that splits text into frequent pieces rather than full words. It helps neural language models handle rare and unseen words by representing them as sequences of subword units.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is the WordPiece tokenization method, while candidate 0 is a single WordPiece token and candidate 1 is byte-pair encoding, a different subword method.

concepts.name: 知识增强编码器
concepts.description: 一种将输入与检索到的知识联合编码的神经网络模块。它把外部检索内容融入表示学习过程，以便在语言建模或问答等任务中利用补充知识。
concepts.match_reason: “知识增强编码器”是联合编码输入和检索知识的编码模块；候选项要么是检索器，要么是检索增强预训练框架，都不是同一概念。

concepts.name: 开放域问答微调
concepts.description: 一种针对开放域问题回答任务的监督微调过程。模型根据问题和文档生成答案字符串，通常依赖于从文档中抽取连续文本片段作为答案。
concepts.match_reason: “开放域问答微调”是针对该任务的监督训练过程，不是“开放域问答”这一任务本身。

concepts.name: 跨度集合
concepts.description: 与某个目标答案匹配的一组文本跨度。它列出文档中所有能对应同一答案字符串的位置，用于在存在多个匹配时对答案概率进行汇总。
concepts.match_reason: “跨度集合”是多个匹配同一答案的文本跨度集合；“答案片段”是单个连续答案跨度，概念不同。

concepts.name: log-likelihood
concepts.description: The logarithm of the probability assigned to the correct output under a model. It is commonly maximized during training to make the model assign higher probability to the observed targets.
concepts.match_reason: Log-likelihood is the log probability itself, while negative log-likelihood is its negated loss; they are opposite quantities, not the same concept.

concepts.name: relevance score
concepts.description: A numerical score that measures how well a candidate document matches an input. In inner-product retrieval systems, it is computed from the similarity between the input embedding and the document embedding.

concepts.name: parameter staleness
concepts.description: The mismatch that occurs when a precomputed index or representation is not updated after model parameters change. It can cause retrieval results to reflect outdated embeddings until the index is refreshed.

concepts.name: primary trainer job
concepts.description: The main training process that performs gradient updates on model parameters. It carries out the optimization of the model while other jobs may handle auxiliary tasks such as index maintenance.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept refers to the main training job/process, while the candidate is a single optimization step/update, so they are not the same concept.

concepts.name: secondary index builder job
concepts.description: A background process that embeds documents and builds or refreshes an index. It runs in parallel with training so that retrieval structures can be updated without interrupting gradient-based optimization.

concepts.name: posterior distribution p
concepts.description: A conditional distribution over a latent variable z given an input x. In latent-variable models, it assigns probability mass to candidate latent explanations and is often differentiated to support learning.

concepts.name: 异步 MIPS 刷新
concepts.description: 一种在训练过程中并行重建最大内积搜索索引的机制。训练器在继续更新参数的同时，把当前参数快照交给索引构建器，后者在后台生成新索引并在完成后返回，从而减少检索索引更新对训练的阻塞。

concepts.name: 最大内积搜索索引
concepts.description: 一种用于近似或加速按向量内积进行检索的索引结构。它支持根据查询表示快速找到与之内积最大的文档表示，常用于神经检索系统中的候选召回。
concepts.match_reason: 前者是用于最大内积搜索的索引结构，后者是最大内积搜索这一检索技术本身，不是同一概念。

concepts.name: Embeddoc
concepts.description: 一种把文档映射到检索空间的参数化编码器。它用于为文档构建向量表示，以便在最大内积搜索中与查询表示进行匹配，并可在预训练时随索引一起刷新。
concepts.match_reason: Embeddoc is a document encoder, while Embedinput is a query/input encoder; they are complementary components, not the same concept.

concepts.name: 显著跨度掩蔽
concepts.description: 一种掩蔽语言建模策略，优先遮蔽那些更可能需要世界知识才能恢复的命名实体或日期等片段。它通过把训练焦点从只依赖局部上下文的简单片段转向更依赖外部知识的片段，来引导模型学习有意义的检索。
concepts.match_reason: “显著跨度掩蔽”是掩码语言建模的一种特定变体，优先遮蔽更依赖知识的片段；候选[0]只是通用的 masked language model，范围更广，不是同一概念。

concepts.name: 空文档
concepts.description: 一种在检索结果中加入的虚拟空条目，用来表示当前样本不需要任何外部文档即可完成预测。它允许模型把“无需检索”的情况归入一个一致的接收项，从而更合理地分配学习信号。

concepts.name: 平凡检索
concepts.description: 一种不希望出现的检索行为，指检索器通过查找与输入文本的字面重合或近似重复来获得答案，而不是学习更一般的相关性。它会让模型过度依赖精确字符串匹配，并削弱检索对世界知识的利用。
concepts.match_reason: “平凡检索”描述的是一种不希望出现的字面/近重复匹配行为，不是一般的检索器或潜在知识检索器。

concepts.name: cold-start problem
concepts.description: A training failure mode in which a model begins with poor representations and therefore retrieves unhelpful evidence, causing downstream components to ignore the retrieved information. This can create a feedback loop in which the retriever receives little useful gradient and cannot improve.

concepts.name: short answer type
concepts.description: An answer category used to indicate brief, span-like answers rather than longer or more complex responses. In benchmark filtering, it is used to select questions whose answers can be expressed in at most a few tokens.

concepts.name: entity linking
concepts.description: A retrieval heuristic that maps mentions in a question to entities in a knowledge source in order to identify relevant documents. It is often used to narrow the search space before later ranking stages.

concepts.name: latent variable model
concepts.description: A probabilistic model that includes hidden variables which are not directly observed but influence the observed output. In retrieval-and-generation systems, latent variables can represent hidden document choices or other unobserved decisions.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a probabilistic model that uses hidden variables, while the candidate is the hidden variable itself, not the model.

concepts.name: sequence prediction task
concepts.description: A learning setting in which a model predicts an output sequence conditioned on an input sequence. In open-domain question answering, this means generating an answer token by token from an encoded question.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is the general sequence-to-sequence prediction setting, while candidate [0] is a specific open-domain QA application of that setting.

concepts.name: Lucene-BM25 system
concepts.description: A retrieval system built on Lucene that uses BM25 as its ranking function. It is a standard sparse-search baseline for document and passage retrieval.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a Lucene-based retrieval system that uses BM25, while the candidate is just the BM25 ranking function itself; they are related but not the same concept.

concepts.name: inverted index
concepts.description: A data structure that maps terms to the documents or positions in which they occur. It enables efficient keyword-based retrieval over large text collections.
concepts.match_reason: An inverted index is a specific text-retrieval structure mapping terms to documents/positions, while a search index is a broader general concept.

concepts.name: 问题-段落对
concepts.description: 由一个问题与一个相关段落组成的训练样本，用于监督检索模型学习哪些文本应被视为与问题相关。它们是训练问题段落检索器的核心监督信号。
concepts.match_reason: “问题-段落对”是用于训练的样本/监督信号；Dense Passage Retriever 是利用这些样本训练的检索模型本身，不是同一概念。

concepts.name: dense encoder
concepts.description: A neural encoder that maps a text passage into a d-dimensional real-valued vector representation. These vectors are used to place passages in a continuous embedding space for similarity-based retrieval.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is an encoder for passages/text, while the candidates are either a sentence encoder, a question encoder, or an embedding representation rather than the encoder itself.

concepts.name: fixed-length passages
concepts.description: Passages segmented to a uniform length rather than using natural paragraph boundaries. They are used as retrieval units when a fixed passage size improves retrieval and end-to-end question answering performance.
concepts.match_reason: The candidate is a general text passage retrieval unit, while the incoming concept specifically means passages segmented to a fixed uniform length.

concepts.name: natural paragraphs
concepts.description: Text segments corresponding to paragraph boundaries in a document. They can serve as retrieval units, although they may be less effective than fixed-length passages in some retrieval systems.
concepts.match_reason: Natural paragraphs are text segments based on paragraph boundaries, while fixed-length passages are uniform-length segments; they are different retrieval units, not the same concept.

concepts.name: 问题编码器
concepts.description: 一种将输入问题映射到固定维度向量表示的编码网络。它的输出用于与段落向量计算相似度，从而支持检索排序。
concepts.match_reason: “问题编码器”指的是用于编码问题的查询向量网络；候选项中没有单独对应这一概念，0 是整个检索模型，1 是通用编码器结构，2 是知识增强编码器。

concepts.name: 马氏距离
concepts.description: 一种考虑协方差结构的距离度量，用于衡量两个向量在变换空间中的差异。它可以等价地表示为某个线性变换空间中的 L2 距离。
concepts.match_reason: 马氏距离与 L2 距离不是同一概念；前者考虑协方差并可在变换后对应到 L2，但本质上是不同的距离度量。

concepts.name: 稠密向量
concepts.description: 一种在较低维连续空间中表示对象的向量表示，通常每个维度都包含实值信息。它们适合用于相似度计算、最近邻搜索和向量索引。

concepts.name: 度量学习
concepts.description: 一种学习表示空间或距离函数的训练范式，目标是让相似样本彼此接近、不同样本彼此远离。它常用于检索、匹配和度量比较任务。
concepts.match_reason: 度量学习是学习距离/表示空间的训练范式，而 L2 distance 是一种具体距离度量，不是同一概念。

concepts.name: 正样本
concepts.description: 在监督学习或检索任务中，被标记为相关、匹配或正确的样本。正样本用于指示模型应当优先检索或提高得分的目标对象。
concepts.match_reason: “正样本”指被标记为相关/正确的样本；“sample efficiency”是模型利用样本学习的效率，概念不同。

concepts.name: 检索
concepts.description: 从大规模候选集合中找出与查询最相关条目的任务。检索系统通常依赖表示学习或排序函数来对候选项进行打分并返回最优结果。
concepts.match_reason: “检索”指从候选集合中找出相关条目的任务/过程；候选0是执行该任务的检索器模块，候选1是基于检索的整类方法，均不是同一概念。

concepts.name: 金标准样本
concepts.description: 在标注数据中被视为正确答案或最可靠参考的样本。金标准样本常用于训练、评估或构造高质量监督信号。
concepts.match_reason: “金标准样本”是被视为最可靠的标准答案样本；“正样本”只是被标记为相关/正确的样本，范围更广，不是同一概念。

concepts.name: 小批量
concepts.description: 在训练过程中一次送入模型的一小组样本，用于高效计算梯度并更新参数。小批量还能在训练时提供组内比较或采样机制。
concepts.match_reason: “小批量”指 mini-batch 本身，而候选项[0]是“批量大小”这一数量概念，不是同一概念；其余候选更不匹配。

concepts.name: full batch setting
concepts.description: A training regime in which all available examples in a batch are used together when computing the objective. In retrieval learning, it can provide a large set of in-batch negatives for contrastive training.
concepts.match_reason: Full batch setting is a training regime, while the candidates are either a generic batch concept or a different variant like large mini-batch training; none denotes the same concept exactly.

concepts.name: English Wikipedia dump
concepts.description: A snapshot of the English-language Wikipedia content released as a bulk data dump. It is commonly used as a source corpus for information retrieval and question answering tasks.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a bulk data dump/snapshot of English Wikipedia, while the candidates describe the Wikipedia site/edition itself, not the dump.

concepts.name: Wikipedia article
concepts.description: A standalone encyclopedic entry in Wikipedia containing text and metadata such as a title. Articles can be split into smaller passages for retrieval and question answering systems.

concepts.name: TREC QA tracks
concepts.description: A sequence of question answering evaluation tracks within the Text REtrieval Conference. They provide benchmark questions and assessments for QA systems.
concepts.match_reason: TREC QA tracks are the evaluation tracks in the TREC conference, while CURATEDTREC is a specific benchmark derived from the TREC question set, not the same concept.

concepts.name: learning rate
concepts.description: 优化算法在每次参数更新时所采用的步长大小。它决定模型参数变化的快慢，并对训练稳定性和收敛速度有重要影响。
concepts.match_reason: 学习率是参数更新的步长本身；候选中没有同义概念，learning rate schedule 只是调整学习率的策略，不是学习率本身。

concepts.name: Top-100 retrieval accuracy
concepts.description: 在前100个检索结果中包含答案段落的比例，用于衡量检索系统在较大候选集上的召回能力。它反映系统覆盖相关证据的能力。
concepts.match_reason: 候选0是通用的 top-k 指标，不特指 top-100；候选1是 top-20，数值不同。

concepts.name: multi-dataset encoder
concepts.description: A multi-dataset encoder is an encoder model trained on data pooled from multiple datasets so that it can generalize across them. It is intended to produce representations that work well on several related tasks or benchmarks rather than adapting to only one dataset.

concepts.name: linear combination
concepts.description: A linear combination is a scoring or prediction rule formed by adding weighted components together. In retrieval, it can be used to merge the scores of two systems into a single ranking function.

concepts.name: BM25+DPR
concepts.description: BM25+DPR is a hybrid retrieval approach that combines the scores of BM25 and a dense passage retriever to rank candidate passages. It leverages the complementary strengths of sparse lexical matching and dense semantic matching.
concepts.match_reason: BM25+DPR is a hybrid of BM25 and dense passage retrieval, while the candidates are just BM25-based sparse retrieval systems, so they are not the same concept.

concepts.name: TREC
concepts.description: 一个经典的信息检索评测集合，包含多种检索任务和基准，用于比较不同检索系统的性能。较小的数据规模使其在使用更多训练样本时往往受益更明显。
concepts.match_reason: TREC 是信息检索评测基准/数据集，而候选项“检索”是任务本身，二者不是同一概念。

concepts.name: lexical overlap
concepts.description: 查询与文档在表面词汇上的共享程度，通常指两者使用了相同或相近的词语。高词汇重叠往往会让基于词匹配的检索方法更占优势。

concepts.name: question–passage pairs
concepts.description: Paired examples consisting of a question and a passage, usually where the passage is relevant to the question. They serve as supervised training data for retrieval models that learn to match questions with passages.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is supervised training pairs of a question and a relevant passage, while the candidates are a passage unit, the retrieval process, and a fixed-length passage variant; none denotes those paired examples.

concepts.name: 1-of-N training setting
concepts.description: A retrieval training setup in which each question is paired with one positive passage and N negative passages, and the model is trained to select the positive among all candidates. It is a standard contrastive ranking formulation for learning passage retrieval.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a specific 1-of-N contrastive training setup with one positive and multiple negatives, while the candidate is just general question–passage pairs and does not require that ranking formulation.

concepts.name: gold negative passage
concepts.description: A passage that is relevant to some other question but treated as a negative example for the current question. Gold negatives are often harder than random negatives because they are known positives in another training instance.

concepts.name: distantly-supervised passage
concepts.description: 一种由弱监督方式获得的训练段落，通常不是人工标注的金标准，而是通过现有检索器或启发式规则自动选出的。它常用于在缺少人工标注上下文时构造训练数据。
concepts.match_reason: incoming is a weakly supervised training passage, while candidate 0 is a self-training learning method; they are related but not the same concept.

concepts.name: triplet loss
concepts.description: A ranking loss that trains a model using an anchor example, a positive example, and a negative example. It encourages the score or distance between the anchor and the positive example to be better than the score or distance between the anchor and the negative example by a margin.

concepts.name: positive passage
concepts.description: A passage that is relevant to a question or query and should be ranked above irrelevant passages. It serves as a positive training example in retrieval and ranking methods.
concepts.match_reason: A positive passage is a relevant example passage used for training, while passage retrieval is the process of finding passages; they are related but not the same concept.

concepts.name: negative passage
concepts.description: A passage that is not relevant to a question or query and should be ranked below relevant passages. It is used as a negative training example to teach a ranking model to distinguish useful evidence from distractors.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is the general notion of a negative passage, while the candidates are specific subtypes (hard negative, gold negative), not exact synonyms.

concepts.name: discriminative training
concepts.description: A training approach that learns to distinguish correct examples from incorrect ones directly through a scoring or classification objective. In retrieval models, it typically optimizes representations so relevant passages score higher than irrelevant ones.
concepts.match_reason: The candidate is a specific context-based objective for learning word representations, not the broader concept of discriminative training across models or tasks.

concepts.name: non-iid setting
concepts.description: A setting in which training and test data are not independently and identically distributed. It is used to study how well a model handles distribution shift between different data sources or domains.

concepts.name: knowledge-intensive NLP task
concepts.description: A natural language processing task that depends heavily on external factual knowledge rather than only on surface pattern recognition. Examples include open-domain question answering and other tasks where accessing precise world knowledge is important.

concepts.name: retrieve-and-extract architecture
concepts.description: A system design that retrieves evidence from an external source and then extracts the answer directly from the retrieved text. It is commonly used in question answering systems that rely on explicit supporting passages.

concepts.name: differentiable access mechanism
concepts.description: A retrieval or memory-access method that can be trained end-to-end through gradient-based learning. It enables a model to learn how to select or weight external memory content while remaining compatible with neural optimization.

concepts.name: differentiable retriever
concepts.description: 可微检索器是一种可通过梯度训练的检索组件，用于从外部语料或知识库中选择相关内容。它把检索过程纳入端到端优化，使检索与生成或理解模块能够联合学习。
concepts.match_reason: “Differentiable retriever” specifically means an end-to-end, gradient-trainable retrieval component, while the candidates are either generic retrievers or a more specific latent-retrieval variant.

concepts.name: state-of-the-art parametric-only seq2seq baseline
concepts.description: 仅参数化的序列到序列基线是只依赖模型参数进行输入到输出映射的基准系统，不使用显式检索或外部记忆。它常被用作比较对象，以衡量引入检索或外部知识后的改进。

concepts.name: pre-trained neural retriever
concepts.description: A retrieval model that encodes queries and documents into vectors and uses similarity search to find relevant documents. When pre-trained, it can be used as a component in downstream systems without training from scratch.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a pre-trained retriever model, while candidate 0 is the general retriever concept without the pre-training qualifier; the others are different retrieval frameworks or approaches.

concepts.name: memory network
concepts.description: 一种带有显式记忆组件的神经网络架构。它通过读取和写入记忆来支持对外部信息的存取，适用于需要长期或可检索知识的任务。
concepts.match_reason: “Memory network”是更一般的架构概念，而“端到端记忆网络”只是其一种具体变体，不是同一概念。

concepts.name: stack-augmented network
concepts.description: 一种在神经网络中加入栈式外部存储的架构。栈提供后进先出的可操作记忆结构，使模型能够表示需要嵌套或递归操作的计算过程。
concepts.match_reason: stack-augmented network is a specific neural architecture with a stack-based external memory, while memory network is a broader class with generic explicit memory, not the same concept.

concepts.name: memory layer
concepts.description: 一种在神经网络中引入记忆访问能力的层。它允许模型在前向计算中查询外部或内部记忆，以增强表示和推理能力。
concepts.match_reason: “memory layer”是更一般的神经网络层/组件概念，候选项里只有带记忆组件的整体架构或特定记忆机制，没有完全同一概念。

concepts.name: latent document variable
concepts.description: A hidden retrieved document treated as an unobserved variable in a probabilistic text generation model. Its value is summed or marginalized out to obtain the probability of the generated output.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a specific hidden document variable, while candidate 0 is the general latent variable and candidate 1 is a model containing latent variables, not the variable itself.

concepts.name: denoising objective
concepts.description: A pretraining objective in which corrupted input text is reconstructed from a noised version of the original text. It encourages a model to learn robust contextual representations useful for downstream generation tasks.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a training objective, while the candidate is a model architecture; they are related but not the same concept.

concepts.name: negative marginal log-likelihood
concepts.description: A training objective that minimizes the negative logarithm of the marginal probability assigned to each target output. It is used to adjust model parameters so that the model assigns higher probability to the correct output under all relevant latent choices or retrieved contexts.
concepts.match_reason: Negative marginal log-likelihood is a specific loss over marginalized latent choices, not the general negative log-likelihood, and candidate 2 is the probability itself rather than its negative log.

concepts.name: Thorough Decoding
concepts.description: A decoding procedure for retrieval-augmented sequence generation that evaluates candidate hypotheses across retrieved documents and performs additional forward passes when a hypothesis is missing from a document-specific beam. It estimates sequence probabilities by combining generator scores with retrieval probabilities across all relevant documents.
concepts.match_reason: Thorough Decoding is a specific decoding procedure for RAG, while the candidates are a model and a generic beam search algorithm, not the same concept.

concepts.name: Hierarchical Navigable Small World approximation
concepts.description: A graph-based approximation method for fast nearest-neighbor search in large vector spaces. It speeds up retrieval by navigating a hierarchical small-world graph instead of exhaustively comparing all vectors.

concepts.name: train
concepts.description: A predefined partition of a dataset into training, development, and test subsets. These splits are used to make model comparison fair and to ensure that evaluation is performed on held-out data.

concepts.name: TQA Wiki test set
concepts.description: A held-out test set associated with the TQA benchmark that contains Wikipedia-based questions for evaluation. It is used to compare systems on the same testing data and support direct comparison with prior work.
concepts.match_reason: TQA Wiki test set is a specific held-out evaluation split, while TriviaQA is the broader dataset/benchmark; they are not the same concept.

concepts.name: search engine
concepts.description: A system that indexes documents and retrieves passages in response to a query. It is commonly used to obtain candidate evidence for question answering and information retrieval tasks.
concepts.match_reason: A search engine is a retrieval system, while a search index is the underlying data structure it uses; they are related but not the same concept.

concepts.name: full sentence answer
concepts.description: An answer written as a complete sentence rather than a short phrase or span. It is typical of abstractive question answering and natural language generation settings.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a type of answer format (a complete sentence), while the candidate is an entire QA setting that generates free-form answers; related but not the same concept.

concepts.name: MSMARCO
concepts.description: 一个开放域抽取式问答基准，包含用于回答自然语言问题的文档检索与参考答案。它也被用作开放域生成式问答的评测来源，其中一些问题如果没有金标准段落就难以与参考答案完全匹配。
concepts.match_reason: MSMARCO is a specific open-domain QA benchmark, while candidate 0 is a particular NLG task variant of it and candidate 2 is only the general task type.

concepts.name: 开放域生成式问答
concepts.description: 一种问答任务，模型需要在不限定于单一封闭知识库的情况下生成答案。它通常结合检索到的证据和模型内部参数化知识，以输出自然语言回复。
concepts.match_reason: 候选0是更宽泛的开放域问答，包含抽取式和生成式；“开放域生成式问答”更具体，未与其完全同义。

concepts.name: Jeopardy 格式
concepts.description: 一种问答游戏中的题目形式，先给出关于某个实体的事实性陈述，再要求猜出对应实体。该格式强调精确、事实性的线索，并以“答案实体”作为生成问题的条件。
concepts.match_reason: “Jeopardy 格式”是题目呈现/线索结构本身；[0] 是利用该格式进行的问句生成任务，属于相关应用而非同一概念。

concepts.name: 事实性
concepts.description: 指生成内容是否能够被可信外部来源证实的性质。它常用于衡量生成系统输出的真实性和可核验性。

concepts.name: 特异性
concepts.description: 指输入与输出之间具有较高互相依赖性的性质，也就是输出是否紧密围绕给定条件展开。它常用于衡量生成结果是否足够贴合输入而非泛泛而谈。

concepts.name: natural language claim
concepts.description: A natural language claim is a declarative statement expressed in ordinary language that can be evaluated for truth or falsity. In fact verification tasks, such a claim is paired with evidence and classified according to whether the evidence supports, contradicts, or fails to resolve it.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a factual claim statement, while the candidate is the Natural Language Inference task that classifies relationships between sentences; they are related but not the same concept.

concepts.name: Wikipedia evidence retrieval
concepts.description: Wikipedia evidence retrieval is the process of finding Wikipedia passages or articles relevant to a claim. It supplies the evidence needed for downstream verification or reasoning over whether the claim is supported or refuted.

concepts.name: entailment reasoning
concepts.description: Entailment reasoning is the process of determining whether a set of evidence logically supports, contradicts, or is insufficient to determine a claim. In fact verification, it connects retrieved evidence to a final classification label.

concepts.name: claim-class pair
concepts.description: A claim-class pair is a training example consisting of a claim and its associated verification label. Such pairs are used to train models to map a claim directly to a support, refute, or uncertainty decision.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a specific claim-verification training example, while the candidate is a general sentence-pair classification task; they are related but not the same concept.

concepts.name: label accuracy
concepts.description: Label accuracy is the proportion of examples for which a model predicts the correct class label. It is a standard evaluation measure for classification tasks such as fact verification.

concepts.name: supports
concepts.description: Supports/refutes/not enough info is a three-way classification scheme used in FEVER. It assigns a claim to one of three outcomes: the evidence supports the claim, the evidence refutes it, or the available evidence does not suffice to decide.
aliases: supported, refuted, or not enough information

concepts.name: 文档边缘化
concepts.description: 一种在存在多个潜在文档证据时，对不同文档条件下的预测概率进行加权求和的推断方法。它把文档的不确定性纳入模型输出，使系统能够利用多个检索结果共同支持答案生成。
concepts.match_reason: “文档边缘化”是对多个候选文档上的预测进行边缘化/加权求和的推断方式；候选项中只有文档检索和检索增强生成，前者是取文档、后者是整体框架，都不是这一具体推断方法。

concepts.name: 生成式问答
concepts.description: 一种通过生成自然语言答案来回答问题的问答范式。与只抽取原文片段的方法不同，生成式问答可以综合多篇文档的信息并输出不必逐字出现于证据中的答案。
concepts.match_reason: “生成式问答”是泛指生成答案的问答范式；候选[0]是更具体的“开放域”生成式问答，范围不完全相同，其他候选也都不等同。

concepts.name: intermediate retrieval supervision
concepts.description: Training supervision that explicitly guides a model’s retrieval component before or during final prediction. It encourages the model to select useful evidence documents rather than learning retrieval only indirectly from the end task.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a specific form of retrieval supervision applied at an intermediate stage, while the candidate is the broader general concept of retrieval supervision.

concepts.name: The Sun Also Rises
concepts.description: A novel by Ernest Hemingway, first published in 1926. It is one of Hemingway’s best-known works and is associated with the expatriate community of the 1920s Lost Generation.

concepts.name: A Farewell to Arms
concepts.description: A novel by Ernest Hemingway, published in 1929. It draws on wartime experience and is considered one of his classic works of American literature.

concepts.name: Lost Generation
concepts.description: A term for the expatriate community of writers and artists associated with the 1920s. It refers to a group marked by postwar disillusionment and cultural displacement, especially among Americans living in Europe.

concepts.name: 梯度更新
concepts.description: 通过计算损失函数对参数的梯度并据此调整模型参数的优化步骤。它是训练和微调神经网络的核心机制，用于让模型在目标任务上改进。
concepts.match_reason: “梯度更新”是根据梯度调整参数的更新步骤；“反向传播”是计算并传递梯度的方法，二者相关但不是同一概念。

concepts.name: 文本交互
concepts.description: 通过输入文本提示、示例或指令与模型进行通信并获得输出的方式。它让模型在不显式修改参数的情况下响应任务要求。
concepts.match_reason: “文本交互”指通过提示/示例/指令与模型交互而不更新参数；“文本微调”是继续训练并更新模型参数，概念不同。

concepts.name: 临时推理
concepts.description: 一种在面对新问题时即时进行推断、组合和计算的能力，而不是依赖预先见过的固定模式。它通常体现为模型在少量上下文提示下完成新颖任务。

concepts.name: 单词重排
concepts.description: 一种要求识别并重新排列字母或词语以恢复正确形式的任务。它常用于测试模型对字符级模式和即时操作的能力。

concepts.name: using a novel word in a sentence
concepts.description: Using a novel word in a sentence is a task that tests whether a model can infer a new word’s meaning or usage from minimal context and deploy it appropriately. It is commonly used to assess one-shot or few-shot generalization to unfamiliar vocabulary.

concepts.name: large web corpora
concepts.description: Large web corpora are very large collections of text harvested from web pages and online sources. They are often used to train language models, but they can introduce noise, duplication, and other methodological issues.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a general category of large web-based text corpora, while the candidates are specific corpora (WebText and Common Crawl), not the same concept.

concepts.name: Common Crawl filtering
concepts.description: A preprocessing process that removes unwanted or low-quality content from the Common Crawl corpus before it is used for downstream tasks. It is used to improve the quality and suitability of web text for language-model training or analysis.
aliases: A Details of Common Crawl Filtering
concepts.match_reason: Common Crawl is the corpus itself, while Common Crawl filtering is a preprocessing step applied to that corpus; they are different concepts.

concepts.name: Task phrasing
concepts.description: The wording used to present a task or instruction to a model. Different phrasing choices can change how well a model interprets the task and performs on it.
aliases: Details of Task Phrasing and Specifications

concepts.name: 预训练循环语言模型
concepts.description: 先在大规模文本上学习语言建模能力的循环神经网络语言模型。它能够获得通用的语言知识，并在下游任务上进一步微调。
concepts.match_reason: 这是“预训练的循环神经网络语言模型”这一特定模型类型；候选项要么更泛化（预训练神经语言模型、自回归语言模型），要么是训练范式/表示学习，不是同一概念。

concepts.name: 预训练 Transformer 语言模型
concepts.description: 基于 Transformer 架构、先在大规模文本上预训练的语言模型。它们可以直接迁移到下游任务，并通过微调适应具体应用。
concepts.match_reason: Incoming is a generic pretrained Transformer language model, while the candidates are either specific variants (GPT/BERT) or broader non-Transformer pretrained language models.

concepts.name: 任务特定架构
concepts.description: 专门为某个具体任务设计的模型结构或系统组件。它通常针对任务目标进行定制，与通用预训练模型相对。

concepts.name: 阅读理解
concepts.description: 一种要求模型从给定文本中提取或推断答案的自然语言处理任务。它通常考查模型对篇章内容、指代关系和信息整合能力的理解。
concepts.match_reason: “阅读理解”是从文本中提取或推断答案的任务；候选中“问答”更偏泛化的问答任务，不是同一概念，其他候选也不匹配。

concepts.name: 任务特定数据集
concepts.description: 为某一具体任务收集和标注的训练样本集合。它为模型微调提供监督信号，并决定模型在该任务上的适应程度。
concepts.match_reason: 一个是任务特定训练数据集，另一个是任务特定模型架构，概念不同。

concepts.name: SuperGLUE
concepts.description: 一个标准的自然语言处理基准套件，用于评估模型在多种语言理解任务上的表现。它通常由多个具有挑战性的子任务组成，以比较不同系统的综合能力。
concepts.match_reason: SuperGLUE 和 GLUE 是不同的自然语言理解基准套件；前者是对后者的更难升级版，不是同一概念。

concepts.name: NLP基准套件
concepts.description: 一组用于评测自然语言处理系统性能的标准化任务集合。它提供可比较的测试环境，以衡量模型在不同语言任务上的能力。
concepts.match_reason: “NLP基准套件”是泛指自然语言处理领域的标准化评测套件；候选项中没有同样泛化的对应概念，GLUE/SuperGLUE只是具体实例，benchmark 又过于泛泛。

concepts.name: rapid adaption
concepts.description: The ability to adjust behavior quickly from a small amount of new information. In language models, it refers to learning a task pattern from very few examples or instructions.

concepts.name: ANLI
concepts.description: An adversarial natural language inference benchmark designed to be difficult for current models. It contains challenging examples that require robust reasoning beyond superficial patterns.
concepts.match_reason: ANLI and WNLI are different NLI benchmarks; both are challenging entailment tasks, but they denote distinct datasets with different evaluation setups.

concepts.name: QuAC
concepts.description: A conversational reading comprehension benchmark in which questions are asked in a dialogue over a passage. It evaluates comprehension across multi-turn interactions and context dependence.
concepts.match_reason: QuAC 和 CoQA 都是对话式阅读理解基准，但它们是不同的数据集/概念，不是同一个。

concepts.name: arithmetic
concepts.description: The branch of mathematics dealing with basic numerical operations such as addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division. In language-model evaluation, arithmetic tasks test whether the model can carry out simple calculations from context.

concepts.name: novel word
concepts.description: A word that is newly introduced or unfamiliar to the model at test time. It is used to probe whether the model can infer meaning and use a term correctly after only minimal exposure.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is the unfamiliar word itself, while the candidate is the task of using such a word in a sentence.

concepts.name: 预训练模型
concepts.description: 一种先在大规模通用数据上训练、再迁移到下游任务的模型。它通常具有可被进一步微调或用于少样本条件推理的参数化知识表示。
concepts.match_reason: “预训练模型”是通用的模型概念；候选项中没有同一概念，(2)是训练范式，(3)(4)是特定类型模型，(0)是训练阶段。

concepts.name: 条件化
concepts.description: 一种让模型在额外上下文信息约束下生成或预测输出的机制。通过将示例、提示或其他上下文作为输入，模型的行为会被这些条件所引导。
concepts.match_reason: “条件化”是更一般的机制；候选0是具体的条件文本生成模型，候选1是通过提示与模型交互，二者都不是同一概念。

concepts.name: Mechanical Turk
concepts.description: A human worker service used to collect or annotate data by assigning tasks to workers. It is often used to obtain examples or labels from people when a task must be communicated clearly.

concepts.name: 示范
concepts.description: 在提示中提供给模型的示例输入输出对，用于展示任务的完成方式和输出格式。示范越少，任务设置通常越依赖模型从上下文中推断规则。
concepts.match_reason: “示范”指提示中的示例输入输出对，本身是一个通用的示例概念；候选项中没有与之完全同义的概念，0/3/4分别是基于示范的学习设置或具体数量限制，不是同一概念。

concepts.name: 性能
concepts.description: 模型在特定任务或基准上的表现水平，通常通过准确率、得分或其他评测指标体现。不同任务设置之间常常需要在性能和样本效率之间权衡。
concepts.match_reason: “性能”指模型在任务上的表现水平；候选项中没有同义概念，sample efficiency 是样本效率，benchmark 是评测基准。

concepts.name: few-shot evaluation
concepts.description: An evaluation setting in which a model is given a small number of examples or demonstrations before producing an answer. It is used to measure how well a model can adapt from limited context rather than from full task-specific training.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is an evaluation setting for measuring performance with few examples, while candidate [1] is the learning/inference paradigm itself, not the evaluation setting.

concepts.name: one-shot evaluation
concepts.description: An evaluation setting in which a model is given exactly one example or demonstration before producing an answer. It tests whether a model can perform a task from minimal contextual guidance.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is an evaluation setting with exactly one example, while candidate 1 is the broader learning/in-context setting; they are closely related but not the same concept.

concepts.name: Sparse Transformer
concepts.description: A transformer architecture that reduces attention computation by using sparse attention patterns instead of full dense attention everywhere. It is used as a reference point for later models that combine dense and sparse attention structures.
concepts.match_reason: Sparse Transformer is a specific sparse-attention variant of the Transformer, not the general Transformer architecture.

concepts.name: pre-normalization
concepts.description: A transformer design in which normalization is applied before the main sublayer computation rather than after it. This can improve training stability in deep models.
concepts.match_reason: Pre-normalization is a transformer architecture pattern about where normalization is placed, while layer normalization is a specific normalization operation itself.

concepts.name: validation loss
concepts.description: Validation loss is an error measure computed on held-out data to estimate how well a model generalizes beyond the training set. It is often used to compare models and detect overfitting.
concepts.match_reason: Validation loss is the general held-out validation-set error; candidate 2 is a more specific in-distribution variant, and the others are broader or different concepts.

concepts.name: smooth power law
concepts.description: A smooth power law is a functional relationship in which one quantity changes approximately as a power of another over a wide range of scales. In machine learning scaling studies, it is used to describe how performance metrics vary with model size or data size.

concepts.name: fuzzy deduplication
concepts.description: Fuzzy deduplication is a near-duplicate removal technique that identifies and removes highly similar documents rather than only exact copies. It helps reduce redundancy in training data and prevents overlap between training and evaluation sets.

concepts.name: held-out validation set
concepts.description: A held-out validation set is a subset of data excluded from training and used only for evaluation. It provides an estimate of generalization and overfitting that is not biased by direct exposure during training.

concepts.name: reference corpora
concepts.description: Reference corpora are curated text collections used as high-quality benchmarks or sources for training and filtering other datasets. They are typically selected for their reliability, cleanliness, or domain coverage.

concepts.name: load balancing
concepts.description: Load balancing is the process of distributing computation and memory demands evenly across available hardware. In distributed model training, it helps improve efficiency and avoid bottlenecks on specific devices or nodes.

concepts.name: learning bottleneck
concepts.description: A limitation in model training that slows or weakens learning because the optimization signal is poor, noisy, or uninformative. In retrieval systems, it can arise when training examples do not provide sufficiently strong gradient updates.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a general training limitation from weak or noisy optimization signals, while the candidate is a specific retrieval-system cold-start failure mode; they are related but not the same concept.

concepts.name: deep neural networks
concepts.description: Deep neural networks are multi-layer machine learning models that learn hierarchical representations from data. In dense retrieval, they are used to map texts into continuous vector representations that can be compared for similarity.
