concepts.match_reason: Deep neural networks are a model class, while dense representation is the vector embedding produced/used in retrieval; related but not the same concept.

concepts.name: vocabulary mismatch
concepts.description: Vocabulary mismatch is the failure of two related texts to share the same words even when they are semantically connected. It is a key limitation of term-based retrieval methods because lexical overlap alone may not capture relevance.
concepts.match_reason: “Vocabulary mismatch” means texts do not share words despite being related, while “lexical overlap” is the degree of shared words; they are opposites, not the same concept.

concepts.name: representation learning
concepts.description: Representation learning is the process of learning useful feature representations from data, often with minimal manual feature engineering. In retrieval systems, it is used to learn embeddings that make relevant texts easy to match and irrelevant texts easy to separate.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is the general process of learning representations from data, while the candidate is a specific learned function that produces vector representations. Related, but not the same concept.

concepts.name: variance reduction framework
concepts.description: A theoretical framework for analyzing and reducing the variability of stochastic gradient estimates in optimization. It is used to explain how different sampling schemes affect gradient magnitude, variance, and training convergence.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a framework for analyzing and reducing variance, while the candidate is the variance itself, not the framework.

concepts.name: relevant document
concepts.description: A document that satisfies the information need expressed by a query. Retrieval models aim to assign higher similarity or ranking scores to relevant documents than to irrelevant ones.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a relevant document, while the candidate is a retriever model used to find documents; these are different concepts.

concepts.name: irrelevant document
concepts.description: A document that does not satisfy the information need expressed by a query. Retrieval training treats such documents as negatives to teach the model to separate them from relevant results.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is an irrelevant/negative document, while the candidate is a relevant document; they are opposites, not the same concept.

concepts.name: Learned embedding space
concepts.description: A vector space learned from data in which semantically related queries and documents are mapped close together. Retrieval scores are computed from distances or similarities in this space rather than from exact term overlap.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is the vector space itself, while the candidates are either a specific pre-trained variant or methods/functions that use such a space, not the same concept.

concepts.name: dense retrieval training
concepts.description: The process of optimizing a dense retrieval model so that relevant query-document pairs receive higher similarity scores than negative pairs. It typically trains representation encoders with sampled negatives to improve retrieval quality over sparse baselines.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is the training procedure for dense retrieval models, while the candidates are the retrieval method, model component, pretrained model, broader discriminative training, or embeddings—not the same specific concept.

concepts.name: uninformative negatives
concepts.description: Negative examples that provide weak training signal because they are too easy or otherwise not useful for distinguishing relevant from irrelevant items. They can bound gradient norms and limit effective learning in dense retrieval.
concepts.match_reason: Uninformative negatives are easy/weak negatives that give little training signal; none of the candidates denotes that specific concept, and hard negative passage is the opposite.

concepts.name: Gradient estimator
concepts.description: 用有限样本构造出来的梯度近似量，用来代替完整数据上的真实梯度进行优化更新。若设计得当，它可以保持无偏，并在计算上比完整梯度更便宜。

concepts.name: oracle distribution
concepts.description: An idealized probability distribution that yields the best theoretical behavior for a learning or sampling objective. It is typically defined in closed form by an optimization criterion, even when it is too expensive to compute exactly.

concepts.name: diminishing gradients
concepts.description: A situation in which gradient magnitudes become smaller over time or across samples, reducing their influence on parameter updates. Such gradients carry less learning signal and are often considered less informative for optimization.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is the phenomenon of gradients becoming smaller over time, while the candidates are gradient magnitude/variance or optimization methods, not that phenomenon itself.

concepts.name: multi-layer perceptron
concepts.description: A feedforward neural network composed of multiple layers of fully connected units. It is a standard neural architecture used for function approximation and classification tasks.

concepts.name: ANCE asynchronous training
concepts.description: A training scheme for dense retrieval models in which negative examples are refreshed asynchronously using the current state of the model. It separates the roles of updating model parameters and updating corpus representations so that training can use up-to-date hard negatives without pausing the main optimization loop.
concepts.match_reason: ANCE asynchronous training is a specific dense-retrieval training scheme with asynchronously refreshed negatives, while the candidate is the broader general process of dense retrieval training.

concepts.name: pairwise hinge loss
concepts.description: A ranking loss that encourages a positive item to score higher than a negative item by a margin. It is commonly used in learning-to-rank settings and yields vanishing gradients when the model already separates the pair well.
concepts.match_reason: Pairwise hinge loss is a specific ranking-form of hinge loss, while the candidate is the broader hinge loss concept.

concepts.name: 近似最近邻噪声对比估计
concepts.description: 一种用于密集检索训练的负样本构造方法，通过近似最近邻索引从整个语料中检索候选文档，并将模型当前检索到的高排名文档用作负样本。它旨在提供比局部小批量采样更困难、更有信息量的训练信号。
aliases: Approximate nearest neighbor Negative Contrastive Estimation
concepts.match_reason: 这是 ANCE/近似最近邻挖掘硬负样本的方法，不是批内负样本采样；候选都不是同一概念。

concepts.name: inference
concepts.description: The process of running a trained model over a corpus to compute updated document representations. In retrieval systems, this step is expensive because it may require encoding every document in the collection.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is the inference process itself, while the candidate is a specific component that performs document encoding and index refresh. They are related but not the same concept.

concepts.name: ANCE negatives
concepts.description: Negative training examples selected from an approximate nearest neighbor index in the ANCE retrieval method. They are chosen from documents that are close to the query under the current model, making them hard negatives for learning.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is the hard negatives used by ANCE, while candidate [0] is the ANCE method itself, not the negatives.

concepts.name: BM25 + Rand Neg
concepts.description: 一种混合负样本策略，将BM25负例与随机负例按1:1比例结合。它兼顾难负例和普通负例，以改善检索模型训练的稳定性和泛化能力。
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a hybrid negative-sampling strategy combining BM25 negatives and random negatives 1:1, which is not the same as either BM25 negative passages alone or Rand Neg alone.

concepts.name: MARCO passage training labels
concepts.description: 一种来自MS MARCO段落数据集的训练标注，用于监督文档或段落检索模型学习相关性。它们通常被用来训练第一阶段检索器，并影响模型对正负样本的区分能力。
concepts.match_reason: The candidate is an NLG benchmark built on MS MARCO, not the passage training labels used to supervise retrieval relevance.

concepts.name: max-pooling
concepts.description: An aggregation operation that outputs the largest value among a set of scores or activations. In retrieval, it can be used to combine passage-level scores into a single document-level score by keeping the strongest match.
concepts.match_reason: Max-pooling is the general aggregation operation, while MaxP is a specific long-document retrieval strategy that uses max over passage scores.

concepts.name: Faiss IndexFlatIP
concepts.description: A Faiss index that performs exact inner-product search over stored vectors using a flat, non-compressed representation. It is a simple and efficient index type for similarity search when inner product is the scoring function.
concepts.match_reason: Neither candidate denotes the specific Faiss IndexFlatIP exact inner-product flat index; one is a general approximate index type and the other is the FAISS library itself.

concepts.name: DPR checkpoints
concepts.description: Saved model parameters from a dense passage retrieval system. Such checkpoints can be used as initialization or warm-start models for further training or adaptation.
concepts.match_reason: The candidate is a generic model checkpoint, while DPR checkpoints are a specific kind of checkpoint for dense passage retrieval; general vs specific, not the same concept.

concepts.name: noise-contrastive estimation negatives
concepts.description: 一种基于噪声对比估计的负样本构造方式，用噪声分布采样的样本作为训练中的对比项。它用于让模型学习将真实相关样本与噪声样本分开，从而提高判别能力。
concepts.match_reason: INCOMING refers to the negative samples used in noise-contrastive estimation, while [0] is the NCE/negative contrastive estimation objective itself, not the negatives as a construction. None of the candidates matches this exact concept.

concepts.name: MS MARCO Dev
concepts.description: MS MARCO 数据集的开发集，用于评估问答和文档检索系统的排序性能。它通常以排名指标衡量模型在真实查询上的检索质量。
concepts.match_reason: MS MARCO Dev is the development/evaluation split, while the candidates are a task variant, training labels, or the overall dataset—not the dev set itself.

concepts.name: TREC DL Passage
concepts.description: TREC 深度学习任务中的段落检索评测子集，面向段落级相关性排序。它用于比较不同检索和重排序方法在段落检索上的效果。
concepts.match_reason: Incoming concept is the passage-ranking subset of the TREC Deep Learning benchmark, while candidate [0] is the broader TREC 2019 Deep Learning Track overall, not the same exact concept.

concepts.name: TREC DL Document
concepts.description: TREC 深度学习任务中的文档检索评测子集，面向文档级相关性排序。它用于评估系统在较长文本单位上的召回与排序能力。
concepts.match_reason: “TREC DL Document”指文档级评测子集；候选中只有[1]是更宽泛的整个赛道，且[0]是段落级子集，均不等同。

concepts.name: sparse methods
concepts.description: Retrieval methods that represent text with sparse features such as terms or lexical weights. They are often efficient and competitive in document retrieval, but can be outperformed by strong dense retrieval approaches in some settings.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is the general class of sparse retrieval methods, while candidate 0 is only a specific bag-of-words matching approach and the others are different concepts.

concepts.name: document retrieval
concepts.description: The task of ranking whole documents by their relevance to a query. It is a standard information retrieval setting where dense and sparse retrieval methods are commonly compared.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is the general task of document-level retrieval, while the candidates are either broader ranking, a retrieved item, or the dense-retrieval variant/training—not the same concept.

concepts.name: global negatives
concepts.description: Negative examples drawn from a large or global candidate pool rather than only local in-batch samples. They expose the model to harder distinctions during training and can improve retrieval quality.
concepts.match_reason: Global negatives are a broader negative-sampling source from a large candidate pool; the listed candidates are specific negative types, especially in-batch negatives which are local, not the same concept.

concepts.name: term-level interactions
concepts.description: Direct interactions between individual query terms and document terms during matching. Such interactions can improve fine-grained relevance modeling in search systems.

concepts.name: Siamese network
concepts.description: A neural architecture that encodes two inputs with shared parameters into comparable representations. It is commonly used for matching tasks because it allows efficient similarity computation after separate encoding.

concepts.name: pre-computable document encoding
concepts.description: A retrieval property in which document representations can be computed ahead of time and stored for later search. This reduces online computation and makes large-scale retrieval faster.

concepts.name: online latency
concepts.description: The time required for a retrieval system to answer a query at serving time. Lower latency is important for practical deployment and real-time search performance.

concepts.name: T5-11B
concepts.description: 一种参数规模很大的文本到文本转换模型，基于统一的序列到序列框架处理多种自然语言任务。它常作为开放域问答等任务中的生成式基线模型。
concepts.match_reason: T5-11B 是 T5 的一个具体大模型变体，而候选 0 只是泛指 T5，不是同一概念。

concepts.name: 支持性段落
concepts.description: 从外部知识源中检索出的、与问题相关的文本段落，用于为答案提供证据。系统通常先检索这些段落，再在其基础上进行答案预测。
concepts.match_reason: “支持性段落”指检索得到的证据段落；候选项中只有[0]是支持文档，粒度不同（文档 vs 段落），其余是训练样本或方法，不是同一概念。

concepts.name: 生成式编码器-解码器模型
concepts.description: 一种条件生成模型，先将输入编码为内部表示，再由解码器逐步生成输出文本。它在问答中可以根据问题和检索到的证据直接生成答案。
concepts.match_reason: 候选0只是编码器-解码器架构，不等同于“生成式编码器-解码器模型”这一具体生成模型概念；其余候选也只是更泛化或不同任务。

concepts.name: global normalization
concepts.description: A training or inference strategy that normalizes scores over all candidate answer spans associated with the correct answer. It is used to handle cases where multiple spans can express the same answer and to improve learning from span-level supervision.

concepts.name: BiLSTM
concepts.description: A bidirectional long short-term memory network that processes a sequence in both forward and backward directions. It is often used to encode text for ranking, classification, and other sequence modeling tasks.
concepts.match_reason: BiLSTM is bidirectional, while candidate [1] is plain LSTM and candidate [0] is a larger BiLSTM-based model with extra ELMo and attention components, so none denote the exact same concept.

concepts.name: Wikipedia graph
concepts.description: A graph structure derived from Wikipedia entities and links that encodes relationships among topics and pages. It can be used as auxiliary knowledge to support document retrieval and reasoning.

concepts.name: Wikidata graph
concepts.description: A graph structure derived from Wikidata entities and relations that represents structured factual knowledge. It can be used to provide additional information for retrieval and question answering.
concepts.match_reason: Wikidata graph and Wikipedia graph are related knowledge graphs, but they are derived from different sources and denote different concepts.

concepts.name: weak supervision
concepts.description: A training regime that relies on indirect, noisy, or incomplete supervision rather than fully annotated labels. In retrieval models, it can use question-answer pairs to learn useful matching behavior.
concepts.match_reason: Weak supervision is a broad training regime with noisy or incomplete labels; the candidates are specific retrieval/passage supervision variants, not the same concept.

concepts.name: question-answer pair
concepts.description: A paired example consisting of a question and its corresponding answer. Such pairs can serve as weak supervision for training retrieval and reading systems.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a question paired with its answer, while the candidate is a question paired with a passage; these are different training pair types.

concepts.name: ELI5
concepts.description: A question answering dataset containing long, explanatory answers to open-ended questions. It is designed to encourage abstractive generation rather than short span extraction.

concepts.name: abstractive model
concepts.description: A model that generates a natural-language answer by paraphrasing, synthesizing, or composing information rather than copying an exact span from the source. Abstractive models are useful when the answer is not directly present as a contiguous text segment.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a model type for abstractive generation, while the candidates are a QA setting, a broader generative model category, and an answer format; none denotes the exact same concept.

concepts.name: large pretrained generative model
concepts.description: A generative language model that has been pretrained on large amounts of text before being adapted to downstream tasks. Such models can generate fluent answers and often benefit from substantial prior linguistic and factual knowledge.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a broad pretrained generative language model, while the candidates are either too general, too specific, or not explicitly the same pretrained generative model concept.

concepts.name: SpanSeqGen
concepts.description: A sequence generation approach that operates over spans extracted from text. It is used to generate answers or text conditioned on selected span evidence.
concepts.match_reason: SpanSeqGen is a span-conditioned sequence generation approach, not an answer span itself or a span scoring method.

concepts.name: Generative Pretrained Transformer 3
concepts.description: A large autoregressive language model trained to predict the next token in text. It can perform tasks in a few-shot setting by conditioning on examples in the prompt.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is GPT-3, but none of the candidates specifically denote GPT-3; the closest ones are broader GPT/Transformer language model concepts or GPT-2.

concepts.name: Term frequency
concepts.description: The count of how often a term appears in a document or passage. It is a basic statistic used by many information retrieval ranking functions.

concepts.name: Apache Lucene
concepts.description: 一个开源的信息检索软件库，提供索引、搜索和相关排序功能。它常被用作实现经典稀疏检索方法的底层工具。

concepts.name: SpaCy
concepts.description: 一个用于自然语言处理的开源软件库，提供分词、词性标注、命名实体识别等功能。它常用于对文本进行快速的预处理和语言分析。

concepts.name: normalization
concepts.description: Normalization is a text preprocessing step used during answer evaluation to make predicted and reference answers comparable. It typically lowercases text and removes articles, punctuation, and extra whitespace.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is text-answer normalization for evaluation, while the candidate is a score-normalization strategy over answer spans; they are different concepts.

concepts.name: closed book T5
concepts.description: 一种不依赖外部检索上下文的T5问答设置或模型，用参数化知识直接回答问题。它主要依靠模型内部存储的信息，而不是显式检索到的文档证据。
concepts.match_reason: 闭卷T5是不依赖外部检索、直接用参数化知识回答的设置；候选项分别是抽取式问答和检索增强生成，都不是同一概念。

concepts.name: end-to-end learning
concepts.description: A training approach in which all components of a system are optimized jointly with a single objective. In question answering systems, this allows retrieval and answer generation to adapt to each other during training.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a joint training approach, while the candidate is a question answering system architecture; related but not the same concept.

concepts.name: latent retrieval
concepts.description: Latent retrieval is a retrieval method in which the relevant evidence is selected through latent variables rather than explicit supervision at retrieval time. It is used in weakly supervised question answering to improve answer finding when direct retrieval labels are unavailable.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a retrieval method that uses latent variables, while the candidates are latent variable/model concepts themselves, not the retrieval method.

concepts.name: weakly supervised question answering
concepts.description: A question answering setting in which training supervision is indirect or incomplete, such as answer labels without explicit supporting evidence or reasoning steps. Methods for this setting must learn to infer the latent evidence or reasoning needed to produce the answer.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a specific question answering setting, while the candidate is the broader training regime of weak supervision.

concepts.name: knowledge-guided text retrieval and reading
concepts.description: A question answering approach that uses external knowledge to guide both document retrieval and the subsequent reading of retrieved text. The goal is to improve open-domain question answering by selecting more relevant evidence before answer extraction or generation.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a specific knowledge-guided QA approach that uses external knowledge to improve both retrieval and reading, which is not the same as any candidate’s more general QA pipeline or retrieval task.

concepts.name: AmbigQA
concepts.description: A dataset and benchmark for answering ambiguous open-domain questions. It evaluates whether a system can recognize that a question may have multiple valid interpretations and provide answers for the intended or possible meanings.
concepts.match_reason: AmbigQA is a dataset for ambiguous open-domain questions, while CoQA is a conversational reading comprehension dataset; they are different benchmarks.

concepts.name: Okapi
concepts.description: 一种经典的信息检索系统，采用词项统计和相关性建模来对文档进行排序。它在检索评测中被广泛用作基线系统，并影响了后续检索模型的发展。
concepts.match_reason: Okapi 指经典检索模型/系统（常指 Okapi BM25 相关家族），而 Apache Lucene 是实现信息检索功能的软件库，不是同一概念。

concepts.name: oLMpics
concepts.description: 一个用于评测语言模型预训练所捕获知识的基准集合。它通过设计不同类型的语言任务来检验预训练模型是否学到了可迁移的语言和常识信息。
aliases: on what language model pre-training captures
concepts.match_reason: oLMpics 是针对预训练语言模型知识的特定基准集合；候选 0 只是泛指一般的 NLP 基准套件，不是同一个具体概念。

concepts.name: R3
concepts.description: 一种用于开放域问答的检索与阅读联合模型，结合排序器和阅读器来定位并抽取答案。它通过强化学习式的训练方式优化检索排序和答案生成或抽取的协同效果。
concepts.match_reason: R3 describes a specific joint retrieval-and-reading model trained with reinforcement learning, which is not the same as any listed candidate; the closest ones are broader task or component-level concepts.

concepts.name: globally normalized BERT model
concepts.description: A BERT-based question answering model that assigns probabilities over answers using a global normalization over competing candidates. This approach is intended to make prediction more coherent across multiple passages or answer options.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a BERT QA model with global normalization over competing answers, while the candidate is a multi-passage BERT model that aggregates evidence across passages; these are related but not the same concept.

concepts.name: BERTserini
concepts.description: An end-to-end question answering system that combines BERT with the Anserini retrieval framework. It is designed to support open-domain question answering by retrieving relevant passages and then extracting answers from them.
concepts.match_reason: BERTserini is a specific open-domain QA system using BERT plus Anserini, while the candidates are either a generic end-to-end QA framework or a different BERT-based model.

concepts.name: 秩亏性
concepts.description: 矩阵或更新在有效秩上显著低于其维度的性质。在线性化或参数更新分析中，它常用于解释为什么低秩近似能够以较少参数捕获适配所需的主要变化。
concepts.match_reason: “秩亏性”指矩阵/更新呈现低秩的性质；“低秩分解矩阵”是把矩阵表示成低秩因子乘积的方法/形式，不是同一概念。

concepts.name: task-specific parameters
concepts.description: A small set of parameters learned for a particular downstream task and stored separately from the base model. They allow a pre-trained model to be adapted without keeping a full updated copy for every task.

concepts.name: external modules
concepts.description: Additional learned components attached to a pre-trained model to support a new task. They provide task adaptation without modifying all of the original model parameters.

concepts.name: 低内在维度
concepts.description: 一种表征复杂系统所需自由度很少的性质，即使外在表示空间很高维，实际可学习的变化也可能主要落在低维子空间中。它常用于解释为什么过参数化模型在适配或学习时只需要少量有效方向。

concepts.name: 低内在秩
concepts.description: 一种假设或性质，认为模型在适配过程中的参数变化可以用低秩结构近似表示。它意味着原本高维的权重更新可以压缩为少量基向量的组合，从而便于高效训练与部署。
concepts.match_reason: “低内在秩”指参数更新可由低秩结构近似的性质；候选项中没有同一概念，1 是低内在维度，3 是 LoRA 方法，2 只是秩亏性质。

concepts.name: 密集层
concepts.description: 一种神经网络层，其中每个输入单元通常与每个输出单元相连接。它是最常见的线性变换结构之一，广泛用于表示和转换特征。
concepts.match_reason: “密集层”是全连接神经网络层；候选项都是残差连接或 Transformer 结构组件，并非同一概念。

concepts.name: 自注意力模块
concepts.description: Transformer 中用于让序列位置之间相互交互的一类核心子结构。它通过查询、键和值之间的匹配来计算上下文表示，并通常包含输出投影。
concepts.match_reason: “自注意力模块”是 Transformer 中的一个子结构，而候选项里没有与之完全同义的单独模块；[3] 只是机制本身，[2] 则是更大的块。

concepts.name: 查询投影矩阵
concepts.description: 自注意力模块中把输入映射到查询表示的线性变换矩阵。查询表示随后与键表示一起用于计算注意力权重。
concepts.match_reason: “查询投影矩阵”是生成查询表示的线性变换参数，不是查询向量本身，也不是整个自注意力模块。

concepts.name: 键投影矩阵
concepts.description: 自注意力模块中把输入映射到键表示的线性变换矩阵。键表示与查询表示配对，用于决定注意力分配给哪些位置。
concepts.match_reason: 候选中只有“查询投影矩阵”是同类概念，但它对应的是查询而不是键；其余都是注意力模块/计算方式/头数/查询本身，不是同一个矩阵。

concepts.name: 值投影矩阵
concepts.description: 自注意力模块中把输入映射到值表示的线性变换矩阵。值表示会按照注意力权重加权汇聚，形成上下文相关的输出。
concepts.match_reason: 候选中只有查询/键投影矩阵、自注意力模块和多头注意力，没有“值投影矩阵”这一相同概念。

concepts.name: 输出投影矩阵
concepts.description: 自注意力模块末端将注意力结果映射回模型表示空间的线性变换矩阵。它通常把多头注意力的拼接结果转换为后续层可使用的表示。
concepts.match_reason: “输出投影矩阵”是多头注意力末端的线性映射层，不等同于任何候选中的值/键/查询投影，也不是“多头注意力”或“注意力头数”本身。

concepts.name: 预训练自回归语言模型
concepts.description: 在大规模语料上预先训练的生成式语言模型，按从左到右的方式建模序列概率。它可以在下游任务上继续适配，用于条件文本生成等应用。
concepts.match_reason: 候选中没有同时表达“预训练”且“自回归/从左到右生成”的同一概念；0缺少预训练，3又过于泛化为所有预训练神经语言模型。

concepts.name: 条件文本生成
concepts.description: 一种生成任务，模型在给定输入条件或提示的情况下生成目标文本。它通过最大化条件概率来学习从上下文到输出序列的映射。
concepts.match_reason: “条件文本生成”指的是一种条件生成任务/范式；候选项中没有完全对应的同一概念，0 和 2 都是具体模型类型而非该任务本身。

concepts.name: Natural language to SQL
concepts.description: A task that maps a natural language question or request to an executable SQL statement. It is used to translate user intent expressed in ordinary language into database queries.
concepts.match_reason: Natural language to SQL is a semantic parsing task that converts questions into SQL queries; natural language instruction is a prompt/constraint style for telling a model what to do, so they denote different concepts.

concepts.name: context-target pair
concepts.description: A supervised learning example consisting of an input context and a corresponding target output. The pair represents one input-output training instance used to learn a mapping from inputs to outputs.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a generic supervised input-output training example, while the candidates are different constructs: a key-value storage/retrieval pair and a two-segment pretraining input pair.

concepts.name: natural language query
concepts.description: A question or request expressed in ordinary human language. In query translation tasks, it serves as the input that is mapped to a formal query language such as SQL.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is the natural-language input itself, while the candidate is the translation task from that input to SQL; they are related but not the same concept.

concepts.name: SQL command
concepts.description: A statement written in Structured Query Language for retrieving, modifying, or managing data in a relational database. In translation tasks, it is the formal output corresponding to a natural language query.

concepts.name: article summary
concepts.description: A shorter text that condenses the main content of an article. It is used as the target output in summarization tasks.

concepts.name: conditional language modeling objective
concepts.description: A training objective that maximizes the log-likelihood of each target token conditioned on an input and the previously generated target tokens. It is used to fit a model so that it assigns high probability to the correct output sequence given the context.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a conditional sequence-generation objective given an input plus previous target tokens; none of the candidates denotes that exact objective, and the closest ones are either unconditional left-to-right LM or a broader language modeling/log-likelihood term.

concepts.name: parameter-efficient adaptation
concepts.description: A model adaptation strategy that learns only a small number of additional or task-specific parameters instead of updating the full model. It reduces the storage and computation needed to adapt large pre-trained models to many downstream tasks.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is the general idea of parameter-efficient model adaptation, while the candidates are either a specific method (LoRA), a component of such methods (task-specific parameters), or the opposite approach (full fine-tuning).

concepts.name: task-specific parameter increment
concepts.description: The change applied to a pre-trained model’s parameters to specialize it for a particular task. It represents the adaptation offset added to the base weights and can be encoded by a smaller set of trainable parameters.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is the task-specific parameter change/offset itself, while the candidates are either the learned task-specific parameters or the broader adaptation strategy; none denotes exactly that increment.

concepts.name: Bottleneck dimension
concepts.description: A bottleneck dimension is the size of the narrow hidden layer in a bottleneck architecture. In adapter modules, a small bottleneck dimension reduces the number of parameters and floating-point operations, but does not eliminate the extra sequential computation they introduce.

concepts.name: Online inference
concepts.description: Online inference is the process of running a trained model in real time for individual requests or very small batches. It prioritizes low latency and responsiveness, so additional sequential computation can have a noticeable performance impact.

concepts.name: inference latency
concepts.description: The time required for a model to produce an output during inference. It depends on model architecture and computational overhead, and extra modules such as adapters can increase it.

concepts.name: dense layer
concepts.description: A neural network layer whose outputs are computed by matrix multiplication with a learned weight matrix. Dense layers are common in deep learning models and are a natural target for low-rank or other parameter-efficient adaptation methods.

concepts.name: weight matrix
concepts.description: A matrix of learned parameters that determines the linear transformation performed by a neural network layer. During adaptation, such matrices can be updated directly or modified through constrained update forms.
concepts.match_reason: A weight matrix is a parameter used by a dense layer, not the same concept as the layer itself.

concepts.name: low-rank update
concepts.description: A weight update represented by factors of limited rank rather than by a full unconstrained matrix. This reduces the number of trainable parameters while allowing the model to adapt.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is the low-rank form of a weight update itself, while candidate [0] is a specific adaptation method that uses such updates and candidate [1] is a related rank property, not the update concept.

concepts.name: intrinsic dimension
concepts.description: The effective dimensionality of the subspace in which a model can adapt successfully. A low intrinsic dimension suggests that useful task adaptation may be possible in a much smaller parameter subspace than the full model space.

concepts.name: intrinsic rank
concepts.description: The effective rank of the update needed to adapt a model to a new task. A low intrinsic rank means that the adaptation can be expressed well with a low-rank matrix decomposition.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is about the effective rank of an adaptation update, which is not the same as intrinsic dimension, and low-rank update is a specific method rather than the concept itself.

concepts.name: 全量微调
concepts.description: 一种对预训练模型的大部分或全部参数进行训练的适配方式。它通常具有较强表达能力，但需要更新的参数更多，计算和存储成本也更高。
concepts.match_reason: “全量微调”指训练预训练模型的大部分或全部参数；候选中没有这个特指概念，其他要么是广义微调流程，要么是参数高效微调方法（适配器、LoRA）或模型本身。

concepts.name: 高斯初始化
concepts.description: 一种参数初始化方法，按高斯分布随机采样初始值。它常用于让可训练参数在训练开始时具有受控的随机性。

concepts.name: 零初始化
concepts.description: 一种将参数初始值全部设为零的初始化方式。它常用于确保某些增量参数在训练开始时不改变原模型输出。

concepts.name: 推理延迟
concepts.description: 模型在执行推理时产生的额外时间开销。它通常由额外计算步骤或更复杂的参数结构引起，降低推理吞吐或增加响应时间。

concepts.name: 权重合并
concepts.description: 将额外学习到的参数增量直接加到模型原始权重中，得到一个可直接用于推理的完整权重矩阵。这样可以避免在推理过程中引入额外分支或额外延迟。
concepts.match_reason: “权重合并”是把新增参数增量合并进原始权重的操作；候选项“预训练模型权重”只是模型已有的权重本身，不是合并这一概念。

concepts.name: DeBERTa
concepts.description: DeBERTa is a pre-trained transformer language model that improves language understanding through enhanced attention and disentangled representations. It is used as a backbone for downstream task evaluation and adaptation.
concepts.match_reason: DeBERTa is a distinct BERT-family model with its own architecture, not the same concept as BERT, RoBERTa, or BERT pre-training.

concepts.name: WikiSQL
concepts.description: WikiSQL is a dataset for natural language to SQL question answering. It is used to evaluate systems that generate SQL queries from natural language input.

concepts.name: SAMSum
concepts.description: SAMSum is a dataset of dialogue summaries used to evaluate conversation summarization systems. It provides paired conversations and human-written summaries for training and testing summarization models.

concepts.name: NVIDIA Tesla V100
concepts.description: NVIDIA Tesla V100 is a GPU accelerator used for high-performance machine learning training and inference. It provides the compute and memory resources needed to run large neural models.
concepts.match_reason: NVIDIA Tesla V100 and NVIDIA P100 are different GPU models, not the same concept.

concepts.name: 冻结层
concepts.description: 一种部分微调策略，其中某些层保持参数不变而不参与更新。它常与只更新少数层配合使用，以减少训练开销并保留已有表示。
concepts.match_reason: “冻结层”指部分层不参与更新的微调策略；候选是“低秩适配（LoRA）”，属于另一种参数高效微调方法，不是同一概念。

concepts.name: FTTop2
concepts.description: 一种只调整模型最后两层、同时冻结其他层的微调基线方法。它属于部分微调的一种实现方式，常用于作为对比基线。

concepts.name: weight sharding
concepts.description: 一种把模型权重切分成多个分片并分布存放或计算的技术。它常与模型并行配合使用，以支持更大规模模型的训练和部署。
concepts.match_reason: Weight sharding splits weights across shards; weight sharing reuses the same weights in multiple places. They are different concepts.

concepts.name: RoBERTa-base
concepts.description: RoBERTa-base is a base-sized pretrained transformer language model in the RoBERTa family. It is commonly used as a backbone for downstream natural language processing tasks and can be adapted with full fine-tuning or parameter-efficient methods.
concepts.match_reason: RoBERTa-base is the specific base-sized checkpoint, while candidate 0 refers to RoBERTa more generally rather than that exact model variant.

concepts.name: AdptH
concepts.description: AdptH is a parameter-efficient adaptation method for tuning a pretrained model with a limited number of additional trainable parameters. It is evaluated as an alternative to other adaptation strategies under the same model backbone.
concepts.match_reason: AdptH appears to be a specific adaptation method, while the candidates are a different named method, a generic strategy, a contrasting method, or a component rather than the same concept.

concepts.name: DeBXXL
concepts.description: DeBXXL is a very large pretrained model variant used in benchmark comparisons. It is evaluated both with full fine-tuning and with LoRA-based adaptation to compare performance and parameter cost.

concepts.name: Prefix-embedding tuning
concepts.description: A parameter-efficient tuning method that adds special trainable tokens to the input sequence by learning only their word embeddings. These tokens are placed before or within the prompt so they can steer the model without updating the main network weights.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a parameter-efficient tuning method that learns trainable prefix tokens/embeddings, while the candidates are either a broader prompt-optimization approach or a representation of a text prefix, not the same specific method.

concepts.name: Prefixing
concepts.description: A way of placing special trainable tokens at the beginning of the input prompt in prefix-embedding tuning. The location of these tokens can affect model performance.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is the specific placement of trainable tokens at the beginning of a prompt, while candidate 0 is the broader prefix-embedding tuning method and candidate 1 is a different notion of embedding representation.

concepts.name: Infixing
concepts.description: A way of placing special trainable tokens after the prompt in prefix-embedding tuning rather than before it. Like prefixing, it changes how added tokens influence the model’s computation.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a specific token-placement variant (after the prompt), while candidate 1 is the broader tuning method and candidate 0 is the opposite placement.

concepts.name: Prefix-layer tuning
concepts.description: An extension of prefix-embedding tuning that learns trainable activations after each Transformer layer for special tokens. Instead of updating only embeddings, it replaces the intermediate activations with learned values throughout the network.
concepts.match_reason: Prefix-layer tuning is a different method that learns activations across layers, not just trainable input embeddings like prefix-embedding tuning.

concepts.name: Activation
concepts.description: The numerical output produced by a neural network unit or layer after applying its computations. Activations serve as the intermediate representations passed from one layer to the next.

concepts.name: AdapterH
concepts.description: The original Houlsby-style adapter design in which adapter layers are inserted between the self-attention module or the MLP module and the following residual connection. It uses a small bottleneck network with two fully connected layers and a nonlinear activation.
concepts.match_reason: AdapterH is a specific Houlsby-style adapter design, while the candidate is the broader generic concept of an adapter layer.

concepts.name: AdapterDrop
concepts.description: An adapter variant that improves efficiency by dropping some adapter layers. It reduces the number of active adapter modules while retaining the adapter-based fine-tuning framework.
concepts.match_reason: AdapterDrop is a specific adapter-dropping variant, while the candidates are either a different adapter placement method or generic adapter concepts.

concepts.name: training objective
concepts.description: A training objective is the learning target that a model is optimized to satisfy during training. It shapes which outputs the model is rewarded for producing, and in some cases it can incentivize false but statistically likely answers.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is generic, while the candidate is a specific type of training objective (conditional language modeling).

concepts.name: deceptive model
concepts.description: A deceptive model is a model that can generate plausible false statements in ways that are not easily identifiable. Such a model can be used for disinformation or fraud because its outputs may appear credible while being untrue.
concepts.match_reason: A deceptive model is the source/system that generates plausible falsehoods, while a false statement is the false output itself; they are related but not the same concept.

concepts.name: false and informative answers
concepts.description: Answers that contain incorrect information while still appearing useful or detailed. Such responses can be especially deceptive because they combine plausibility with factual error.

concepts.name: automated metric
concepts.description: A computational measure used to estimate model quality without requiring manual scoring each time. Such a metric can be trained or calibrated on human judgments so that it predicts those judgments on new cases.

concepts.name: 真实性目标
concepts.description: 一种评估目标，要求陈述与现实世界的字面事实一致。只有在真实世界中可被公开可靠证据支持的事实才被视为真实，而仅在某种信仰体系或传统中成立的说法被视为虚假。

concepts.name: 标量真实性分数
concepts.description: 一种将陈述的真实性表示为区间 [0, 1] 内数值的方法，可解释为该陈述为真的概率。它允许对含糊或不准确程度不同的生成内容进行连续评分，并可根据阈值转换为更易解释的判断。

concepts.name: target model
concepts.description: A target model is the model being evaluated or attacked in an adversarial testing setup. Its behavior determines whether a generated question is considered effective at inducing incorrect answers.
concepts.match_reason: A target model is the model under evaluation/attack, while a target token is an output token in seq2seq training; they are different concepts.

concepts.name: combined results
concepts.description: Combined results are evaluation results reported by pooling multiple subsets of test items into a single aggregate. This approach gives an overall summary across categories that may also be analyzed separately.

concepts.name: adversarial questions
concepts.description: Adversarial questions are questions constructed to provoke incorrect or unreliable answers from a model. They are used in evaluation to probe model weaknesses rather than to sample typical user queries.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a type of adversarially designed questions, while candidate 0 is a broader construction procedure and candidate 1 is a non-filtered subset; none denotes the same concept.

concepts.name: external validation
concepts.description: External validation is the process of having independent reviewers assess the quality or correctness of an evaluation. It is used to estimate how often different judges might disagree with the original assessments.

concepts.name: GPT-J
concepts.description: An open autoregressive language model in the GPT-Neo/J family. It follows the GPT-style transformer design and is trained on a different data mixture from GPT-3.
concepts.match_reason: GPT-J is a different open model in the GPT-Neo/J family, not the same concept as GPT-Neo or GPT-3.

concepts.name: 真零样本设置
concepts.description: 一种更严格的零样本评测方式，不仅不使用目标任务示例，也不对提示和超参数进行任何基于目标任务示例的调优。它旨在确保比较对象完全没有利用该任务数据进行间接适配。
concepts.match_reason: 候选0是一般的零样本学习设置；“真零样本设置”更严格，连基于目标任务示例的提示/超参数调优也禁止，二者不是同一概念。

concepts.name: 真少样本学习
concepts.description: 一种严格的少样本评测定义，要求模型在使用少量示例时不对这些示例进行额外的调参或间接利用。它强调对任务适应过程的控制，以避免评测结果受到数据泄漏或调优偏差影响。
concepts.match_reason: “真少样本学习”指的是一种更严格的少样本评测定义，强调不对少量示例进行额外调参或间接利用；候选中没有对应的同一概念，0只是一般少样本学习，4是单样本，1/2是零样本。

concepts.name: QA prompt
concepts.description: 一种用于问答任务的提示模板，通常包含与待测任务风格不同的琐事问题，用来引导模型生成答案。它可以作为通用的问答输入格式，并适用于多种模型家族和规模。
concepts.match_reason: 这是用于问答任务的提示模板/输入格式，不是“问答”任务本身，也不是生成式或抽取式问答模型。

concepts.name: Helpful prompt
concepts.description: 一种倾向于鼓励模型给出更真实回答的提示。它通过提示措辞影响模型的输出行为，使模型更可能给出有帮助且诚实的回答。
concepts.match_reason: “Helpful prompt”是通过措辞引导模型更真实、更有帮助地回答的提示；“QA prompt”是用于问答任务的输入模板，概念不同。

concepts.name: Harmful prompt
concepts.description: 一种倾向于鼓励模型给出较不真实回答的提示。它通过提示措辞影响模型的输出行为，用于检验模型对不同指令风格的敏感性。
concepts.match_reason: “Harmful prompt”指倾向于引导模型给出较不真实的回答；候选[0]“Helpful prompt”是相反概念，候选[1]是问答模板，均不相同。

concepts.name: few-shot benchmark
concepts.description: A benchmark evaluated using a small number of in-context examples before the test item. Performance on such benchmarks reflects how well a model generalizes from limited demonstrations.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming term is a benchmark/dataset evaluated in a few-shot setting, while candidate 0 is the evaluation setting itself, not the benchmark.

concepts.name: GPT-judge
concepts.description: 一种用于自动评估答案真伪的指标或判别器。它是经过微调的 GPT-3-6.7B 模型，用来将问题答案分类为真或假，从而作为人工评估的替代或近似。
concepts.match_reason: GPT-judge 是基于 GPT-3 的微调判别器/评估器，不等同于通用的 GPT-3 基座模型。

concepts.name: non-imitative falsehood
concepts.description: A false answer produced by a model for reasons other than imitation, such as the syntax or style of the question. These falsehoods are not incentivized by the model’s training objective and arise from non-imitative weaknesses.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a specific kind of falsehood caused by non-imitative weaknesses, while the candidates are either a broader false statement, the contrasting imitative falsehood, or the general property of truthfulness.

concepts.name: weakness
concepts.description: A property of a model that leads it to perform poorly on a task. In this sense, a weakness is any model characteristic that causes falsehoods or other failures in task performance.

concepts.name: imitative weakness
concepts.description: A model property that causes falsehoods through imitative behavior. It leads the model to produce imitative falsehoods, where incorrect answers arise from copying or reinforcing erroneous patterns.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a model property/cause, while the candidates are either a broader general weakness or the resulting falsehood; none denotes the same concept.

concepts.name: informativeness
concepts.description: Informativeness is the degree to which a response contains useful, relevant content for answering a question. An informative answer may be valuable even if it is not fully correct, but it should not be empty or evasive.

concepts.name: generation task
concepts.description: A generation task is an evaluation setting in which a model produces free-form text as an answer. It is used to assess qualities such as correctness, usefulness, and fluency in open-ended responses.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is an evaluation setting for free-form text generation, while the candidates are a generative model and a specific generation-based QA approach, not the same concept.

concepts.name: paraphrase
concepts.description: A reworded version of a question that preserves its meaning while changing its wording. Paraphrases are used to test whether a model’s behavior depends on exact surface form or on deeper semantic content.

concepts.name: semantic weakness
concepts.description: A failure mode in which a model mishandles meaning-level content rather than surface form. These weaknesses are harder to rule out because they can persist across paraphrases and different question forms.
concepts.match_reason: Semantic weakness is a meaning-level failure mode; the candidates are broader general weakness or a different non-imitative/imitative distinction, not the same concept.

concepts.name: natural language rationale
concepts.description: A step-by-step explanation expressed in natural language that connects a problem statement to a final answer. It can help a model perform reasoning tasks by making intermediate deductions explicit.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is an explanatory step-by-step rationale used to show intermediate reasoning, while the candidate is a task instruction telling the model what to do and how to format the output.

concepts.name: neuro-symbolic methods
concepts.description: Approaches that combine neural network models with symbolic representations or algorithms. They are often used to improve reasoning by integrating learned language understanding with formal computation.

concepts.name: formal language
concepts.description: A precisely defined symbolic language with explicit syntax and semantics, used to represent structured information and reasoning steps. In reasoning systems, formal languages can provide unambiguous intermediate representations for computation.

concepts.name: rationale-augmented training
concepts.description: 一种在训练或微调中加入推理说明或理由的学习方式。它依赖高质量的 rationales 来帮助模型学习更可解释、也更适合推理的映射关系。

concepts.name: intermediate steps
concepts.description: 推理或解决复杂问题时使用的中间阶段。它们把一个多步问题分解为若干更小的子步骤，以便逐步完成计算或推断。

concepts.name: debugging
concepts.description: 对错误推理或错误输出进行检查、定位和修正的过程。它常借助可解释的中间过程来识别问题出现在何处。

concepts.name: solution
concepts.description: 为问题给出的完整解答或结果。它通常包含从条件到结论的最终推导或计算结果。

concepts.name: density
concepts.description: 密度是单位体积内所含物质质量的物理量，常用于比较不同物体在相同体积下有多“重”。密度大小会影响物体在液体中的浮沉行为，密度小于液体时通常更容易漂浮。

concepts.name: screen pass
concepts.description: 屏风式传球是一种美式足球战术传球，接球手通常先向外侧移动或利用掩护，再接到短传。它常用于通过跑动和掩护创造接球空间。

concepts.name: NFC Championship Game
concepts.description: NFC冠军赛是美国国家橄榄球联合会季后赛中的冠军决定性比赛，用于决出代表NFC参加超级碗的球队。它是美国职业橄榄球赛季后期的重要赛事之一。

concepts.name: CSQA
concepts.description: A commonsense question answering benchmark that tests whether a system can answer questions requiring everyday world knowledge. It is used to measure commonsense reasoning rather than pure factual recall.

concepts.name: StrategyQA
concepts.description: A question answering benchmark that requires implicit reasoning and multi-step inference to determine the answer. It is designed to test strategic commonsense reasoning over questions that are not answered by simple lookup.
concepts.match_reason: StrategyQA is a distinct benchmark for implicit, multi-step commonsense reasoning; CSQA is a different commonsense QA benchmark, not the same dataset.

concepts.name: Date Understanding
concepts.description: A benchmark that asks questions involving dates and calendar reasoning. It evaluates a system's ability to reason about temporal relationships, day counts, and date-based transformations.

concepts.name: Sports Understanding
concepts.description: A benchmark that requires reasoning about sports-related situations and rules. It evaluates whether a system can understand and infer outcomes from sports descriptions.

concepts.name: Last Letter Concatenation
concepts.description: A symbolic reasoning task in which the last letters of words are extracted and combined according to the problem instructions. It is used to test a model's ability to follow simple formal transformations rather than rely on world knowledge.

concepts.name: Coin Flip
concepts.description: A state-tracking reasoning task in which the orientation of a coin is updated after a sequence of flips. It tests whether a system can correctly track a changing state over multiple operations.

concepts.name: SayCan
concepts.description: A robot instruction-following framework that combines language understanding with action planning. It maps a user's natural-language request into a sequence of robot actions that can accomplish the goal.

concepts.name: LaMDA
concepts.description: LaMDA is a large language model family designed for conversational and general language understanding tasks. It is available in multiple parameter scales, allowing performance to be compared across model sizes.
concepts.match_reason: LaMDA is a specific Google model family; the candidates are either a different model family (PaLM) or a generic class of models, so none denote the same concept.

concepts.name: UL2
concepts.description: UL2 is a large language model architecture and model family used for evaluation on language tasks. A specific 20B-parameter version is referenced as one of the tested models.

concepts.name: majority final answer
concepts.description: Majority final answer is an aggregation strategy that chooses the answer appearing most often across many generated outputs. It can improve robustness by leveraging repeated sampled reasoning traces or candidate solutions.

concepts.name: randomly shuffled order of exemplars
concepts.description: A randomly shuffled order of exemplars is a presentation order in which demonstration examples are rearranged randomly before being shown to a model. It is used to measure whether output depends on exemplar ordering.

concepts.name: SingleOp
concepts.description: An easy subset of MAWPS that requires only a single operation to solve. It is used to assess performance on simpler arithmetic problems with minimal multi-step reasoning.

concepts.name: one-step problem
concepts.description: A problem that can be solved with a single reasoning step or direct calculation. Such problems often do not require extended intermediate reasoning to derive an equation from the question.

concepts.name: two-step problem
concepts.description: A problem that requires two reasoning steps to reach the answer. It is more complex than a one-step problem but still simpler than tasks requiring longer chains of reasoning.
concepts.match_reason: The candidate is the opposite concept: a one-step problem requires a single reasoning step, not two.

concepts.name: variable computation
concepts.description: The use of different amounts of intermediate processing, often reflected in generating more or fewer tokens before an answer. In this context, it refers to the hypothesis that harder problems may benefit from spending more computation on intermediate outputs.

concepts.name: intermediate tokens
concepts.description: Tokens generated between the input prompt and the final answer that represent intermediate computation or reasoning. They provide a way for a model to allocate additional processing to a problem before committing to an output.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is about the intermediate tokens themselves, while the candidate is the broader idea of variable computation or spending more processing; they are related but not the same concept.

concepts.name: natural language
concepts.description: Human-readable linguistic form used to express meanings and reasoning in words and phrases. In chain-of-thought prompting, natural language is used to render intermediate steps explicitly instead of leaving them implicit.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is the general idea of natural language, while the candidates are specific uses of it (a rationale or an instruction), not the same concept.

concepts.name: PaLM 540B
concepts.description: A 540-billion-parameter language model variant used for evaluating prompting methods. It is a large pretrained language model designed to perform a wide range of language tasks, including reasoning benchmarks.
concepts.match_reason: PaLM 540B is a specific model variant, while candidate [0] is the broader PaLM family rather than that exact 540B version.

concepts.name: annotator
concepts.description: A person who prepares or supplies examples, labels, or prompt demonstrations for an evaluation setup. Different annotators can introduce variation in the style and content of prompt exemplars.

concepts.name: exemplar-based prompting
concepts.description: A prompting approach that relies on example demonstrations to steer a language model toward a desired task behavior. Its effectiveness can vary with the specific exemplars used, their order, and their wording.
concepts.match_reason: Exemplar-based prompting is a prompting method that uses demonstrations/examples, while candidate 3 refers to the demonstrations themselves, not the prompting approach.

concepts.name: unintended behaviors
concepts.description: Outputs from a language model that depart from the intended goal of the system or the user's request. These behaviors can include fabricating facts, producing biased or toxic text, or failing to follow instructions.

concepts.name: user instructions
concepts.description: Directives or requests given by a user to guide a system's behavior or output. In language-model applications, following user instructions means producing responses that satisfy the requested task, format, or constraints.

concepts.name: 有帮助、诚实且无害
concepts.description: 一种用于评价或规范语言模型行为的三项准则。模型应帮助用户完成任务，避免捏造或误导信息，并且不应对人、社会或环境造成物理、心理或社会伤害。
concepts.match_reason: “有帮助、诚实且无害”是评估模型行为的三项准则本身；[0] 讲的是更宽泛的“语言模型对齐”过程，不是同一概念。

concepts.name: GPT-3 architecture
concepts.description: A transformer-based autoregressive language-model architecture used by GPT-3 and related models. It defines the network structure and generation style while allowing different models to share the same underlying architecture but differ in training and alignment.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming item is an architecture, while the candidates are specific model instances/families, not the same concept.

concepts.name: closed-domain question answering
concepts.description: A question-answering setting in which the correct response must be grounded in the provided input or a restricted source of information. The model should avoid introducing extra facts that are not present in the context.

concepts.name: human data
concepts.description: Data annotated or produced by people that reflects human preferences, judgments, or demonstrations. It is used to supervise or align models toward desired behavior.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a broad category of data produced or annotated by humans, while the candidates are specific subtypes (demonstrations or preference comparisons), not the same concept.

concepts.name: RealToxicityPrompts
concepts.description: A benchmark dataset of prompts designed to probe whether language models continue with toxic content. It is used to measure toxicity in generated outputs through automatic and human evaluation.
concepts.match_reason: RealToxicityPrompts is a benchmark dataset for evaluating toxicity, while the candidate is the general toxicity property itself, so they are not the same concept.

concepts.name: Winogender
concepts.description: A benchmark dataset for evaluating gender bias in language understanding and generation. It tests whether systems exhibit stereotypical or biased behavior when resolving pronouns in gendered contexts.

concepts.name: CrowS-Pairs
concepts.description: A benchmark dataset for measuring social bias in language models. It contains paired examples that contrast stereotyped and anti-stereotyped sentences to assess whether a model prefers biased language.

concepts.name: performance regression
concepts.description: A decrease in model performance on one set of tasks or benchmarks after changes intended to improve other behaviors. In language models, it often appears as worse scores on established public NLP datasets after alignment training.

concepts.name: public NLP dataset
concepts.description: A publicly available benchmark dataset used to evaluate natural language processing systems. Such datasets cover tasks like question answering, commonsense reasoning, and translation.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a generic category of publicly available NLP benchmark datasets, while the candidates are specific datasets or a benchmark suite, not the same concept.

concepts.name: DROP
concepts.description: A reading comprehension benchmark that requires discrete reasoning over paragraphs, including arithmetic and counting. It is designed to test whether models can combine textual understanding with symbolic reasoning.

concepts.name: HellaSwag
concepts.description: A commonsense reasoning benchmark based on choosing plausible continuations for short contexts. It evaluates whether a model can predict the most sensible ending among several candidates.

concepts.name: WMT 2015 French to English translation
concepts.description: A machine translation benchmark for translating French text into English. It is used to evaluate translation quality on standardized test data from the WMT 2015 shared task.
concepts.match_reason: Candidate 2 is English-to-French, which is the opposite translation direction from French-to-English.

concepts.name: alignment tax
concepts.description: A reduction in performance on some tasks that occurs as a cost of making a model more aligned with human preferences or safety goals. The term emphasizes that improving behavior on one axis can sometimes degrade benchmark results on another.

concepts.name: PPO-ptx
concepts.description: 一种将PPO更新与增加预训练分布对数似然的更新混合在一起的训练方法。它用于在保持标签器偏好分数不变的同时，减少某些任务上的性能退化。

concepts.name: 预训练分布
concepts.description: 模型在大规模预训练阶段所拟合的数据分布。对数似然朝向这一分布的优化可用于保留通用语言建模能力，并减轻后续对齐训练带来的性能回退。
concepts.match_reason: “预训练分布”特指模型在预训练阶段所学到的数据分布；候选[2]只是泛指训练分布，范围更广，不是同一概念。

concepts.name: 标签器偏好分数
concepts.description: 由人类标签器对模型输出进行偏好比较后得到的评分指标。它反映输出是否更符合人工偏好，常用于评估对齐训练后的模型质量。

concepts.name: 留出标签器
concepts.description: 未参与生成训练数据、但用于评估模型泛化能力的人类标注者群体。若模型对这些标注者的偏好判断表现与训练标注者相近，说明其偏好对齐具有一定泛化性。
concepts.match_reason: “留出标签器”指用于评估泛化能力的人类标注者群体；“标签器偏好分数”是基于标注者偏好比较得到的评估指标，概念不同。

concepts.name: 监督微调基线
concepts.description: 在监督学习阶段对语言模型进行微调后得到的基准模型。它通常作为后续对齐方法的比较对象，用来衡量额外训练是否带来改进。
concepts.match_reason: “监督微调基线”指监督微调后作为后续对齐方法比较对象的基准模型；候选项中只有泛指微调方式，没有这个特定“基线模型”概念。

concepts.name: 代码总结
concepts.description: 对程序代码的功能、结构或行为进行简洁自然语言概括的任务。它要求模型从源代码中提炼核心信息并用人类可读语言表达出来。

concepts.name: 代码问答
concepts.description: 围绕程序代码内容提出问题并生成答案的任务。它要求模型理解代码语义、变量关系和程序行为。
concepts.match_reason: “代码问答”是针对程序代码的专门问答任务；候选[1]只是通用问答，不限定代码语义。

concepts.name: human preferences
concepts.description: Human preferences are judgments expressed by people about which model outputs are better, more helpful, or more appropriate. They can be used as a training signal when explicit gold-standard answers are unavailable or insufficient.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is about preference judgments themselves, while candidate 0 is broader human-produced/annotated data and candidate 1 is the people who evaluate outputs, not the preference signal.

concepts.name: simple mistakes
concepts.description: Simple mistakes are basic failures in model behavior such as ignoring an instruction, hallucinating facts, or mishandling straightforward questions. They indicate that a model may still be unreliable even after alignment-oriented training.

concepts.name: false premises
concepts.description: False premises are assumptions in a prompt that are incorrect or misleading. Detecting them requires the ability to recognize when a question contains an invalid presupposition rather than answering it directly.

concepts.name: safety and reliability
concepts.description: Safety and reliability are desirable properties of a model that reduce harmful behavior and make outputs consistently trustworthy. They are often treated as central goals in alignment work and model deployment.

concepts.name: human feedback
concepts.description: Human feedback is information provided by people about model behavior, such as preferences, rankings, or corrections. It can be used to guide training toward outputs that better match human expectations.
concepts.match_reason: Human feedback is a broader signal category (preferences, rankings, corrections); none of the candidates denotes exactly that same concept.

concepts.name: 强化学习
concepts.description: 一种通过试错来学习决策策略的机器学习方法，学习过程依赖于奖励信号来逐步改进行为。它常用于控制、对齐和序列生成等任务。
concepts.match_reason: “强化学习”是通用的试错式决策学习方法；候选项分别是奖励模型和基于人类反馈的微调，都是相关应用/组件，不是同一概念。

concepts.name: 对话
concepts.description: 一种以多轮交流为核心的自然语言任务或应用场景，系统需要根据上下文生成合适的回应。它常用于评估和训练面向交互的语言模型。
concepts.match_reason: “对话”是多轮交流式任务；候选项要么是问答，要么是泛指自然语言处理任务，都不是同一概念。

concepts.name: 语义解析
concepts.description: 一种将自然语言句子映射为可执行的形式化表示的任务，常见于把用户意图转换为逻辑形式、查询或程序。它需要同时捕捉语言含义与结构化输出约束。
concepts.match_reason: 语义解析是把自然语言映射为形式化表示的任务；候选中没有同义概念，instruction following/自然语言指令只是按指令生成输出，范围更泛且不等同于语义解析。

concepts.name: 故事生成
concepts.description: 一种根据给定条件自动生成叙事文本的生成任务，通常要求内容连贯、情节合理并具有一定的创造性。它常用于研究开放式文本生成能力。
concepts.match_reason: “故事生成”是面向叙事文本的特定生成任务，属于自然语言生成/条件文本生成的一个子类，不与候选项完全同义。

concepts.name: 评论生成
concepts.description: 一种自动生成产品、服务或内容评价文本的生成任务，通常需要表现出特定观点、语气和内容组织方式。它常用于研究可控文本生成和风格建模。
concepts.match_reason: 评论生成是生成评价/评论文本；故事生成是生成叙事文本，属于不同任务。

concepts.name: 证据抽取
concepts.description: 一种从文本中识别并提取与某个结论、答案或主张相关支持性片段的任务。它常用于可解释推理、信息检索和事实核查。
concepts.match_reason: “证据抽取”是识别并提取支持性证据片段的任务；候选项中没有同一任务，3和4只是证据载体，2是抽取式问答模型。

concepts.name: 书面人类反馈
concepts.description: 以书面形式记录的人类评价、建议或批注，可作为训练信号、提示补充或数据增强材料。它能够提供比单纯分数更细粒度的指导信息。

concepts.name: 文本环境
concepts.description: 一种以文本作为状态、动作或观测形式的交互环境，智能体只能通过读写文本与环境交换信息。它常用于研究语言驱动的决策和推理。
concepts.match_reason: “文本环境”指智能体在环境中以文本作为状态/动作/观测进行交互；“文本交互”只是通过文本提示与模型通信的方式，概念不同。

concepts.name: 智能体对齐
concepts.description: 使智能体的行为、目标和输出与人类意图、规范或价值一致的过程或研究方向。它关注在复杂环境中控制行为偏差并提高可靠性。

concepts.name: normative prior
concepts.description: A prior assumption or preference structure that encodes normative judgments about desired behavior. It guides learning toward outputs considered better or more appropriate under those judgments.

concepts.name: language assistants
concepts.description: Language-model-based systems designed to assist users through conversation and task completion. They are often used as a testbed for studying alignment and safety properties in realistic interactive settings.
concepts.match_reason: Language assistants are interactive systems built using language models, not the same concept as the models themselves.

concepts.name: cross-task generalization
concepts.description: The ability of a language model trained on one set of tasks or datasets to perform well on different, unseen tasks. It is often evaluated by fine-tuning on multiple tasks and testing on held-out tasks.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming term is specifically about transferring to unseen tasks, while candidate [1] is the broader generalization concept rather than the same narrowly defined notion.

concepts.name: held-out tasks
concepts.description: Tasks that are excluded from training and used only for evaluation. They measure whether a model generalizes beyond the examples it has seen during training.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is about evaluation tasks excluded from training, while the candidate is a held-out validation data set; related but not the same concept.

concepts.name: instruction following for navigation
concepts.description: A form of learning in which a model is trained to interpret natural-language directions and execute them to move through an environment. It is commonly studied in simulated navigation settings.

concepts.name: simulated environment
concepts.description: A computationally generated world used for training or evaluating agent behavior without acting in the physical world. It allows controlled experiments on tasks such as navigation.

concepts.name: harms of language models
concepts.description: Negative real-world effects that can arise from deploying language models, including biased outputs, harmful content, and other unsafe or misleading behavior. Studying these harms motivates methods for safer model design and alignment.
concepts.match_reason: The candidate is about aligning language models to reduce harmful behavior, while the incoming concept is the harms themselves; they are related but not the same concept.

concepts.name: gaming misspecified objectives
concepts.description: Behavior in which a model exploits flaws in an objective function or training signal to appear successful while failing the underlying intended goal. It is a common failure mode when the specified objective does not fully capture the desired behavior.

concepts.name: private data leakage
concepts.description: Private data leakage is the unintended release of sensitive information that was present in training data or internal model representations. In language models, this can occur when the model memorizes and later reproduces personal or confidential text.

concepts.name: stereotype
concepts.description: A stereotype is an oversimplified and often socially biased generalization about a group of people. In language-model evaluation, stereotypes are used as a benchmark category for measuring whether generated text reproduces harmful associations.

concepts.name: social bias
concepts.description: Social bias is systematic preferential or prejudicial treatment reflected in language or predictions about social groups. In language models, it is commonly evaluated by checking whether generated text encodes unequal associations or disparities.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a specific kind of bias limited to social groups, while candidate [0] is the broader general notion of bias and [1] is biased text, a particular output form.

concepts.name: value-targeted dataset
concepts.description: A value-targeted dataset is a curated training set built to express or reinforce specific human values or behavioral goals. It is used to steer a model toward preferred responses during fine-tuning or similar adaptation methods.

concepts.name: conditional likelihood
concepts.description: Conditional likelihood is the probability a model assigns to text given a context or preceding input. It is used to score how likely a language model is to generate particular phrases or documents under specific conditions.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is the probability score itself, while the candidate is a training objective that maximizes that score; related but not the same concept.

concepts.name: safety-specific control token
concepts.description: A safety-specific control token is an added special token used to condition a language model’s generation toward safer or less harmful outputs. By providing the model with explicit control signals during training or decoding, it can bias generation toward a desired safety behavior.

concepts.name: human-in-the-loop data collection
concepts.description: Human-in-the-loop data collection is a training strategy in which humans actively provide examples, labels, or feedback during the data-gathering process. It is used to improve model behavior on tasks where automatic data alone is insufficient, especially for safety-sensitive generation.
concepts.match_reason: Human-in-the-loop data collection is a data-gathering strategy/process, while human data is the resulting data produced or annotated by people; related but not the same concept.

concepts.name: word embedding regularization
concepts.description: Word embedding regularization is a technique that constrains or adjusts learned word representations to reduce unwanted associations in a model. It is used to mitigate biased or harmful generations by shaping the geometry of the embedding space.
concepts.match_reason: The candidate is the embedding representations themselves, while the incoming concept is a regularization technique applied to them.

concepts.name: null space projection
concepts.description: Null space projection is a representation-editing method that removes or suppresses information associated with sensitive attributes by projecting model representations onto a subspace that excludes those attributes. It is used to make token distributions more uniform or less biased with respect to protected concepts.

concepts.name: causal mediation analysis
concepts.description: Causal mediation analysis is a method for decomposing the effects of an input or intervention into indirect pathways that operate through intermediate variables. In language-model analysis, it can be used to identify how internal representations contribute to biased or harmful outputs.

concepts.name: language model steering
concepts.description: Language model steering is the process of guiding a model’s generated text toward a desired style, content, or safety property at inference time. It commonly uses an auxiliary model, prompts, or control signals to influence the generation without retraining the main model.
concepts.match_reason: Language model steering is an inference-time control technique for guiding generation, while language models are the base generative models themselves; they are different concepts.

concepts.name: OpenAI Playground
concepts.description: OpenAI Playground is an interface for directly interacting with models through the OpenAI API. It is used for submitting prompts and testing model behavior in an interactive setting.

concepts.name: Plain prompt
concepts.description: A prompt type in which a labeler invents an arbitrary task, with an emphasis on ensuring the set of tasks is diverse. It is used to gather varied instruction-like examples for training instruction-following models.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a specific prompt format for generating diverse instruction data, while the candidate is the general notion of a prompt.

concepts.name: User-based prompt
concepts.description: A prompt type derived from stated use cases in API waitlist applications. It is designed to resemble real user needs and to supply training prompts that match practical application scenarios.
concepts.match_reason: User-based prompt is based on real stated use cases, while plain prompt is an arbitrarily invented task for diversity; they are different prompt types.

concepts.name: SFT dataset
concepts.description: A supervised fine-tuning dataset made of labeler demonstrations used to train supervised instruction-following models. It contains training prompts from both API sources and labeler-written prompts.

concepts.name: RM dataset
concepts.description: A reward-model training dataset composed of prompts and labeler rankings of model outputs. It is used to train reward models that score or prefer candidate responses.

concepts.name: PPO dataset
concepts.description: A dataset of prompts without human labels that serves as input for policy optimization in reinforcement learning from human feedback. It is used to fine-tune models with PPO-based RLHF training.

concepts.name: labeler intent
concepts.description: The inferred purpose or goal behind a user prompt as judged by human annotators. It captures what the user most likely wanted the model to do, especially when the prompt is indirect or ambiguous.

concepts.name: biased or toxic language
concepts.description: Language that expresses unfair prejudice, discrimination, or harmful abuse toward people or groups. It is often treated as a form of undesirable output when evaluating model responses.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept combines biased and toxic language, while each candidate covers only one of those ideas or a related subcase, not the same combined concept.

concepts.name: screening test
concepts.description: A screening test is a preselection assessment used to evaluate candidates against specific criteria before they are chosen for a task or role. It is designed to identify individuals who meet the desired performance standards on the relevant dimensions.

concepts.name: alignment criteria
concepts.description: Alignment criteria are the standards used to judge whether an AI system's behavior matches desired goals such as usefulness, safety, or user preference. Different criteria can conflict with one another, requiring a choice about which objectives to prioritize in a given situation.
concepts.match_reason: “Alignment criteria” are standards for evaluating alignment, while “alignment” is the broader goal/problem itself, so they are related but not the same concept.

concepts.name: potentially harmful response
concepts.description: A potentially harmful response is an output that may cause harm, misinformation, or other negative consequences if given to a user. Such responses are often treated specially in safety-oriented evaluation and training because they can conflict with other goals like helpfulness.

concepts.name: helpfulness
concepts.description: Helpfulness is a quality of an assistant response that measures how well it satisfies the user's request and supports the user's goals. In alignment settings, it is one of the core objectives that may need to be balanced against safety-related criteria.

concepts.name: fact checking
concepts.description: Fact checking is the task of assessing whether a claim is supported or refuted by available evidence. It typically requires retrieving relevant information and comparing it against the claim to determine its truthfulness.

concepts.name: generalisation
concepts.description: 模型将从训练中获得的能力迁移到未见任务或未见样本上的能力。它体现了模型在新情境下继续表现良好的程度，而不只是记忆训练数据。
concepts.match_reason: “generalisation” is the broad ability to perform well on unseen data, while candidate [0] is a specific form of it (out-of-distribution generalization), not the same concept.

concepts.name: memorisation
concepts.description: 模型在参数中存储和复现训练数据或相关信息的能力。它使模型能够回忆细节，但不一定意味着能够对新任务做出良好的泛化。
concepts.match_reason: “memorisation”指模型将训练数据或相关信息存入参数并复现的能力；候选项中没有同一概念，且有些是不同机制或架构，不是记忆能力本身。

concepts.name: 稠密检索器
concepts.description: 稠密检索器是一种基于向量表示进行文档检索的检索系统。它通过学习查询和文档的嵌入来衡量语义相似性，从而找出与当前上下文最相关的资料。
concepts.match_reason: “稠密检索器”是具体的向量检索器/密集检索器；候选0是检索范式“密集检索”，候选2是泛指“检索器”，都不完全等同。

concepts.name: Wikipedia index
concepts.description: An index built over Wikipedia documents for retrieval-based systems. It supports searching and retrieving relevant Wikipedia passages as external evidence for a model.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is an index/data structure over Wikipedia for retrieval, while the candidate is the retrieval process itself; related but not the same concept.

concepts.name: product quantisation
concepts.description: A vector compression technique that splits vectors into subspaces and quantizes each part separately. It reduces memory usage and speeds up approximate nearest-neighbor retrieval while aiming to preserve retrieval quality.
concepts.match_reason: Product quantisation is a specific vector-compression method that splits vectors into subspaces, while the candidates are only the general idea of quantization or a quantized vector representation, not the same concept.

concepts.name: compressed index
concepts.description: A retrieval index stored in a compact representation to reduce memory consumption. It trades off some representation detail for lower storage cost and often faster retrieval.

concepts.name: uncompressed index
concepts.description: A retrieval index stored in its full original representation without compression. It typically uses more memory but can preserve the exact stored vectors or embeddings more faithfully.
concepts.match_reason: Uncompressed index and compressed index are opposites: one stores the full original representation, the other uses a compact representation.

concepts.name: updatability
concepts.description: The ability of a model or retrieval system to incorporate new information over time without full retraining from scratch. It is important for keeping knowledge current and correcting outdated facts.

concepts.name: full-dataset finetuning
concepts.description: A training regime in which a pretrained model is adapted using the entire labeled dataset for a task. It aims to maximize task performance by exposing the model to all available supervision.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is about using the entire labeled dataset during adaptation, while candidate [1] is about updating all or most model parameters; these are different notions.

concepts.name: MoCo contrastive loss
concepts.description: 一种对比学习损失函数，用于拉近正样本表示、推远负样本表示。它通常结合动量编码器和队列机制来构造大量负样本，从而提升表示学习效果。
concepts.match_reason: MoCo contrastive loss is the momentum-encoder/queue-based contrastive objective from MoCo, while negative contrastive estimation is a different noise-contrastive training method.

concepts.name: unsupervised data
concepts.description: 不依赖人工标注标签的数据。它常用于表示学习和预训练任务中，以降低对标注资源的需求。
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is unlabeled data, while the only close candidate is an unsupervised learning setting; they are different concepts, and the other candidate is weakly supervised rather than unsupervised.

concepts.name: gradient descent
concepts.description: 一种通过沿损失函数负梯度方向迭代更新参数的优化方法。它广泛用于训练机器学习模型以最小化目标函数。
concepts.match_reason: “Gradient descent” is the general optimization method; none of the candidates is the same concept—[0] is the stochastic variant, [1] is just a gradient-based update step, and the others are different or related notions.

concepts.name: Attention Distillation
concepts.description: A training objective that transfers document-importance signals from a language model to a retriever. It uses attention-derived document scores as soft targets and updates the retriever so that it assigns higher probability to documents the language model relies on.

concepts.name: retriever probability distribution
