concepts.description: A normalized distribution over candidate documents produced from retriever scores. It assigns higher probability to documents with larger query-document similarity scores, often restricted to the top-K retrieved documents.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a normalized distribution derived directly from retriever scores, while "document posterior" is a broader posterior over documents conditioned on input and generation context; they are related but not the same concept.

concepts.name: dot-product similarity
concepts.description: A similarity function that scores a query-document pair by taking the dot product of their vector representations. It is widely used in retrieval models because higher values indicate greater alignment between the query and a document.
aliases: dot-product between the query and documents vectors
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is the specific similarity scoring function based on dot product, while the candidates are either the raw dot-product operation, a generic similarity function, or a retrieval model that uses it.

concepts.name: pre-softmax score
concepts.description: A raw model score computed before applying the softmax normalization. These scores are often used as stable training signals because they preserve relative preference information before probabilities are formed.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is the raw score values before softmax, while the candidate is the linear layer/module that produces those scores, so they are not the same concept.

concepts.name: 值向量范数
concepts.description: 向量长度的度量，用来反映向量本身的大小。在注意力加权中，它可以与注意力权重结合，用于衡量某个输入项对输出的实际贡献。

concepts.name: 相关性分数
concepts.description: 用于衡量输入项或文档与查询相关程度的数值。它通常由模型中的多个信号组合得到，并可用于后续的排序或概率化处理。

concepts.name: StopGradient算子
concepts.description: 一种自动微分中的控制操作，用于阻断梯度从某个张量向前传播。它常用来限制哪些参数会在训练中被更新。
concepts.match_reason: StopGradient是阻断/截断梯度传播的控制算子；候选项0是梯度下降优化方法，候选项1是反向传播算法，概念都不相同。

concepts.name: EMDR2
concepts.description: 一种用于同时训练多文档阅读器和检索器的端到端方法。它把检索到的文档视为潜变量，并利用检索概率与语言模型似然共同构造训练目标。
aliases: End-to-end training of Multi-Document Reader and Retriever
concepts.match_reason: EMDR2 is an end-to-end training method for jointly training a reader and retriever with latent retrieved documents, while the candidates are a generic latent retriever component and a dual-encoder architecture, not the same concept.

concepts.name: 期望最大化算法
concepts.description: 一种在存在潜变量时进行参数估计的迭代优化框架。它通过在隐变量的期望和参数更新之间交替进行，来最大化观测数据的似然。

concepts.name: 潜变量
concepts.description: 在模型中未被直接观测到、但会影响观测结果的变量。它们通常需要通过边缘化、近似推断或迭代优化来处理。
concepts.match_reason: “潜变量”是模型中未直接观测到的变量本身；候选0是使用潜变量的语言模型，候选1是边缘化后的似然，都不是同一概念。

concepts.name: 多文档阅读器
concepts.description: 一种面向多篇检索文档进行阅读和答案生成的模型。它综合来自多个文档的信息，以支持更准确的答案预测。
concepts.match_reason: “多文档阅读器”指的是直接综合多篇检索文档进行阅读和答案生成的阅读器模型；候选项中没有同一概念，DocReader、RAG、文档边缘化和EMDR2分别是更一般模型或训练/推断方法。

concepts.name: EMDR2 loss function
concepts.description: 一种用于训练检索器的损失函数，其目标是使文档概率分布成为与语言模型输出最高概率对应的文档的指示性分布。它在实践中按 token 级别应用，而不是按整个序列级别应用。
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a specific token-level loss function used within EMDR2, while candidate 0 is the broader EMDR2 method and candidate 1 is a different training objective.

concepts.name: prefix language modeling
concepts.description: A language modeling objective that predicts a continuation from an initial prefix of a text. The input is split into a prefix and a target continuation, and the model is trained to generate the continuation from the prefix.
concepts.match_reason: Prefix language modeling is a specific conditional continuation objective, while the listed left-to-right/autoregressive LM is the broader next-token objective; they are related but not the same concept.

concepts.name: special sentinel mask token
concepts.description: A dedicated token inserted into an input sequence to mark the beginning of a masked span. It allows the model to identify and generate each missing span in a structured way.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a span-start sentinel used to mark and generate masked spans, while [MASK] is a placeholder that replaces masked tokens in standard MLM; related but not the same.

concepts.name: retriever vocabulary
concepts.description: The set of tokens supported by a retrieval component for representing and processing text. It constrains how queries or special symbols are encoded for document retrieval.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a token vocabulary used by a retriever, while the candidate is the retriever model itself, not its vocabulary.

concepts.name: document-conditioned language model
concepts.description: A language model that generates text while being conditioned on one or more retrieved documents. The retrieved documents provide external context that can influence the model’s predictions.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a specific retrieval-conditioned language model, while candidate 0 is the general class of language models and the others are different notions (likelihood or training objective).

concepts.name: title-to-section generation
concepts.description: A text generation task in which a model produces the content of a Wikipedia section from the article title together with the section title. The input is the pair of titles, and the output is the section text itself.

concepts.name: T5-XL
concepts.description: T5-XL is an extra-large configuration of the Text-to-Text Transfer Transformer model. It is a large sequence-to-sequence language model with many parameters, often used as a stronger but more expensive downstream model.
concepts.match_reason: T5-XL and T5-11B are different size variants of the T5 family, not the same configuration.

concepts.name: activation checkpointing
concepts.description: 一种节省训练内存的技术，在前向传播时只保留部分中间激活值，其余在反向传播时按需重新计算。它通常用来在计算开销增加的情况下降低显存占用。

concepts.name: Euclidean distance
concepts.description: A geometric distance measure between two vectors, defined as the length of the straight line connecting them in vector space. In retrieval systems, it is used to score how close a document embedding is to a query embedding.
concepts.match_reason: Euclidean distance is the plain L2 metric, while squared L2 distance is its squared variant, so they are not the same concept.

concepts.name: hard negatives mining
concepts.description: A training strategy that selects non-relevant examples that are especially similar to the query or otherwise difficult to distinguish from relevant ones. It is used to improve retrieval models by forcing them to learn finer-grained distinctions.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is the mining strategy for selecting hard negatives, while the candidates are either specific hard-negative types or broader negative-sampling concepts, not the same concept.

concepts.name: phrase-based retrieval
concepts.description: A retrieval approach that matches queries against stored phrase representations rather than only whole documents. It is used to retrieve relevant textual phrases more precisely than document-level matching.
concepts.match_reason: Phrase-based retrieval is about retrieving by stored phrase representations, while exact string matching relies on literal text overlap; they are related but not the same concept.

concepts.name: salient span masking
concepts.description: A pre-training strategy that masks important or informative spans of text rather than random tokens. The model must recover or infer the masked content, which helps it learn to identify useful evidence for retrieval.
concepts.match_reason: Salient span masking is a span-selection pretraining strategy, while the candidates are either a sentinel marker token or masking-schedule variants; none denotes the same concept.

concepts.name: informed retriever
concepts.description: A retriever trained with access to additional information that is not available at test time, such as the target output. It can serve as a stronger teacher for distilling a deployment-time retriever.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a specific kind of retriever trained with extra training-time information, while the candidate is the general notion of a retriever.

concepts.name: training set weighting
concepts.description: A technique that assigns different importance weights to training examples during optimization. Higher-weighted examples influence the model more strongly than lower-weighted ones.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is about weighting training examples during optimization, while the candidate refers to pretrained model parameters; they are different concepts.

concepts.name: pseudo-positive query-document pair
concepts.description: A query-document pair treated as positive supervision even though it was not annotated by humans. Such pairs are typically created heuristically from data signals to support retriever training.

concepts.name: recurring span
concepts.description: A text span that appears multiple times within a document. Repeated spans can be exploited as weak supervision for constructing training pairs or identifying related content.

concepts.name: search engine interaction
concepts.description: Search engine interaction is a language-modeling setup in which a model generates text queries, sends them to a search engine, and uses the retrieved documents as context. This lets the model incorporate external web or corpus evidence during generation or question answering.

concepts.name: few-shot question answering
concepts.description: Few-shot question answering is a question-answering setting in which a model answers questions after seeing only a small number of examples. Retrieved documents or prompt demonstrations can be added to help the model infer the correct answer format and content.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a specific few-shot question-answering task setting, while the candidates are broader or different notions (evaluation, learning, prompt); none denotes the same concept exactly.

concepts.name: 标度律
concepts.description: 描述模型性能与模型规模、数据量和计算量之间关系的经验规律。它用于指导如何在这些资源之间分配训练预算，以获得更好的模型表现。
concepts.match_reason: “标度律”指模型性能与规模/数据/算力之间的经验关系，不是单独的模型规模或数据集规模。

concepts.name: Chinchilla
concepts.description: 一种大规模语言模型，以相对更小的模型规模配合更多训练数据而获得更高的参数效率。它体现了通过重新平衡模型大小与数据量来改进性能的思路。
concepts.match_reason: Chinchilla is a specific language-model scaling approach/model, while the candidate is the general concept of a large-scale language model; they are not the same concept.

concepts.name: few-shot fine-tuning
concepts.description: 一种结合少量示例对模型进行参数更新的学习方式，用于让模型适应新任务。它不同于纯粹的上下文学习，因为它会通过训练修改模型参数。
concepts.match_reason: “few-shot fine-tuning”指用少量示例进行参数更新的微调；候选中没有这一特定概念，0/1更偏少样本学习且可不更新参数，2/3是全量微调，4更泛。

concepts.name: prompt-based learning
concepts.description: 一种借助文本提示将任务表述为语言建模问题的方法。它通过设计或学习提示，将输入映射到模型更容易处理的形式，从而完成下游任务。
concepts.match_reason: prompt-based learning is the broader paradigm of using prompts to cast tasks as language modeling; none of the candidates is exactly that concept, and some are only related subtypes or applications.

concepts.name: Turing Test
concepts.description: A benchmark for machine intelligence proposed as a way to assess whether a machine can exhibit humanlike language behavior. It evaluates a system by comparing its conversational responses with those of a human.

concepts.name: 马尔可夫假设
concepts.description: 一种序列建模假设，认为当前状态或下一个事件只依赖于有限长度的最近历史，而不需要整个过去。它使语言模型能够用局部上下文近似建模词序列的生成概率。
concepts.match_reason: “马尔可夫假设”是关于只依赖有限历史的建模假设；“statistical language model”是使用这种假设的具体语言模型，不是同一概念。

concepts.name: 回退估计
concepts.description: 一种平滑策略，在高阶统计模型数据稀疏时，退回到低阶模型来估计概率。它通过在不同阶数的模型之间分配概率质量，缓解未见事件带来的估计问题。
concepts.match_reason: “回退估计”是高阶统计模型中的平滑/概率估计策略，而“反向传播”是用于训练神经网络的梯度传播优化方法，概念不同。

concepts.name: Good-Turing估计
concepts.description: 一种用于概率估计的平滑方法，通过根据低频事件的出现情况调整概率分配。它常用于处理稀疏数据，使未观测事件也能获得非零概率。
concepts.match_reason: Good-Turing估计是按频次调整概率的平滑方法；回退估计是数据稀疏时退回低阶模型的策略，属于不同概念。

concepts.name: GPT-1
concepts.description: GPT-1 is an early generative pre-trained language model based on transformer decoding. It demonstrated that large-scale pre-training can improve performance on many downstream language tasks.
concepts.match_reason: GPT-1 is a distinct earlier model; the candidates are GPT-2/3, an architecture, or GPT-J, not the same concept.

concepts.name: LLaMA
concepts.description: LLaMA is a family of large language models trained for strong general-purpose language understanding and generation. It emphasizes efficient scaling of language modeling for downstream use.
concepts.match_reason: LLaMA is a specific model family (Meta’s large language models), while the candidates are either different model families or a generic category, so none denote the same concept.

concepts.name: pre-training and fine-tuning
concepts.description: Pre-training and fine-tuning is a two-stage learning strategy in which a model is first trained on general data and then adapted to a specific task. This approach enables reusable representations that transfer across tasks.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is the full two-stage strategy (pre-training plus fine-tuning), while the candidates are only fine-tuning, pretraining data, a model built with that strategy, or a specific BERT example.

concepts.name: scaling language models
concepts.description: Scaling language models is the practice of improving model capability by increasing model size, data, or compute. It is associated with broader task performance and emergent generalization.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is the practice of scaling up language models, while the candidates are about scaling laws, a large model type, or inverse scaling—not the same concept.

concepts.name: static word representations
concepts.description: Static word representations are fixed embeddings assigned to words regardless of context. They encode general semantic similarity but do not vary with sentence usage.

concepts.name: probability estimation
concepts.description: Probability estimation is the process of assigning likelihoods to linguistic events such as words or sequences. In language modeling, it underlies prediction of the next token or phrase.

concepts.name: transferable NLP task solver
concepts.description: A transferable NLP task solver is a language model that can be adapted to different natural language processing tasks with limited modification. It leverages shared representations learned from broad pre-training.

concepts.name: general-purpose task solver
concepts.description: A general-purpose task solver is a model that can handle many different tasks from the same interface or prompting scheme. It is not restricted to a single application domain.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a broad general-purpose solver across many tasks, while the candidate is specifically a transferable NLP task solver; these are related but not the same concept.

concepts.name: specific task helper
concepts.description: A specific task helper is a model or system optimized to assist with one narrowly defined task. It provides utility within a limited task setting rather than broad generality.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a narrowly specialized helper for one task, while the candidate is a broad model for many tasks; they are contrasting concepts, not the same.

concepts.name: 自然语言处理从零开始
concepts.description: 一种早期神经自然语言处理方法，强调用神经网络直接从数据中学习任务相关表示，而不是依赖大量手工特征。它展示了语言模型和分布式表示在多种自然语言处理任务中的有效性。
concepts.match_reason: 它描述的是早期“端到端/从零开始”的神经自然语言处理思想，并非特指神经语言模型或预训练神经语言模型。

concepts.name: 表示学习
concepts.description: 一种从数据中自动学习可用于下游任务的特征表示的方法。对于语言模型而言，它超越了单纯的词序列建模，把模型输出用作通用表示。
concepts.match_reason: “表示学习”是更一般的从数据中自动学习表示的方法；候选项中没有与之完全同义的概念，最接近的也只是语言领域的具体应用或实现方式。

concepts.name: 双向长短期记忆网络
concepts.description: 一种能够同时利用左侧和右侧上下文信息的循环神经网络结构。它常用于生成上下文相关的词表示，并可通过预训练和微调适配具体任务。
concepts.match_reason: “双向长短期记忆网络”特指 BiLSTM，而候选中只有普通 LSTM 和更泛的双向表示/双向自注意力，并非同一概念。

concepts.name: 预训练策略
concepts.description: 用于在大规模无标注语料上训练语言模型的一组方法和目标设计。不同的预训练策略会影响模型学到的表示质量以及迁移到下游任务时的效果。
concepts.match_reason: “预训练策略”指预训练阶段的方法和目标设计；候选项中只有“预训练”或具体预训练语言模型/分布，概念不完全相同。

concepts.name: context-aware representation
concepts.description: 一种能够利用上下文信息来表示词、句子或文本的表示方式。它会根据周围语境动态变化，从而更好地服务于下游任务。
concepts.match_reason: “Context-aware representation”是更宽泛的概念，而“双向表示”只是利用左右上下文的一种具体表示方式，不是同一概念。

concepts.name: human feature engineering
concepts.description: 由人手工设计输入特征以帮助模型完成任务的过程。它依赖领域知识，但往往耗费大量时间，并限制模型对数据中潜在模式的自动发现。

concepts.name: scaling effect
concepts.description: 随着模型规模、数据规模或计算资源增加而带来的性能提升现象。它常被用来解释更大模型在能力和泛化上的增强。
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is the observed improvement phenomenon from scaling up, while the candidates describe a practice, a study of trends, or specific scaling laws rather than the effect itself.

concepts.name: model capacity
concepts.description: 模型能够表示和学习复杂模式的能力。更高的模型容量通常意味着模型可以拟合更复杂的函数并支持更广泛的任务。
concepts.match_reason: “Model capacity” is the expressive ability of a model, while the candidates are about size, memorization, generalization, or scaling effects rather than that concept itself.

concepts.name: GPT-4 API
concepts.description: 用于通过程序化接口访问 GPT-4 的应用接口。它使外部系统能够调用模型能力，而不必直接修改模型本身。
concepts.match_reason: GPT-4 API is the interface to access GPT-4, while GPT-4 is the model itself; they are related but not the same concept.

concepts.name: distributed parallel training
concepts.description: 将模型训练计算分散到多个计算设备并行执行的方法。它用于支持大规模模型训练，并缓解单机算力和内存限制。
concepts.match_reason: “distributed parallel training” is a broad umbrella concept for training across multiple devices, while the candidates are specific subtypes or related techniques (data parallelism, model parallelism, sharding, or generic parallelization).

concepts.name: large-scale data processing
concepts.description: 对海量数据进行清洗、组织、分发和训练前处理的过程。它是训练大模型时必须解决的重要工程环节。

concepts.name: AI chatbot
concepts.description: A conversational software system that uses artificial intelligence to interact with users in natural language. It can answer questions, assist with tasks, and mediate access to information through dialogue.
concepts.match_reason: AI chatbot is a general concept, while ChatGPT is a specific example of one.

concepts.name: New Bing
concepts.description: A version of Bing that incorporates large language model capabilities to enhance search results and conversational search. It represents an attempt to combine traditional web search with generative AI assistance.

concepts.name: computer vision
concepts.description: A field of artificial intelligence concerned with enabling machines to interpret and analyze visual information from images and video. It includes tasks such as recognition, detection, segmentation, and visual reasoning.

concepts.name: vision-language model
concepts.description: A multimodal model designed to process and relate visual and textual information together. Such models are used for tasks that require understanding images and language jointly, including multimodal dialogue.

concepts.name: multimodal dialogue
concepts.description: An interactive exchange in which a system and user communicate using more than one modality, such as text and images. It requires the model to interpret and respond across modalities in a coherent conversational setting.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a multimodal conversational interaction, while the candidates are a general dialog system and a vision-language model; neither denotes the same concept.

concepts.name: multimodal input
concepts.description: Input to a model that combines more than one kind of data, such as text, images, audio, or video. It allows systems to reason over multiple modalities rather than language alone.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is the input modality mix itself, while the candidates are a dialogue setting and a model type, not the same concept.

concepts.name: Microsoft 365 Copilot
concepts.description: An AI assistant integrated into Microsoft 365 products to help automate office tasks. It uses large language models to generate drafts, summarize information, and support productivity workflows.

concepts.name: plugin
concepts.description: An add-on component that extends the functionality of a software system by providing specialized capabilities. In conversational AI systems, plugins can connect the model to external services or tools.

concepts.name: adaptation
concepts.description: Adaptation is the process of modifying a pre-trained language model so that it performs better for particular uses or tasks. It often involves additional training or prompting methods that specialize the model without retraining it from scratch.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is the general idea of model adaptation, while the candidates are specific variants or mechanisms (especially parameter-efficient methods), not the same concept.

concepts.name: GPT series models
concepts.description: GPT series models are a family of generative pre-trained Transformer language models that have evolved through successive versions. They are commonly used as representative examples in the development of large language models.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept refers to the whole GPT model family across versions, while the candidates are specific models, an architecture, or a different GPT-style family.

concepts.name: reducible loss
concepts.description: The portion of prediction loss that can, in principle, be lowered by improving the model. It is often interpreted as an estimate of the divergence between the true data distribution and the model distribution.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is the reducible part of loss, which is distinct from general loss and opposite to irreducible loss.

concepts.name: training tokens
concepts.description: The number of token units used to train a language model. It is a standard measure of data scale in language-model training and is often paired with parameter count when analyzing compute allocation.

concepts.name: FP-days
concepts.description: A unit of compute equal to one day of floating-point processing at a specified hardware rate. It is used to quantify training compute budgets in large-scale machine learning experiments.

concepts.name: data mixture schedule
concepts.description: 训练过程中不同数据来源或数据子集的混合比例与安排方式。它决定了模型在训练中接触各类数据的顺序和占比，进而影响训练效果。

concepts.name: diminishing returns
concepts.description: 一种缩放现象，指当模型继续增大时，性能提升会逐渐变小。它说明在接近某些极限时，额外扩展带来的收益会下降。
concepts.match_reason: “Diminishing returns”强调随着规模继续增大，边际收益变小；“scaling effect”只是泛指规模增大带来性能提升，更宽泛，不是同一概念。

concepts.name: task-level scaling law
concepts.description: A task-level scaling law describes how model scale relates to performance on a specific downstream task. Unlike loss-based scaling laws, it focuses on task metrics such as accuracy or success rate and may depend strongly on task difficulty and evaluation criteria.
concepts.match_reason: Task-level scaling law is a specific kind of scaling law for downstream task metrics, while performance scaling is broader and includes many kinds of performance/resource relationships.

concepts.name: LaMDA-PT
concepts.description: LaMDA-PT is a language model variant that can be instruction-tuned to improve performance on unseen tasks. It is used as an example of how larger model sizes can be necessary for instruction following to emerge strongly.
concepts.match_reason: LaMDA-PT is a specific LaMDA variant mentioned for instruction tuning behavior, while the candidates are either the general LaMDA family or a different 137B variant; none denotes the same concept exactly.

concepts.name: controlled experiment
concepts.description: A controlled experiment is a study design that varies selected factors while keeping other conditions fixed in order to isolate their effects. It is used to measure how specific changes influence an outcome.

concepts.name: JSON-formatted key-value pair
concepts.description: A key-value pair represented in JSON structure, where each key is associated with a corresponding value. In retrieval tasks, collections of these pairs provide structured input from which a model must select the correct value.

concepts.name: training-time sequence length
concepts.description: The maximum or typical input length used when a model is trained. Performance can change when the model is evaluated on sequences longer than those seen during training.

concepts.name: retriever-reader model
concepts.description: A retriever-reader model is a question answering system that first retrieves candidate documents or passages and then reads the retrieved content to produce an answer. This two-stage design is used to handle questions whose answers are not contained in a single fixed input and to focus computation on a smaller set of relevant texts.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is the generic two-stage retriever-then-reader QA architecture, while the candidates are either only one component or a specific instance of that architecture.

concepts.name: long input context
concepts.description: A long input context is an input sequence that contains a large amount of text for a model to process at once. It can provide more information relevant to a task, but it also increases the amount of content the model must reason over.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is the long input sequence itself, not the model’s capacity (context length) or a model that handles long context.

concepts.name: retriever recall
concepts.description: Retriever recall is the fraction of questions for which the retrieval stage returns at least one document containing the answer. It measures how well the retriever surfaces answer-bearing evidence before the reader model attempts to answer.
concepts.match_reason: Retriever recall is an evaluation metric for retrieval quality, while the candidates are a model, vocabulary, corpus, or probability distribution—not the same concept.

concepts.name: multi-document question answering task
concepts.description: A question answering setting in which a model receives one question and multiple documents as input. Exactly one document contains the answer, and the model must identify and use that document to answer correctly.
concepts.match_reason: The candidate describes a broader multi-document QA setting requiring evidence integration across documents, while the incoming concept is the specific case where exactly one document contains the answer.

concepts.name: Wikipedia paragraph
concepts.description: A paragraph-sized passage extracted from Wikipedia and used as a document unit in question answering systems. Such passages can serve as answer-bearing evidence or as retrieved distractors depending on whether they contain the annotated answer.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is specifically a Wikipedia-derived paragraph, while candidate 0 is the general notion of a text passage used for retrieval.

concepts.name: Wikipedia chunk
concepts.description: A text segment from Wikipedia, often limited to a fixed token length, used as a document within an input context. Chunking allows retrieval systems and models to work with smaller, manageable units of source text.

concepts.name: MS MARCO
concepts.description: A large-scale machine reading and retrieval benchmark used to fine-tune dense retrieval systems. Models trained on it are often better at ranking passages that are relevant to a query.
concepts.match_reason: MS MARCO is the overall benchmark/collection, while the candidates are a dev split, training labels, or a specific NLG task built on it—not the same concept.

concepts.name: document order
concepts.description: The sequence in which documents are arranged within an input context. Changing document order can move the answer-containing document to different positions and thereby alter model performance.

concepts.name: NaturalQuestions annotations
concepts.description: NaturalQuestions annotations are human-provided answer annotations for the NaturalQuestions dataset. They supply acceptable reference answers that can be used to judge whether a predicted output is correct.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is the answer annotations within NaturalQuestions, while the candidates are the dataset/benchmark itself, which is broader and not the same thing.

concepts.name: unambiguous questions
concepts.description: Unambiguous questions are questions for which the available passages do not plausibly support multiple different answers. They are used as a cleaner subset for analysis when ambiguity in the full set may affect results.

concepts.name: random documents
concepts.description: Random documents are unrelated documents selected without regard to the query or answer. They can serve as distractors in retrieval experiments to test robustness to irrelevant context.
concepts.match_reason: Random documents are a specific kind of distractor/negative document; the candidates are broader categories, not the exact same concept.

concepts.name: Wilhelm Conrad Röntgen
concepts.description: Wilhelm Conrad Röntgen was a German physicist who received the first Nobel Prize in Physics in 1901. He is best known for the discovery of X-rays, which had a major impact on medical imaging and physics.

concepts.name: MPT-30B-Instruct
concepts.description: A large instruction-tuned language model with a maximum context length of 8192 tokens. It was pre-trained on a large token corpus and later adapted with longer sequences to better handle extended context.
concepts.match_reason: MPT-30B-Instruct and LaMDA-PT are different language models; the candidate is not the same model or a direct synonym.

concepts.name: sequence length adaptation pre-training
concepts.description: A pre-training phase in which a language model continues training on longer input sequences than those used in earlier training. It is used to adapt the model to handle longer contexts more effectively.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a length-adaptation pretraining phase for longer contexts; none of the candidates denotes that same idea, and [1] is only a fixed full-length training regime.

concepts.name: ALiBi
concepts.description: A positional encoding method for language models that represents positional information through attention biases. It is designed to help models generalize to longer sequence lengths without requiring fixed learned positional embeddings.
concepts.match_reason: ALiBi is a positional bias method for attention, not a learned positional embedding.

concepts.name: LongChat-13B
concepts.description: A language model variant that extends the LLaMA-13B context window to support much longer inputs. It is fine-tuned after modifying the positional representation so the model can process sequences up to 16K tokens.
concepts.match_reason: LongChat-13B is a specific LLaMA-13B-based model variant, while the candidate is a generic long-context language model concept.

concepts.name: LLaMA-13B
concepts.description: A large language model that serves as the base model for later extensions to longer context windows. It is a 13-billion-parameter model whose standard context length can be modified through positional encoding changes and further fine-tuning.
concepts.match_reason: LLaMA-13B is a specific base model, while the candidates are either the broader LLaMA family or later long-context variants derived from it.

concepts.name: condensed rotary positional embeddings
concepts.description: A modified form of rotary positional embeddings used to compress or adapt positional information for longer sequences. This technique helps extend a model’s usable context window beyond its original limit.
concepts.match_reason: Condensed rotary positional embeddings are a rotary-based context-extension method, while learned positional embedding is a different trainable absolute-position encoding.

concepts.name: relevant information
concepts.description: The portion of input content that contains evidence needed to answer a question. Its placement within the input can affect how easily a model can use it.
concepts.match_reason: The candidate is the full input context, while the incoming concept is only the relevant evidence within that input; they are related but not the same concept.

concepts.name: string-serialized JSON object
concepts.description: A JSON object rendered as a linear text string rather than a structured data type. In retrieval benchmarks, it can encode key-value pairs in an input context that a model must read and search.

concepts.name: UUID
concepts.description: A universally unique identifier is a standardized identifier format designed to be highly unlikely to collide with any other generated identifier. In retrieval tasks, random UUIDs can be used as opaque keys and values so that language semantics do not help the model solve the task.

concepts.name: distractor key-value pair
concepts.description: An irrelevant key-value pair included in a retrieval context to make the target association harder to locate. Distractors increase the need for precise matching rather than shallow pattern recognition.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a distractor key-value pair, while the candidate is a distractor document/passage; they are related but not the same kind of distractor.

concepts.name: Little Retrieval Test
concepts.description: A retrieval benchmark for evaluating how well language models can locate and return information from an input context. It is used as a point of comparison for simpler key-value retrieval tasks.
concepts.match_reason: Little Retrieval Test is a benchmark, while candidate 4 is a simpler key-value retrieval task used for comparison; they are related but not the same concept.

concepts.name: fine-grained line retrieval task
concepts.description: A retrieval benchmark that asks a model to find a specific line or span of information within a larger context. It measures precise search and extraction ability over long inputs.
concepts.match_reason: The candidate is a general retrieval benchmark, while the incoming concept specifically targets fine-grained line/span retrieval in long contexts; they are not clearly the same benchmark.

concepts.name: position of relevant information
concepts.description: The location within an input context where the information needed to answer a query appears. Model performance can depend on whether the relevant content is near the beginning, middle, or end of the context.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is about where relevant information appears in the context, while candidate 0 is the information itself and candidate 1 is a prompting strategy.

concepts.name: information retrieval system
concepts.description: An information retrieval system is a system that helps users find relevant information from a collection of documents or other sources. It typically supports searching, ranking, and presenting results for user queries.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a complete retrieval system, while the candidates are the task itself, an index component, or a relevance measure—not the same thing.

concepts.name: data stage
concepts.description: The data stage is the phase of a machine learning pipeline in which training data are collected, prepared, and organized for model development. Problems introduced at this stage can shape what patterns a model learns and may contribute to later errors.

concepts.name: training stage
concepts.description: The training stage is the phase in which a model learns parameters from data by optimizing against a training objective. Choices made during training can affect model behavior, including the emergence of errors or hallucinations.
concepts.match_reason: Training stage is the overall phase of model learning, not the objective being optimized or a single parameter update step.

concepts.name: inference stage
concepts.description: The inference stage is the phase in which a trained model generates outputs for new inputs. Errors at this stage arise during decoding or generation and can lead to hallucinated content even when training has completed successfully.

concepts.name: detection methods
concepts.description: Detection methods are techniques used to identify whether a model output contains a specific undesirable property, such as hallucination. They are designed to assess outputs and determine the extent or presence of the targeted issue.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a general class of methods for detecting undesirable properties, while the candidate is specifically hallucination detection, a particular instance of that class.

concepts.name: Hallucination from Data
concepts.description: Hallucination from data is hallucination that originates in problems with the data used to build a language model. It reflects how issues in the underlying corpus can be learned and reproduced during generation.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a specific cause/type of hallucination caused by problems in training data, while the candidates are general hallucination, its detection, rate, or benchmarks—not the same concept.

concepts.name: Misinformation and Biases
concepts.description: Misinformation and biases are defects in training data that can distort a model’s learned associations and factual outputs. They can cause a model to reproduce false or skewed content as if it were true.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept combines training-data misinformation and bias as defects; the candidates describe only bias/biased text, which is narrower and not the same concept.

concepts.name: Inferior Alignment Data
concepts.description: Inferior alignment data is low-quality data used to align model behavior with desired outputs. If this data is flawed, it can degrade the model’s tendency to respond accurately and consistently.

concepts.name: Hallucination from Training
concepts.description: Hallucination from training is hallucination that arises from how a model is trained, including pre-training and alignment stages. Training choices can shape the model’s factual reliability and its propensity to invent unsupported content.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a cause/category of hallucination arising from training choices, while candidate 2 is the training stage itself, not that hallucination type.

concepts.name: Hallucination from Pre-training
concepts.description: Hallucination from pre-training is hallucination caused by the initial large-scale training phase on raw text. Errors, noise, and statistical patterns in pre-training data can be internalized by the model and later surfaced in generation.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is specifically about pre-training, while candidate [0] is broader training overall and candidate [1] is about data issues rather than the pre-training phase itself.

concepts.name: Hallucination from SFT
concepts.description: Hallucination from supervised fine-tuning is hallucination introduced or amplified during supervised adaptation on instruction or task data. If fine-tuning examples are narrow, noisy, or inconsistent, the model may learn to produce unsupported answers.
concepts.match_reason: Hallucination from SFT is a specific kind of training-induced hallucination during supervised fine-tuning, while candidate 0 is broader training hallucination and candidate 2 is pre-training only.

concepts.name: Hallucination from RLHF
concepts.description: Hallucination from RLHF is hallucination associated with reinforcement learning from human feedback. Reward shaping and preference optimization can sometimes encourage plausible-sounding responses that are not fully grounded.
concepts.match_reason: Hallucination from RLHF is a specific training/alignment-stage source, while candidate 0 is broader and candidate 1 is a different fine-tuning source.

concepts.name: Hallucination from Inference
concepts.description: Hallucination from inference is hallucination that emerges at generation time rather than from the training data itself. Decoding choices, confidence calibration, and reasoning behavior can all influence whether a model invents unsupported content.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a type of hallucination caused during inference, while candidate 1 is the inference stage itself, not the hallucination concept.

concepts.name: Imperfect Decoding Strategies
concepts.description: Imperfect decoding strategies are generation procedures that can increase the chance of hallucination. Sampling or search methods that poorly balance diversity and correctness may lead a model away from the most factual continuation.

concepts.name: Over-confidence
concepts.description: Over-confidence is a condition in which a model assigns excessive confidence to its outputs relative to their actual correctness. This can make incorrect answers appear authoritative and increase hallucinated responses.

concepts.name: Softmax Bottleneck
concepts.description: The softmax bottleneck is a representational limitation in neural language models arising from the softmax output layer. It can restrict the distribution the model can express and thereby contribute to inaccurate or unfaithful generation.

concepts.name: Reasoning Failure
concepts.description: Reasoning failure is an inability to carry out the logical or multi-step inference needed to answer correctly. When reasoning breaks down, the model may fill gaps with unsupported assertions that look coherent but are false.

concepts.name: Factuality Hallucination Detection
concepts.description: Factuality hallucination detection is the detection of hallucinations that involve incorrect factual claims. It evaluates whether generated statements match verifiable real-world facts.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a specific detection task for factual hallucinations, while the candidates are either the hallucination type itself or general hallucination detection; none denote exactly the same concept.

concepts.name: Faithfulness Hallucination Detection
concepts.description: Faithfulness hallucination detection is the detection of hallucinations in which generated content departs from the provided source or context. It assesses whether an output stays faithful to the information it is supposed to reflect.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is the detection task for faithfulness-related hallucinations, while candidate 0 is the hallucination type itself and candidate 3 is the broader general detection task.

concepts.name: 模型编辑
concepts.description: 一种直接修改已训练模型参数或内部知识表示的方法，用于纠正特定事实错误或更新模型记忆。它通常针对局部知识进行定向修正，而不需要重新进行完整训练。
concepts.match_reason: “模型编辑”是对已训练模型进行定向知识修正；候选项“few-shot fine-tuning”是用少量样本做参数微调以适应新任务，二者不是同一概念。

concepts.name: 预训练相关幻觉缓解
concepts.description: 面向预训练阶段引入或积累的幻觉问题所设计的缓解方法。此类方法通常通过调整预训练数据、目标或训练过程来减少模型在基础知识形成阶段学到的错误关联。

concepts.name: 错配幻觉缓解
concepts.description: 用于缓解由模型目标、偏好或对齐过程不一致所引发的幻觉的方法。此类方法关注模型在对人类指令、偏好或安全要求进行对齐时产生的事实性偏差。
concepts.match_reason: 一个针对对齐/偏好不一致导致的幻觉，另一个针对预训练阶段引入的幻觉；问题来源不同，不是同一概念。

concepts.name: 自监督训练
concepts.description: 一种不依赖人工标注、直接从原始数据构造学习信号的训练方式。模型通过预测被遮蔽或后续内容来学习语言模式、知识与推理能力。
concepts.match_reason: “自监督训练”是更一般的训练范式；候选项多为语言模型预训练或自训练方法，属于相关但不完全相同的概念。

concepts.name: self-supervised training
concepts.description: A training paradigm in which supervision signals are derived automatically from the data itself rather than from manually labeled examples. It is commonly used to learn representations from large unlabeled corpora by predicting parts of the input from other parts.
concepts.match_reason: Self-supervised training is a specific form of learning that creates targets from the data itself; the candidates are broader or different regimes, not the same concept.

concepts.name: textual corpora
concepts.description: Large collections of written text used as training data for language models. They provide the linguistic and factual content from which models can learn statistical patterns and higher-level regularities.
concepts.match_reason: "Textual corpora" is a broad category of text collections, while the candidates are specific subtypes or uses (web, books, knowledge, reference, monolingual) rather than the same concept.

concepts.name: lossless compression
concepts.description: A compression method that reduces the size of information without losing any of the original content, allowing exact reconstruction. In information-theoretic interpretations of language modeling, predicting text can be viewed as compressing information about the sequence.

concepts.name: 指令不一致
concepts.description: 指令不一致是指语言模型没有按照用户明确给出的任务要求执行，而是转而生成了其他类型的回答。它反映的是模型输出与指令目标之间的不匹配。
concepts.match_reason: “指令不一致”表示模型没有按指令执行；candidate 是“instruction following”，含义相反，不是同一概念。

concepts.name: 上下文不一致
concepts.description: 上下文不一致是指语言模型的输出与提供给它的上下文材料相矛盾或不相符。模型在摘要、改写或回答时会偏离原始上下文中的关键信息。
concepts.match_reason: “上下文不一致”指输出与给定上下文材料矛盾；候选[1]“指令不一致”是未按用户指令执行，概念不同。

concepts.name: Entity-error hallucination
concepts.description: 一种事实性幻觉，表现为模型生成了错误的实体名称或实体身份。它通常涉及把人物、地点、组织或其他具体对象错误地替换为不正确的实体。
concepts.match_reason: “Entity-error hallucination” is a specific subtype of factuality hallucination focused on wrong entities, while the candidates are broader categories, not the same concept.

concepts.name: query-focused summarization
concepts.description: A summarization task that produces an answer tailored to a specific question or information need rather than a general overview. It aims to synthesize source material in a way that directly addresses the query.

concepts.name: entity knowledge graph
concepts.description: A graph representation in which entities from source documents are linked by relationships. It provides structured connectivity among concepts so that related information can be grouped and analyzed together.

concepts.name: partial response
concepts.description: An intermediate answer generated from a subset of evidence before final synthesis. Multiple partial responses can be combined to produce a more complete overall response.

concepts.name: global sensemaking questions
concepts.description: 针对广泛议题、主题或语料库层面模式而设计的问题，通常没有唯一的事实性标准答案。它们用于测试系统能否从大范围信息中提炼综合性见解。
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is about questions designed to elicit global sensemaking, while the candidate is the broader sensemaking process itself; they are related but not the same concept.

concepts.name: prompt template
concepts.description: 一种预先设计的文本结构，用于将用户查询和检索到的内容组织成适合输入大语言模型的格式。它帮助模型在统一上下文中整合证据并生成回答。
concepts.match_reason: “prompt template” is a general input formatting structure, while “QA prompt” is a specific question-answering prompt template, so they are related but not the same concept.

concepts.name: 自我记忆
concepts.description: 一种将大块数据源的摘要保存为可复用记忆的机制，用来在后续查询时提供上下文和答案线索。它通常通过先生成摘要、再在需要时检索或汇总这些摘要来发挥作用。

concepts.name: 全局摘要
concepts.description: 由多个局部摘要逐步迭代聚合而成的更高层级摘要，用于概括大规模数据源的整体信息。它能够把分散在不同片段中的内容压缩为统一的全局表示，便于检索和问答。
concepts.match_reason: “全局摘要”是由多个局部摘要迭代聚合得到的整体摘要；候选0是特定社区/群组的摘要，范围更窄，不是同一概念，候选1是记忆机制也不相同。

concepts.name: 主题划分
concepts.description: 一种把数据按语义主题分成若干部分的组织方式。它通常基于内容相似性、连接结构或聚类结果，以便更清晰地表示和检索不同主题区域。

concepts.name: 知识图谱作为索引
concepts.description: 一种将知识图谱直接用作检索索引的做法，用图中的实体、关系和结构来定位相关信息。它既可支持内容检索，也可为生成模型提供结构化上下文。

concepts.name: modularity
concepts.description: A structural property of graphs in which nodes can be divided into groups with dense internal connections and sparser connections between groups. It is used to identify community structure and to support hierarchical partitioning of graphs into related subgraphs.

concepts.name: Louvain method
concepts.description: A community detection algorithm for graphs that optimizes modularity to find partitions of nodes into densely connected communities. It is widely used for hierarchical clustering of large networks.

concepts.name: HotPotQA
concepts.description: An open-domain question answering benchmark that emphasizes multi-hop reasoning over multiple supporting facts. It is commonly used to evaluate systems on explicit fact retrieval and compositional question answering.
concepts.match_reason: HotPotQA and StrategyQA are different QA benchmarks: HotPotQA focuses on multi-hop retrieval over supporting facts, while StrategyQA focuses on implicit commonsense reasoning.

concepts.name: MultiHop-RAG
concepts.description: A benchmark for evaluating retrieval-augmented generation systems on questions that require multi-step retrieval and reasoning. It is designed to test how well a system can gather and use evidence across multiple sources.
concepts.match_reason: MultiHop-RAG is a specific benchmark for evaluating RAG systems, while HotPotQA is a different multi-hop QA benchmark; they are related but not the same concept.

concepts.name: MT-Bench
concepts.description: A benchmark for evaluating large language model responses across multiple tasks and prompts. It is used as a standardized test set for comparing model performance on generative outputs.

concepts.name: persona generation
concepts.description: A method that uses a language model to produce diverse, plausible user personas. The personas are then used to represent different kinds of users and to guide downstream task or benchmark creation.

concepts.name: real-world usage
concepts.description: The way a system is actually used by end users in practical settings. In benchmarking, it provides a basis for creating test cases that better reflect authentic interactions and needs.

concepts.name: RAG evaluation criteria
concepts.description: Standards used to judge how well a retrieval-augmented generation system answers questions. These criteria define what counts as a good response and can be applied by human or model-based evaluators.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is about evaluation standards/criteria, while the candidates are RAG models or a benchmark, not the same concept.

concepts.name: 源文档
concepts.description: 作为信息抽取和索引基础的原始文档集合。系统先从这些文档中获取内容，再将其分解并转换为可用于图索引的结构化表示。

concepts.name: 文本块
concepts.description: 从较长文档中切分出来的较小文本片段，用于逐块处理和信息抽取。这样可以让模型在有限上下文内对局部内容进行分析，并为后续结构化表示提供输入。
concepts.match_reason: “文本块”是从文档中切分出的局部片段；“文档检索”是从语料库中选取候选文档的过程，二者不是同一概念。

concepts.name: 实体
concepts.description: 文本中可以被识别并作为图节点表示的具体对象、概念或事物。实体是图索引中的基本节点类型之一，通常与关系和其他属性共同构成结构化知识。

concepts.name: 协变量
concepts.description: 在图索引中除节点和边之外，还可被检测、提取并摘要的附加信息。它们用于补充图结构中的语义内容，例如与事实性陈述或其他可比较信息相关的属性。

concepts.name: 领域定制摘要
concepts.description: 针对特定数据领域设计的摘要方式，利用相应领域的提示和表达习惯来概括信息。它旨在提高摘要对领域内容的覆盖度、准确性和可用性。

concepts.name: 索引阶段
concepts.description: 构建图索引并对其内容进行抽取、分区和摘要的处理阶段。该阶段发生在查询之前，主要目标是把源文本转化为可检索、可汇总的结构化表示。
concepts.match_reason: “索引阶段”是构建/处理图索引的流程阶段，不是某种索引结构本身；候选项中只有索引类型（层次化索引、graph index）或相关对象（源文档、检索式方法），不等同。

concepts.name: 查询阶段
concepts.description: 在用户提出问题后，对图索引及其摘要进行检索、汇总和答案生成的处理阶段。该阶段侧重于围绕具体查询组织信息并输出结果。

concepts.name: 管道阶段
concepts.description: 信息处理流程中的一个离散步骤或阶段，用于表示从输入到输出的不同处理环节。它有助于把整条工作流拆解为可管理、可实现的步骤。

concepts.name: 可验证事实陈述
concepts.description: 能够通过外部证据或模型抽取结果加以核实的事实性表述。它们常被整理为“claims”，用于统计分析、评估或事实一致性验证。
concepts.match_reason: “可验证事实陈述”指的是可被证据核实的陈述/claims；候选[0]是事实验证任务，[1]是事实性这一性质，均不是同一概念。

concepts.name: Recall-precision Trade-off
concepts.description: The recall-precision trade-off describes the balance between retrieving as much relevant information as possible and keeping the extracted information accurate and complete. In text processing, changing parameters such as chunk length can improve one side while hurting the other.

concepts.name: NeoChip

concepts.name: low-power processors
concepts.description: Low-power processors are semiconductor processors designed to minimize energy consumption while performing computing tasks. They are especially useful in battery-powered and power-constrained devices.

concepts.name: wearables
concepts.description: Wearables are electronic devices designed to be worn on the body, such as smartwatches or fitness trackers. They often require compact, energy-efficient components.

concepts.name: IoT devices
concepts.description: IoT devices are internet-connected physical devices that collect, exchange, or act on data. They commonly use low-power hardware because many operate with limited power and small form factors.

concepts.name: named entities
concepts.description: Named entities are explicitly identifiable real-world people, organizations, places, and similar referential expressions. They are often extracted as structured items in information extraction systems.

concepts.name: claims
concepts.description: Claims are factual statements about entities, events, dates, or interactions that can be extracted from text. They provide concise propositions that capture important information beyond simple entity mentions.

concepts.name: claim extraction
concepts.description: Claim extraction is the process of identifying factual statements about entities and events from text. It captures informative propositions that may be implicit or distributed across a passage.

concepts.name: abstractive summarization
concepts.description: Abstractive summarization is a summarization method that generates new, concise statements capturing the meaning of source text. Unlike extractive methods, it may rephrase or synthesize information rather than copying spans verbatim.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is general abstractive summarization, while the candidates are a model, abstractive question answering, and query-focused summarization—related but not the same concept.

concepts.name: public debut
concepts.description: A public debut is the first appearance of a company as a publicly traded entity on a market or exchange. It marks the transition from private ownership to public trading.

concepts.name: technology IPOs
concepts.description: Technology IPOs are initial public offerings by companies in the technology sector. They are often analyzed to understand market behavior for newly listed tech firms.

concepts.name: entity matching
concepts.description: The task of reconciling different extracted names that refer to the same real-world entity. It is used to merge duplicates so that mentions from multiple sources can be combined into a single graph node.

concepts.name: hierarchical community detection
concepts.description: A community detection strategy that repeatedly partitions detected communities into smaller sub-communities. It produces a multi-level decomposition of a graph, often stopping when no further meaningful splits are possible.

concepts.name: leaf community

concepts.match_reason: A leaf community is a terminal, unsubdivided community, while the candidates describe either the hierarchical detection method or the overall nested community structure, not the specific terminal level concept.

concepts.name: duplicate entities
concepts.description: Multiple extracted instances that refer to the same underlying entity. They arise when the same entity is detected repeatedly across documents and are typically merged or clustered during summarization.

concepts.name: edge weight
concepts.description: A numerical value attached to a graph edge that summarizes the strength, frequency, or importance of the relationship it represents. In extracted knowledge graphs, repeated relationships are often aggregated into heavier edges.

concepts.name: 层次化分区
concepts.description: 一种将图中的节点按多个层级组织成分区的方法，使每一层都把节点划分为彼此互斥且共同穷尽的社区。它常用于把全局结构分解为可逐层汇总的子结构，从而支持分治式的整体总结。

concepts.name: 社区摘要模板

concepts.name: 叶子级社区
concepts.description: 社区层次结构中最底层的社区，通常对应最细粒度的分区单元。其元素摘要会被优先加入上下文窗口，以生成该社区的详细摘要。

concepts.name: 更高层级社区

concepts.name: global answer
concepts.description: The final answer returned to a user after intermediate answers are aggregated and used to fill a new context window. It is generated by combining the most helpful intermediate responses within the available token limit.

concepts.name: head-to-head comparison approach
concepts.description: A comparative evaluation method that judges two outputs against each other rather than against a gold-standard answer. It is used when reference answers are unavailable and relative quality is more informative than absolute scoring.

concepts.name: tech policy
concepts.description: The set of rules, proposals, and public decisions that shape how technology is developed, deployed, and governed. It often covers issues such as market competition, privacy, online speech, and platform accountability.

concepts.name: government regulation
concepts.description: Official rules and enforcement measures created by public authorities to control conduct in an industry or society. In technology, regulation can set standards for safety, privacy, competition, and responsibility.

concepts.name: privacy laws
concepts.description: Legal rules that limit how personal information can be collected, stored, shared, and used. They are designed to protect individual privacy and influence how companies design data-driven products and services.

concepts.name: technology development

concepts.name: innovation

concepts.name: ethical considerations
concepts.description: Questions about what is morally acceptable or responsible in decision-making and practice. They guide judgments about harms, fairness, accountability, and the social consequences of actions or technologies.

concepts.name: tech companies
concepts.description: Businesses that develop, sell, or operate technology products and services. They can include software firms, platform companies, hardware makers, and internet services.

concepts.name: government
concepts.description: The public institutions and authorities that make laws, set policy, and enforce regulation. Governments interact with industries through oversight, collaboration, and rule-setting.

concepts.name: preventive medicine
concepts.description: A branch of medicine focused on preventing disease and reducing health risks before illness develops. It uses screening, vaccination, lifestyle intervention, and early detection to improve health outcomes.

concepts.name: wellness

concepts.name: public health priorities
concepts.description: The health issues and population-level concerns that receive the greatest attention from health systems, policymakers, and communities. They reflect which risks, diseases, and interventions are considered most urgent or important.

concepts.name: health literacy
concepts.description: The ability to find, understand, and use health information to make appropriate decisions about care and behavior. It supports informed choices, prevention, and navigation of health systems.

concepts.name: health education curriculum
concepts.description: An organized set of lessons and learning goals aimed at teaching health-related knowledge and skills. It can cover disease prevention, wellness, nutrition, mental health, and other topics relevant to students.

concepts.name: Directness
concepts.description: A control criterion that measures how specifically and clearly an answer addresses a question. It captures the concision of an answer in a general way and serves as a reference point for judging the soundness of other evaluation criteria.

concepts.name: Podcast transcripts
concepts.description: Written transcripts of spoken podcast episodes. They provide text representations of audio conversations that can be chunked and analyzed as a corpus.

concepts.name: high-level community summary

concepts.name: map-reduce summarization

concepts.name: few-shot example

concepts.name: generic prompt

concepts.name: graph reasoning benchmark

concepts.name: 引用图

concepts.name: 文本子图检索

concepts.name: high dimensionality of textual features
concepts.description: The property of textual representations in nodes and edges having a very large feature space. This makes similarity search and retrieval over graph elements more difficult and computationally expensive.

concepts.name: graph encoders
concepts.description: Neural models that transform graph-structured input into vector representations. They can encode topology and node or edge attributes so that graph information can be used by a language model.

concepts.name: subgraph search

concepts.name: AutoPrompt
concepts.description: A prompt-based method that automatically searches for effective prompt tokens to improve a language model’s behavior on a task. It replaces manual prompt design with algorithmic prompt discovery.
concepts.match_reason: AutoPrompt is a specific automatic prompt-token search method, while the candidates are only general prompt optimization or other prompt types, not the same named concept.

concepts.name: Prompt embeddings
concepts.description: Prompt embeddings are learned vector representations used in place of discrete prompt text. They allow prompt content to be optimized directly in embedding space so the prompt can adapt to a task or domain.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is learned prompt embeddings used instead of discrete text, while the candidates are broader prompt concepts or a specific tuning method rather than the embedding representation itself.

concepts.name: Graph prompt tuning
concepts.description: Graph prompt tuning is a prompt-based adaptation approach designed for graph-oriented tasks. It uses learned prompts to help large language models incorporate structural or topological information from graphs.
concepts.match_reason: Graph prompt tuning is a graph-specific prompt-based adaptation method, while the candidates are general prompt optimization or AutoPrompt; neither denotes the same concept.

concepts.name: Triple
concepts.description: A triple is a relational fact represented as a three-part structure, typically consisting of a subject, a relation, and an object. In graph and knowledge representation settings, triples are used to encode more complex relational information than isolated nodes or edges.

concepts.name: Textual Information
concepts.description: Natural-language content associated with graph elements such as nodes or edges. It provides semantic detail that can be combined with topology to improve retrieval and representation of graph-structured data.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is the text content attached to graph elements, while the candidates are graph representations or subgraphs that contain text, not the text content itself.

concepts.name: retrieval-then-pruning approach
concepts.description: A two-stage strategy that first retrieves a limited set of candidate structures and then prunes them to reduce the effective search space. It improves efficiency by avoiding exhaustive exploration of all possible subgraphs.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a two-stage retrieve-then-prune strategy, while candidate 0 is only a pruning mechanism and candidate 1 is only retrieval; neither denotes the same full concept.

concepts.name: induced subgraph
concepts.description: A subgraph formed by taking a set of nodes and including all edges among them that exist in the original graph. Induced subgraphs are a standard way to represent the local structure around selected nodes.

concepts.name: key node
concepts.description: A node identified as important for forming the backbone of a retrieved subgraph. Key nodes guide which local neighborhoods are combined during retrieval.

concepts.name: K-hop neighbor
concepts.description: A node that lies within K edge steps of a given node in a graph. K-hop neighbors define the local neighborhood used to build partial subgraphs around important nodes.

concepts.name: NP-hard problem
concepts.description: A computational problem for which no polynomial-time algorithm is known and for which solving it efficiently would imply efficient solutions to all problems in NP. NP-hardness is used to characterize problems that are intractable in the worst case.

concepts.name: 文本子图索引
concepts.description: 文本子图索引 is a preprocessing and retrieval method that assigns identifiers to subgraphs and stores vector embeddings for them. It enables fast lookup of promising candidate subgraphs by comparing the query against precomputed subgraph representations.
concepts.match_reason: “文本子图索引”强调对子图做索引与向量存储以便快速查找；候选0是子图检索过程，候选1是通用图索引，概念都不完全相同。

concepts.name: 文本子图排序
concepts.description: Textual subgraph ranking is a retrieval step that orders candidate subgraphs by their semantic similarity to a query. The highest-ranked subgraphs are retained for further refinement or task-specific selection.
concepts.match_reason: “文本子图排序”强调对候选子图进行按相似度排序的步骤；候选项中只有检索/索引，没有单独的排序概念。

concepts.name: Top-N selection
concepts.description: Top-N selection is a ranking operation that keeps the N highest-scoring items from a candidate set. It is used to narrow retrieval results to the most relevant candidates before further processing.

concepts.name: 可学习剪枝器
concepts.description: A learnable pruner is a trainable component that removes irrelevant parts of a candidate structure while preserving the parts most useful for a query or task. It refines retrieved neighborhoods into smaller, more focused subgraphs.

concepts.name: Sentence-BERT
concepts.description: Sentence-BERT is a neural sentence embedding model that produces fixed-size vector representations for sentences or short text spans. It is commonly used to encode natural-language queries and text attributes so their semantic similarity can be compared efficiently.
concepts.match_reason: Sentence-BERT is a specific sentence embedding model, while candidate 2 is the generic concept of a sentence encoder, so they are related but not the same.

concepts.name: Textual Subgraph Soft Pruning
concepts.description: 一种针对检索到的文本子图的软剪枝机制，用于降低无关节点和边对后续生成的影响。它通过学习每个节点或边相对于查询的相关性缩放系数，对不相关内容进行自适应掩蔽，而不是直接硬删除。
concepts.match_reason: The candidate is a general graph soft pruning method, while the incoming concept is a specific variant for textual subgraphs; they are related but not the same concept.

concepts.name: scaling factor
concepts.description: 一种用于按比例放大或缩小输入贡献的系数。它可以根据相关性或距离自适应地调节节点、边或特征对最终结果的影响。

concepts.name: element-wise distance
concepts.description: 一种在对应维度上逐元素比较两个表示差异的距离计算方式。它常用于生成局部差异特征，以便后续模型根据差异大小进行加权或筛选。

concepts.name: tree structure

concepts.name: graph traversal

concepts.name: tree traversal
concepts.description: 一种按预定义顺序访问树中节点的过程，如先序、后序或中序遍历。它常用于把层次结构转换为序列、计算节点关系或生成树的线性表示。

concepts.name: Breadth-First Search
concepts.description: A graph traversal algorithm that explores vertices level by level starting from a source node. It is often used to construct a spanning tree or shortest-path layering in an unweighted graph.

concepts.name: pre-order traversal
concepts.description: A tree traversal method that visits each node before recursively visiting its children. It is useful for producing a linear order that preserves hierarchical structure.

concepts.name: tree rooted at the ego node

concepts.name: edge set
concepts.description: A collection of edges considered as a separate part of a graph representation. In graph decomposition, it can hold the edges not included in a selected spanning tree or hierarchical backbone.

concepts.name: lossless conversion
concepts.description: A transformation between two representations that preserves all information needed to reconstruct the original object exactly. In graph-text settings, it means the graph and its description can be converted back and forth without information loss.
concepts.match_reason: Lossless conversion is a general information-preserving transformation between representations, while lossless compression is a specific size-reducing technique; related but not the same concept.

concepts.name: relevance scaling factor
concepts.description: A learned weight that modulates the contribution of a node or edge during graph message passing. It is used to emphasize relevant graph elements and suppress irrelevant ones.

concepts.name: message passing
concepts.description: A computation scheme in graph representation learning where nodes update their embeddings by aggregating information from neighboring nodes and edges. Repeated message passing allows information to flow across the graph structure.

concepts.name: GraphQA benchmark
concepts.description: 一个用于图问答任务的基准数据集集合，包含与图结构相关的问答样本，用来评测模型是否能够理解图的上下文并给出正确答案。它通常覆盖多个图推理或图问答子任务，以便比较不同方法的性能。

concepts.name: E5

concepts.name: Llama2-7b

concepts.name: 软剪枝

concepts.name: redundant information in graphs
concepts.description: Information in a graph that is repetitive, unnecessary, or not useful for the current reasoning task. Such redundancy can distract a model and degrade performance unless filtered or retrieved selectively.

concepts.name: small graphs
concepts.description: Graph inputs with relatively few nodes or edges. They are often easier for models to process and can benefit strongly from task-specific adaptation.

concepts.name: large graphs
concepts.description: Graph inputs with many nodes, edges, or connected facts. They tend to be more complex and can make reasoning harder because of increased information load.

concepts.name: transferability
concepts.description: The ability of a learned model or representation to be applied successfully to a different dataset or task than the one used for training. High transferability means useful knowledge generalizes across domains or benchmarks.

concepts.name: graph encoding capabilities
concepts.description: A model’s ability to represent graph structure and graph-associated text in a form that supports reasoning or generation. Strong encoding capabilities help preserve relationships, connectivity, and relevant content from the graph.

concepts.name: graph-related tasks

concepts.name: related entity
concepts.description: An entity that is connected to another entity through a meaningful relationship in a knowledge structure. Related entities are often retrieved together to preserve context and connectivity.

concepts.name: 图增强检索增强生成系统
concepts.description: 一种将图结构引入检索增强生成流程的系统，用于更好地表示实体之间的相互依赖关系并生成更连贯、上下文更丰富的回答。它通常结合结构化关系信息与文本检索，以提升信息整合能力和响应质量。

concepts.name: adaptive retrieval system
concepts.description: A retrieval system designed to adjust efficiently as new information arrives or conditions change. It aims to preserve effectiveness while minimizing the cost of updating its index or retrieval behavior.

concepts.name: response efficiency
concepts.description: The speed and computational economy with which a system produces responses after retrieval. In retrieval-augmented generation, it reflects how quickly useful answers can be generated from retrieved evidence.

concepts.name: LLM profiling

concepts.name: data indexer
concepts.description: A component that builds a structured data representation from an external database or document collection. It prepares information for efficient retrieval by extracting, organizing, and storing relevant signals.

concepts.name: external database
concepts.description: A source collection of documents or records used as the basis for indexing and retrieval. It provides the raw content from which structured representations and relevant answers are derived.

concepts.name: LLM Profiling for Key-Value Pair Generation
concepts.description: A language-model-based function that generates a key-value pair for each entity or relation in a graph. The key is used for efficient retrieval, while the value stores a text summary of relevant supporting information for downstream generation tasks.

concepts.name: multi-hop subgraph
concepts.description: A subgraph reached by traversing multiple edges from a starting node, capturing indirect relationships among entities or text units. Such subgraphs are useful for exposing broader context and connections that are not visible from a single hop of the graph.

concepts.name: incremental knowledge base
concepts.description: A knowledge base that is updated continuously as new information arrives instead of being rebuilt from scratch. It preserves existing structure while incorporating new data so that the system can stay current with lower computational cost.

concepts.name: chunk traversal
concepts.description: A retrieval strategy that examines document chunks sequentially or by following chunk-by-chunk links. It can be inefficient when many chunks must be explored to find relevant information.

concepts.name: 具体查询
concepts.description: 一种面向细节的查询类型，通常直接指向图中的特定实体、节点或边。它的目标是检索与某个明确对象相关的精确信息。

concepts.name: 抽象查询

concepts.name: higher-order relatedness

concepts.name: one-hop neighboring node

concepts.name: profiling function
concepts.description: A function that converts retrieved graph elements into concatenated textual values such as names, descriptions, and excerpts. Its output is used as input context for answer generation.

concepts.name: entities and relationships
concepts.description: The basic semantic components extracted from text and represented in a graph for retrieval and reasoning. Entities denote real-world or abstract items, while relationships describe how those items are connected.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is the semantic components themselves (entities and relationships), while the candidates are an extraction process or a specific knowledge graph representation, not the same concept.

concepts.name: community-based traversal
concepts.description: A graph navigation strategy that explores connected communities or subgraphs to gather relevant information. It is used to retrieve context by moving through related nodes rather than performing direct similarity search alone.

concepts.name: UltraDomain benchmark
concepts.description: A benchmark corpus built from textbooks and organized into multiple subject domains for evaluation. It is used to assess system performance across diverse domain-specific datasets.

concepts.name: Agriculture dataset
