concepts.name: 索引型GraphRAG
concepts.description: 一种以图结构索引外部知识的GraphRAG类别，重点在于通过图索引组织和定位相关信息。它通常通过结构化索引来提高检索效率和信息覆盖度。
concepts.match_reason: “索引型GraphRAG”指基于图索引的GraphRAG类别；候选中没有同一概念，#2是更泛的GraphRAG，#3只是图索引本身。

concepts.name: Non-graph RAG
concepts.description: A retrieval-augmented generation workflow that organizes a corpus into text chunks, ranks those chunks by similarity to a query, and retrieves the most relevant passages for generation. It relies on unstructured text retrieval rather than an explicit graph representation of knowledge.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is specifically chunk/similarity-based non-graph retrieval, while the candidates are either graph-based variants or the broader generic RAG umbrella, not the same concept.

concepts.name: Topic node
concepts.description: A graph node that represents a high-level topic or theme rather than a single low-level fact. Topic nodes are used to summarize and organize content for coarse-grained retrieval and navigation.

concepts.name: Topic linking
concepts.description: The process of connecting topic nodes to one another within a topic graph or index structure. These links help organize related themes and support traversal across topics.
concepts.match_reason: Topic linking is the act of connecting topic nodes; a topic node is the node itself, so they are different concepts.

concepts.name: Fact linking
concepts.description: The process of associating topic-level abstractions with the underlying factual text or evidence that supports them. It enables a system to move from a summary topic to detailed source information.

concepts.name: Subgraph pruning
concepts.description: The removal of less useful nodes or edges from a retrieved subgraph to make it more focused and manageable. Pruning helps reduce noise and improve the efficiency of downstream reasoning.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a general notion of pruning a retrieved subgraph, while the candidates are specific soft-pruning methods or broader/different processes, not the same concept.

concepts.name: Knowledge organization
concepts.description: The structuring of information into representations that make it easier to store, retrieve, and reason over. In retrieval systems, it often involves organizing text or facts into graphs, chunks, or indexed topics.
concepts.match_reason: Knowledge organization is a broader concept about structuring information, while knowledge preparation is a specific retrieval-system preprocessing step.

concepts.name: granularity optimization
concepts.description: The process of choosing the size and scope of retrieval units to balance contextual richness against efficiency. Coarser units preserve more context but can be redundant, while finer units may lose semantic completeness.

concepts.name: indexing optimization
concepts.description: Methods that improve how content is organized for retrieval so that search is faster and more accurate. It includes structural enhancements such as metadata attachment and hierarchical organization.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is about optimizing indexing methods, while candidate 0 is the indexing process itself and candidate 1 is the resulting search index data structure.

concepts.name: metadata-addition techniques
concepts.description: Indexing methods that attach descriptive fields such as titles, timestamps, categories, or keywords to text chunks. The added metadata supports filtering and reranking during retrieval.
concepts.match_reason: The candidate is broader indexing optimization that includes metadata attachment, while the incoming concept is specifically about metadata-addition techniques.

concepts.name: 最佳匹配25
concepts.description: 一种广泛使用的概率检索排序算法，用于根据查询词与文档词项的匹配程度对候选文档进行排序。它在信息检索中常作为强基线方法。
concepts.match_reason: “最佳匹配25”指 BM25 排序算法；候选项分别是检索任务、top-k 截断和词汇重叠，均不是该算法本身。

concepts.name: RETROprompt
concepts.description: 一种在RETRO基础上扩展的检索增强方法，结合少样本知识存储来适配更复杂的提示场景。它通过更灵活的知识组织与调用方式增强提示效果。

concepts.name: 少样本知识存储
concepts.description: 一种面向少样本提示而构建的知识存储机制，用于保存可直接用于上下文示例的相关知识。它帮助系统在样本较少时仍能提供更合适的检索支持。
concepts.match_reason: 它是面向少样本提示的知识存储机制，和候选中的少样本学习/显式记忆都不是同一概念，只是相关。

concepts.name: EAR
concepts.description: 一种通过对多个扩展查询的候选结果进行重排序来提升检索准确性的框架。它从多个候选中选择最优结果，以改善最终检索质量。
concepts.match_reason: EAR 是一个基于多个扩展查询候选进行重排序的特定框架，而 [0] 只是通用的重排序概念，不是同一概念。

concepts.name: 句子变换器
concepts.description: 一种将句子编码为向量表示的神经表示模型，常用于语义相似度计算和近邻检索。它能够把文本映射到适合距离度量的嵌入空间中。
concepts.match_reason: “句子变换器”特指用于生成句子嵌入的模型；候选项中没有同一概念，0 只是段落编码器，2 过于宽泛。

concepts.name: 近似邻居
concepts.description: 在向量空间中与查询最相近但并非精确最近的候选对象。它们通常用于在大规模检索中在效率和准确性之间取得平衡。
concepts.match_reason: “近似邻居”指的是近似最近邻结果/对象本身，而候选中只有[2]是“近似最近邻搜索”这一方法，不是同一概念。

concepts.name: Knowledge Graph Construction from Corpus
concepts.description: A process that builds a knowledge graph by extracting entities and relations from a text corpus. The resulting graph is used to represent facts and connections in structured form.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a construction process from a corpus, while both candidates denote the resulting graph structure, not the process itself.

concepts.name: GraphRAG with Existing Knowledge Graphs
concepts.description: A GraphRAG setting that augments generation with knowledge graphs that already exist before the system is applied. It retrieves and uses those external graphs as structured evidence for answering queries.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is specifically GraphRAG that uses pre-existing external knowledge graphs, while the candidates describe GraphRAG built from source documents or more general/hybrid graph-RAG variants rather than existing KG retrieval.

concepts.name: Retrieval Technique
concepts.description: A family of methods for selecting relevant information from a knowledge source. Different techniques vary in whether they rely on similarity, logic, graph neural networks, language models, or reinforcement learning.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a family of retrieval methods, while the candidates are a task, component, system, or benchmark rather than that general technique family.

concepts.name: Similarity-based Retriever
concepts.description: A retriever that selects evidence by measuring similarity between the query and candidate items. It typically ranks graph nodes, passages, or other units by embedding or lexical closeness.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a specific retriever type that ranks items by similarity, while candidate 0 is the broader retriever concept and candidate 3 is only the scoring function it uses.

concepts.name: Logical-based Retriever
concepts.description: A retriever that uses logical rules, symbolic reasoning, or structured inference to identify relevant knowledge. It is designed to follow explicit constraints or reasoning chains rather than only surface similarity.
concepts.match_reason: Logical-based retrieval uses symbolic rules and inference, while similarity-based retrieval ranks by embedding/lexical closeness; they are different retrieval paradigms.

concepts.name: GNN-based Retriever
concepts.description: A retriever that uses a graph neural network to encode graph structure and produce relevance scores. By propagating information across nodes and edges, it can retrieve context informed by graph topology.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a retriever specifically based on a graph neural network, while the candidates are either a generic retriever, a GNN architecture, or different graph-retrieval variants without that exact definition.

concepts.name: LLM-based Retriever
concepts.description: A retriever that relies on a large language model to decide what information is relevant and should be retrieved. It can use the model's semantic understanding and reasoning to guide selection.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is an LLM-driven retriever, while none of the candidates denote that specific retrieval method; candidate 0 is broader supervision, and the others are different retrieval baselines.

concepts.name: RL-based Retriever
concepts.description: A retriever that is trained with reinforcement learning to improve retrieval decisions through reward signals. It learns a policy for choosing relevant knowledge items over time.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a retriever trained with reinforcement learning, which is more specific than the general RL paradigm and different from a generic retriever.

concepts.name: Multi-round Retrieval
concepts.description: Multi-round retrieval is a retrieval approach that performs several successive retrieval steps rather than a single search. Each round can refine the query or use earlier results to improve relevance and completeness.
concepts.match_reason: Multi-round retrieval means repeated successive searches, while dual-level retrieval means searching at two granularity levels; related but not the same concept.

concepts.name: Node-level Knowledge
concepts.description: Node-level knowledge is information represented at the level of individual graph nodes. It encodes facts or entities as discrete units that can be learned from or generated by a model.
concepts.match_reason: Node-level knowledge is a granularity of knowledge representation at individual nodes, while a knowledge graph is the graph structure itself; they are related but not the same concept.

concepts.name: Path-level Knowledge
concepts.description: Path-level knowledge captures relationships as sequences of connected nodes in a graph. It represents multi-hop structure and can support reasoning over chains of linked facts.
concepts.match_reason: Path-level knowledge refers to multi-hop relationships along connected nodes, while node-level knowledge is about individual nodes; they are different levels of representation.

concepts.name: Subgraph-level Knowledge
concepts.description: Subgraph-level knowledge represents a local graph neighborhood containing multiple nodes and edges. It preserves richer structural context than single nodes or individual paths and is useful for structured reasoning.
concepts.match_reason: Subgraph-level knowledge is a granularity of knowledge representation, while multi-hop subgraph is a particular kind of subgraph; they are related but not the same concept.

concepts.name: Graph-enhanced Chain-of-Thought
concepts.description: Graph-enhanced chain-of-thought is a reasoning approach that combines step-by-step verbal reasoning with graph structure or graph-derived evidence. It uses graph information to guide intermediate reasoning steps and improve factual consistency.
concepts.match_reason: Graph-enhanced chain-of-thought is a specific variant of chain-of-thought using graph evidence, not the general CoT concept, and it is also distinct from a graph-enhanced RAG system.

concepts.name: Collaborative Knowledge Graph Refinement
concepts.description: Collaborative knowledge graph refinement is a process in which a system improves a knowledge graph by iteratively updating, correcting, or enriching its structure. It aims to make the graph more accurate and more useful for downstream reasoning or retrieval.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is about iteratively improving an existing knowledge graph, while the candidates are about building a graph from scratch or the graph itself.

concepts.name: LeanContext
concepts.description: LeanContext is a method that selectively keeps only the most relevant sentences for a query. It reduces context size and computational cost by removing less useful retrieved content.

concepts.name: 句子选择
concepts.description: 一种从候选文本中挑选最相关句子的过程，目的是保留对当前输入最有用的信息并减少无关内容。它常用于压缩上下文、提高效率，并降低后续计算开销。
concepts.match_reason: “句子选择”是对句子级候选进行筛选的具体过程；候选[2]“软剪枝”只是更泛化的过滤/裁剪方法，不是同一概念。

concepts.name: 自我反思机制
concepts.description: 一种让模型对自身输出或外部检索内容进行评估与反省的机制，用于发现错误、遗漏或不一致之处。它通常用于在生成过程中触发修正，从而提升结果质量。

concepts.name: SKR
concepts.description: 一种动态检索框架，使大语言模型在面对已知或可由内部知识回答的查询时优先使用预训练知识，仅在必要时才调用检索。它旨在减少不必要的检索，并提升响应效率。
concepts.match_reason: SKR is a selective retrieval framework that prefers internal knowledge and only retrieves when needed; none of the candidates denote this same concept, and the closest ones are general retrieval components or retrieval-augmented models rather than this specific adaptive no/low-retrieval strategy.

concepts.name: 上下文过滤模型
concepts.description: 一种用于识别并移除上下文中无关或低价值内容的模型。它通常在检索增强系统中作为筛选器，以保留更有用的证据并抑制噪声。
concepts.match_reason: “上下文过滤模型”是用于筛选上下文的模型，而“软剪枝”是更泛的选择性过滤方法，不一定是同一概念。

concepts.name: 检索内容重要性评估方法
concepts.description: 一种专门衡量检索到的内容对最终生成或回答有多大贡献的评估方法。它帮助系统识别哪些证据应被保留、削弱或删除。
concepts.match_reason: 这是衡量检索内容对最终答案贡献度的评估方法，不是检索过程、检索指标召回率或RAG方法本身。

concepts.name: 剪枝
concepts.description: 一种从候选集合中移除不重要部分的操作，以保留更有价值的信息并降低复杂度。它常用于压缩检索结果或模型输入。
concepts.match_reason: “剪枝”是通用的移除不重要部分以降复杂度的操作；候选0“软剪枝”只是其具体变体，1-3分别是相关但不同的文本压缩/切分概念。

concepts.name: 重加权
concepts.description: 一种调整不同信息片段相对权重的方法，使更重要的部分在后续处理或生成中发挥更大作用。它常用于优化检索结果的影响力分布。
concepts.match_reason: “重加权”是调整信息片段的重要性分布；“权重合并”是把参数增量合并进模型权重，二者不是同一概念。

concepts.name: Selfmem
concepts.description: 一种在检索语料有限时构建记忆池的框架，利用大语言模型生成的结果作为可选择的信息来源。它通过迭代选择来提升生成质量。
concepts.match_reason: Selfmem is a specific memory-pool construction framework using LLM-generated candidates and iterative selection, not the general retrieval-augmented language model concept.

concepts.name: 记忆池
concepts.description: 一种用于存放可供后续检索、选择或复用的信息集合。它通常保存模型生成结果或外部知识片段，以支持后续生成过程。
concepts.match_reason: “记忆池”指用于后续检索/选择/复用的信息集合；候选项中没有同义概念，Selfmem 只是利用记忆池的一个具体框架，不是同一概念。

concepts.name: 迭代选择框架
concepts.description: 一种反复评估并筛选候选项的机制，通过多轮选择逐步保留更优的信息。它常用于从较大的候选集合中构建更高质量的子集。

concepts.name: SAIL
concepts.description: 一种基于检索结果构造指令微调数据集的方法，用于训练大语言模型将回答建立在可靠内容之上。它同时尽量排除会干扰学习的无关元素。
concepts.match_reason: SAIL is a data-construction method for instruction tuning from retrieval results, while the candidates are a memory-pool framework and a selective retrieval framework; none denotes the same concept.

concepts.name: 指令微调数据集
concepts.description: 一种用于指令微调的训练数据集合，通常包含输入指令、相关上下文和期望输出。它用于让模型更好地遵循任务要求并生成符合指令的回答。
concepts.match_reason: “指令微调数据集”特指用于 instruction tuning 的数据集；候选项里只有更泛化的监督训练数据集或任务特定数据集，不是同一概念。

concepts.name: 语义相似片段
concepts.description: 与查询在向量空间中具有较高相似度的文本块或信息片段。它们通常是检索系统返回的候选证据，但未必包含完成复杂推理所需的全部信息。
concepts.match_reason: “语义相似片段”指的是被检索出来的文本块/证据片段本身，不是检索方法（最近邻搜索、密集检索）也不是抽取任务（证据抽取）。

concepts.name: 多跳问题
concepts.description: 一种需要通过多个中间事实或推理步骤才能回答的问题。它通常无法仅凭单个局部片段直接解决，而需要跨多个证据源进行组合推理。
concepts.match_reason: “多跳问题”指需要多个中间事实/推理步骤才能回答的问题；候选项中没有完全同义的概念，0 和 1 都是相关任务或具体应用场景，不是同一概念。

concepts.name: 块粒度
concepts.description: 文本被切分成块时每个块的大小或细化程度。块粒度越小，单个块包含的信息越局部，可能更难支撑复杂推理所需的完整上下文。
concepts.match_reason: “块粒度”指切分后每个块的大小/细化程度；“文本块”是切分出来的块本身，不是粒度概念。

concepts.name: 领域知识
concepts.description: 特定专业领域内的专门知识体系，通常包含术语、规则、事实和推理模式。它在问答和生成任务中常需要结合多步推理才能被有效理解和使用。

concepts.name: 多跳推理
concepts.description: 一种需要连续使用多个中间结论来得出最终答案的推理方式。它常用于连接分散在不同文本片段中的证据，从而解决复杂问题。
concepts.match_reason: “多跳推理”是通用的多步推理方式；候选项中只有[2]是特定于图上的多跳推理，其他要么是问题、步骤或证据聚合，概念不完全相同。

concepts.name: multi-hop information
concepts.description: Information that must be connected across multiple pieces of evidence or multiple reasoning steps to answer a query. It is common in complex questions that cannot be resolved from a single local text segment.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is about multi-hop information/evidence itself, while the candidates are about reasoning, a graph-reasoning task, or a subgraph—not the same denotation.

concepts.name: distributed domain knowledge
concepts.description: Domain-specific knowledge that is scattered across many documents, sources, or sections rather than concentrated in one place. Effective use of this knowledge requires connecting dispersed fragments and preserving their contextual relationships.
concepts.match_reason: The incoming concept is a specific kind of domain knowledge that is distributed across multiple sources; the candidate is the broader notion of domain-specific knowledge, not specifically distributed.

concepts.name: structural database
concepts.description: A database organized around explicit structural relationships rather than only unstructured text. It is used to store and retrieve knowledge in a form that supports relational querying and structured access.
